title: 20 Kwacha
description: Zambia 20 Kwacha banknote from the 2024 Heritage Series featuring mauve and blue coloring with agricultural and wildlife motifs.
body:
The 20 Kwacha note was released as part of the Bank of Zambia's Heritage Series launched in 2024. Featuring mauve and blue coloring, this denomination represents a middle-value note in the six-banknote family. The obverse displays a flower as a registration device, a tractor tilling a field representing Zambia's agricultural sector, a fish eagle in flight symbolizing the nation's fauna, a map of Zambia rendered in bronze-to-green OVI, and the Zambian coat of arms.

The reverse showcases a water buffalo, representing Zambia's rich wildlife heritage, and a candelabra tree. The series design philosophy employs single background colors for each note, with the central portion of both sides printed in a contrasting color reflecting the main color of the next higher denomination, creating a unified aesthetic progression through the series.

Agriculture plays a fundamental role in Zambia's economy, employing over half the population and contributing significantly to GDP. The tractor imagery on the 20 Kwacha note acknowledges the importance of mechanized farming in boosting agricultural productivity and rural development. The water buffalo depicted on the reverse is native to Zambia's game management areas and national parks, representing the country's commitment to wildlife conservation alongside economic development.

highlights.design: Obverse: Bank of Zambia logo, flower registration device, tractor in field, fish eagle, bronze-to-green OVI map, coat of arms. Reverse: Water buffalo, candelabra tree. Colors: mauve and blue.
highlights.reason: Heritage Series denomination emphasizing Zambia's agricultural sector and wildlife, modernizing currency with advanced security features.
highlights.security: Advanced security thread with color-shifting properties; watermark integration; tactile elements for visually impaired users; hybrid banknote technology.

title: 50 Kwacha
description: Zambia 50 Kwacha banknote from the 2024 Heritage Series featuring gold-green coloring with baobab tree, birds, and Lumangwe Falls.
body:
The 50 Kwacha banknote was released as part of the Bank of Zambia's Heritage Series in 2024. Featuring gold and green coloring, this note emphasizes Zambia's natural environment and wildlife biodiversity. The obverse includes English text and Bank of Zambia branding, a flower registration device, a dump truck representing industrial development, a fish eagle in flight, a map of Zambia rendered in gold Optically Variable Ink, and the Zambian coat of arms.

The reverse highlights endemic natural elements including a baobab tree, two birds perched on a branch representing avian diversity, and the scenic Lumangwe Falls, one of Zambia's notable natural attractions located in Chienge District. The banknote was printed by Crane Currency, representing collaboration with international security printing expertise. The note's design emphasizes the connection between Zambian industrial development and its preserved natural heritage.

Lumangwe Falls, situated on the Kalungwishi River in northern Zambia, is one of the country's most visited waterfalls, forming part of a cluster of falls in the region alongside Kabwelume and Kundabwika Falls. The baobab tree, sometimes called the tree of life, is iconic to the African landscape and holds deep cultural significance for many Zambian communities, symbolizing longevity and resilience.

highlights.design: Obverse: Bank of Zambia logo, dump truck representing industry, fish eagle in flight, gold OVI map of Zambia, coat of arms. Reverse: Baobab tree, two birds on branch, Lumangwe Falls. Colors: gold and green.
highlights.reason: Heritage Series denomination emphasizing natural environment, wildlife biodiversity, and industrial development themes.
highlights.security: Gold-to-green windowed RollingStar i+ security thread with demetalized denomination and Bank emblem; gold-to-green Spark Flow Prime ink patch with flame effect; see-through register featuring endemic flowers; latent image of stylized flower.

title: 100 Kwacha
description: Zambia 100 Kwacha banknote from the 2024 Heritage Series featuring blue coloring with wildlife and natural landscape imagery.
body:
The 100 Kwacha banknote was released as part of the Bank of Zambia's Heritage Series in 2024. Measuring 130 x 69 millimeters, this blue-colored denomination serves as a higher-value note in the six-banknote Heritage Series. The obverse displays a fish eagle in flight, the Bank of Zambia logo, a flower as a registration device, a dump truck, a map of Zambia rendered in gold OVI, and the Zambian coat of arms.

The reverse features a baobab tree, two birds perched on a branch, and Lumangwe Falls, emphasizing Zambia's natural heritage and biodiversity. The color progression through the series — from green at lower denominations through mauve and gold to blue at higher values — creates visual hierarchy and aids rapid denomination recognition in everyday transactions.

The fish eagle (Haliaeetus vocifer), featured prominently on the obverse, is Zambia's national bird and a symbol of the nation's identity across multiple denominations in the Heritage Series. Its iconic cry and distinctive appearance over Zambia's many lakes and rivers make it one of Africa's most recognizable birds of prey. The blue color scheme of the 100 Kwacha note visually distinguishes it as a higher-value denomination within the series.

highlights.design: Obverse: Fish eagle in flight, Bank of Zambia logo, flower registration device, dump truck, gold OVI map of Zambia, coat of arms. Reverse: Baobab tree, two birds on branch, Lumangwe Falls. Color: blue. Dimensions: 130 x 69 mm.
highlights.reason: Higher-value Heritage Series denomination celebrating national wildlife heritage while modernizing Zambian currency to international standards.
highlights.security: Gold-to-green windowed RollingStar i+ security thread; gold-to-green Spark Flow Prime ink patch with flame effect; see-through register with endemic flowers; latent image of stylized flower on lower left front.

title: 200 Kwacha
description: Zambia 200 Kwacha banknote from the 2024 Heritage Series featuring purple and brown coloring with Kazungula Bridge and Kalambo Falls.
body:
The 200 Kwacha banknote was introduced in 2024 as part of the Bank of Zambia's Heritage Series, representing a newly introduced higher denomination designed to promote more efficient transactions in line with international best practices. The note features purple and brown coloring and bears the signature of Denny Kalyalya, the Bank of Zambia Governor.

The obverse depicts a fish eagle, the Kazungula Bridge connecting Zambia to Botswana across the Zambezi River, and the national coat of arms. The Kazungula Bridge, completed in 2021, is one of Africa's most significant infrastructure projects, carrying both road and rail traffic and symbolizing Zambia's role in regional commerce and connectivity. The reverse showcases the Ing'ombe Ilede tree, Kalambo Falls representing Zambia's scenic waterfall heritage, and rhinoceroses illustrating wildlife conservation efforts.

Kalambo Falls, at 235 meters, is one of Africa's highest uninterrupted waterfalls and is a UNESCO World Heritage Site, recognized for its archaeological significance as a site of early human activity dating back over 300,000 years. The introduction of the 200 Kwacha denomination alongside the 500 Kwacha note reflects the Bank of Zambia's modernization strategy to reduce reliance on multiple lower-value notes for larger transactions.

highlights.design: Obverse: Fish eagle, Kazungula Bridge connecting Zambia to Botswana, coat of arms. Reverse: Ing'ombe Ilede tree, Kalambo Falls (UNESCO World Heritage Site), rhinoceroses. Colors: purple and brown.
highlights.reason: New denomination introduced to facilitate efficient transactions and modernize the currency system in line with international practices.
highlights.security: Gold windowed security thread with demetalized Bank logo and denomination; watermark featuring fish eagle with electrotype designation.

title: 500 Kwacha
description: Zambia 500 Kwacha banknote from the 2024 Heritage Series — highest denomination — featuring purple and green coloring with the Bank of Zambia headquarters.
body:
The 500 Kwacha banknote was introduced in 2024 as part of the Bank of Zambia's new Heritage Series and represents the highest denomination in the six-note series. The note features distinctive purple and green coloring that distinguishes it from lower denominations. The obverse displays the Bank of Zambia headquarters building in Lusaka, representing the nation's financial governance and institutional strength, a fish eagle in flight symbolizing Zambian wildlife, a map of Zambia rendered in gold OVI, the Bank of Zambia logo, and a flower as a registration device.

The reverse features natural and cultural heritage elements that emphasize Zambia's environmental richness and biodiversity, consistent with the Heritage Series theme of celebrating the nation's natural and cultural patrimony. The 500 Kwacha and 200 Kwacha denominations were specifically introduced following international best practices for currency modernization, allowing for more efficient handling of large transactions.

The Bank of Zambia headquarters, depicted on the obverse, is located in Lusaka's central business district and serves as the institutional symbol of Zambia's monetary authority. The building's appearance on the highest denomination banknote reinforces the connection between financial governance and currency value, emphasizing the Bank's role in maintaining monetary stability and overseeing Zambia's financial system.

highlights.design: Obverse: Bank of Zambia Lusaka headquarters, fish eagle in flight, gold OVI map of Zambia, Bank logo, flower registration device. Reverse: Cultural and natural heritage elements. Colors: purple and green.
highlights.reason: Highest denomination in Heritage Series introduced to support efficient high-value transactions and modernize currency to international standards.
highlights.security: Advanced security features consistent with Heritage Series standards; color-shifting inks and threads; watermark with fish eagle motif; tactile design elements.

title: 1 ZiG (Zimbabwe Gold)
description: Zimbabwe 1 ZiG (Zimbabwe Gold) — issued as a coin only in April 2024; not released as a banknote in the initial ZiG currency introduction.
body:
The Reserve Bank of Zimbabwe announced on April 5, 2024 the introduction of the Zimbabwe Gold (ZiG), a new gold-backed currency to replace the Zimbabwean dollar. The ZiG was backed by Reserve Bank reserves consisting of USD 100 million in cash and 2,522 kilograms of gold valued at approximately USD 185 million. Eight denominations from 1 ZiG to 200 ZiG were planned for the new currency series.

However, the 1 ZiG denomination was not released as a banknote. On April 30, 2024, it was issued exclusively as a circulation coin in nickel-plated steel as part of the ZiG coin family alongside 2 ZiG and 5 ZiG denominations. The Reserve Bank's decision to issue the three lowest denominations as coins rather than banknotes reflected monetary policy considerations and practical circulation requirements. Only the 10 ZiG and 20 ZiG banknotes entered circulation in April 2024.

The ZiG currency represented Zimbabwe's sixth attempt at currency stabilization since dollarization in 2009. The gold-backing mechanism was designed to provide credibility and stability to the new currency, addressing the hyperinflationary episodes that had plagued previous Zimbabwe dollar iterations. The coin design features Zimbabwe Bird imagery and Domboremari (Great Zimbabwe ruins), representing the nation's historical and cultural heritage.

highlights.design: Coin (not banknote): features Zimbabwe Bird and Domboremari (Great Zimbabwe ruins) imagery in nickel-plated steel.
highlights.reason: Lowest ZiG denomination issued as coin only; the Reserve Bank restricted initial banknote circulation to 10 ZiG and 20 ZiG denominations to manage inflation during currency transition.
highlights.security: Coin specifications: nickel-plated steel composition; Zimbabwe Bird watermark equivalent design on coin face.

title: 2 ZiG (Zimbabwe Gold)
description: Zimbabwe 2 ZiG (Zimbabwe Gold) — issued as a coin only on April 30, 2024; not released as a banknote in the initial ZiG currency introduction.
body:
The Reserve Bank of Zimbabwe's April 5, 2024 announcement regarding the new ZiG currency outlined plans for eight denominations, with the 2 ZiG initially projected as a potential banknote denomination. However, this denomination was subsequently issued exclusively as a coin on April 30, 2024, minted in nickel-plated steel as part of the ZiG circulation coinage system alongside 1 ZiG and 5 ZiG denominations.

This decision reflected the Reserve Bank's monetary policy strategy to manage inflation and economic stability during the currency transition from the Zimbabwean dollar to the ZiG. The Reserve Bank maintained that restricting higher denominations to coin form would prevent the release from fueling inflation while still providing necessary denominations for daily transactions. The ZiG was Zimbabwe's sixth attempt at a stable currency since 2009, backed by gold reserves and foreign currency.

The 2 ZiG coin represented a mid-range value within the coin family, bridging transactions between the 1 ZiG and 5 ZiG. Only banknote denominations of 10 ZiG and 20 ZiG were released to the public in April 2024. The coin's design features Zimbabwe's national symbols including the Zimbabwe Bird and imagery from the Great Zimbabwe ruins at Domboremari, connecting the new currency to the nation's historical heritage.

highlights.design: Coin (not banknote): features Zimbabwe national symbols and Domboremari (Great Zimbabwe ruins) imagery; nickel-plated steel composition.
highlights.reason: Issued as coin only to control inflation during ZiG currency transition; the Reserve Bank limited banknote issuance to 10 ZiG and 20 ZiG denominations.
highlights.security: Coin specifications: nickel-plated steel; features Zimbabwe cultural heritage imagery consistent with other ZiG coin denominations.

title: 5 ZiG (Zimbabwe Gold)
description: Zimbabwe 5 ZiG (Zimbabwe Gold) — issued as a coin only on April 30, 2024; not released as a banknote in the initial ZiG currency introduction.
body:
The Reserve Bank of Zimbabwe's announcement of the ZiG currency on April 5, 2024 outlined plans for the gold-backed Zimbabwe Gold currency across multiple denominations. The 5 ZiG denomination was issued exclusively as a circulation coin on April 30, 2024, minted in nickel-plated steel. This decision was part of the Reserve Bank's comprehensive monetary policy strategy to introduce the gold-backed ZiG currency while managing inflationary pressures during the transition period.

The 5 ZiG coin served as the highest-value denomination within the three-coin family (1, 2, 5 ZiG), designed for everyday retail and commercial transactions. The Reserve Bank's decision to limit banknote circulation to 10 ZiG and 20 ZiG denominations, while postponing higher denominations (50, 100, 200 ZiG), reflected concern that releasing additional banknotes could undermine currency stability and fuel inflation in an economy with a history of hyperinflationary episodes.

The ZiG was backed by a combination of USD 100 million in foreign currency reserves and 2,522 kilograms of gold held by the Reserve Bank, providing tangible backing for the new currency. The gold-backing mechanism represented a significant departure from previous Zimbabwean dollar iterations and was designed to build public confidence in the new monetary system. The 5 ZiG coin features Zimbabwe's national symbols, including the Zimbabwe Bird and Great Zimbabwe ruins imagery.

highlights.design: Coin (not banknote): features Zimbabwe national symbols and Great Zimbabwe ruins imagery; nickel-plated steel composition.
highlights.reason: Highest denomination in the ZiG coin family, issued as coin only to manage inflation during the gold-backed currency transition; Reserve Bank limited banknote issuance to 10 ZiG and 20 ZiG.
highlights.security: Coin specifications: nickel-plated steel composition; Zimbabwe Bird and Domboremari designs consistent with ZiG coin series.

title: 10 ZiG (Zimbabwe Gold)
description: Zimbabwe 10 ZiG gold-backed banknote issued April 2024 featuring Chiremba balancing rocks and QR code authentication.
body:
The Reserve Bank of Zimbabwe introduced the 10 ZiG note on April 5, 2024, as part of the initial launch of the gold-backed ZiG currency system replacing the hyperinflated Zimbabwean dollar. This dark blue and red banknote measures 155 x 65 mm and bears the signature of Dr. John Mushayavanhu. The obverse displays the iconic Chiremba balancing rocks located in Epworth, representing Zimbabwe's natural heritage and geological significance. A QR code on the obverse provides instant verification of authenticity when scanned.

The reverse side depicts molten gold pouring into an ingot mold atop stacked gold bars, symbolizing Zimbabwe's mineral wealth and the currency's gold-backing mechanism. Both sides feature the Zimbabwe Bird as a registration device. Security features include a green-to-blue windowed security thread with demetalized RBZZIG inscription and a Zimbabwe Bird watermark.

The ZiG (Zimbabwe Gold) was backed by Reserve Bank reserves consisting of USD 100 million in cash and 2,522 kilograms of gold valued at approximately USD 185 million. The 10 ZiG was one of only two denominations that actually entered circulation initially; higher denominations (50, 100, 200 ZiG) were postponed to prevent inflationary pressures during the currency's critical adoption phase.

highlights.design: Obverse: Chiremba balancing rocks in Epworth, Zimbabwe Bird registration device, QR code for authentication. Reverse: Molten gold poured into ingot mold, stacked gold bars, Zimbabwe Bird registration device.
highlights.reason: Initial denomination of the Reserve Bank of Zimbabwe's new gold-backed currency system launched April 5, 2024 to replace the Zimbabwean dollar.
highlights.security: Green-to-blue windowed security thread with RBZZIG demetalization, Zimbabwe Bird watermark, QR code with plaintext verification string.

title: 20 ZiG (Zimbabwe Gold)
description: Zimbabwe 20 ZiG gold-backed banknote issued April 2024 featuring Great Zimbabwe ruins and Victoria Falls.
body:
The Reserve Bank of Zimbabwe released the 20 ZiG note on April 5, 2024, as the second active denomination of the newly launched gold-backed currency. The obverse prominently displays the Great Zimbabwe ruins, a UNESCO World Heritage Site representing the nation's pre-colonial architectural achievement and the seat of the Medieval Kingdom of Zimbabwe. The reverse showcases the majestic Victoria Falls, one of the world's most spectacular natural wonders located on the Zambezi River between Zimbabwe and Zambia.

The banknote's enhanced security features include a dynamic holographic security strip running alongside the central motif, providing sophisticated anti-counterfeiting protection. The serial number appears in striking red font, contributing both to visual appeal and security. The design is further secured by a detailed Zimbabwe Bird watermark that is aligned precisely across both obverse and reverse surfaces.

Victoria Falls, known locally as Mosi-oa-Tunya (The Smoke that Thunders), is one of the Seven Natural Wonders of the World and forms the world's largest curtain of falling water. Its placement on the 20 ZiG banknote alongside the Great Zimbabwe ruins reflects Zimbabwe's pride in both its ancient civilizational heritage and its extraordinary natural landscape. The 20 ZiG and 10 ZiG were the only ZiG denominations in active circulation during 2024.

highlights.design: Obverse: Great Zimbabwe archaeological ruins (UNESCO World Heritage Site). Reverse: Victoria Falls landscape with Zambezi River.
highlights.reason: Second denomination of the Reserve Bank of Zimbabwe's gold-backed ZiG currency system, launched April 5, 2024.
highlights.security: Dynamic holographic security strip, red serial number, Zimbabwe Bird watermark with precise front-to-back alignment.

title: 50 ZiG (Zimbabwe Gold)
description: Zimbabwe 50 ZiG banknote planned for 2024 but postponed due to inflation concerns; eventually released in 2026 as part of the upgraded BiG5 series.
body:
The Reserve Bank of Zimbabwe announced the 50 ZiG denomination as part of the original eight-denomination ZiG banknote series unveiled on April 5, 2024. However, the Reserve Bank postponed its release on May 30, 2024, citing inflationary concerns that higher denomination circulation could destabilize the economy during the currency's critical early adoption phase. The 50 ZiG was held in reserve, along with the 100 and 200 ZiG denominations, while only the 10 and 20 ZiG notes entered initial circulation.

By early 2026, economic conditions had improved significantly, with official inflation declining substantially, enabling the Reserve Bank to reconsider its conservative approach. The 50 ZiG was released as part of the new BiG5 second-generation series in 2026, featuring enhanced security features and updated designs compared to the original 2024 specifications. This delayed emission reflected monetary policy priorities aimed at establishing currency credibility before introducing higher-value notes.

The ZiG currency was backed by a combination of USD 100 million in foreign currency and 2,522 kilograms of gold, representing a significant departure from Zimbabwe's previous unsupported currency regimes. The phased introduction strategy demonstrated the Reserve Bank's commitment to sustainable monetary policy following Zimbabwe's turbulent currency history.

highlights.design: Part of ZiG series design family; specific design details per 2026 BiG5 release specifications.
highlights.reason: Postponed from 2024 launch due to inflation control measures; eventually issued in 2026 as part of upgraded BiG5 series once economic conditions stabilized.
highlights.security: Enhanced security features as part of 2026 BiG5 series standards.

title: 100 ZiG (Zimbabwe Gold)
description: Zimbabwe 100 ZiG banknote planned for 2024 but not issued; scheduled for later release as part of the BiG5 series.
body:
The 100 ZiG denomination was included in the original comprehensive ZiG banknote series unveiled by the Reserve Bank of Zimbabwe on April 5, 2024. Like the 50 and 200 ZiG notes, its release was postponed indefinitely on May 30, 2024, as monetary authorities determined that introducing higher denominations could exacerbate inflationary pressures during the critical early phase of the new currency's adoption. The Reserve Bank's cautious approach prioritized currency stabilization by limiting circulation to the 10 and 20 ZiG denominations.

The ZiG was Zimbabwe's sixth attempt at currency stabilization since the country's abandonment of the Zimbabwean dollar in 2009 in favor of a multi-currency system. The gold backing mechanism — supported by 2,522 kilograms of gold reserves and USD 100 million in foreign currency — was intended to provide credibility that previous currency iterations lacked. Restricting higher denominations from circulation was seen as prudent monetary management to preserve this credibility.

As of early 2026, the 100 ZiG remained in planned status, with the Reserve Bank indicating its eventual inclusion in subsequent phases of the BiG5 series rollout. The specific timeline depends on ongoing assessments of Zimbabwe's monetary stability and inflation metrics.

highlights.design: High-denomination note within ZiG series portfolio; specific design to be confirmed upon release.
highlights.reason: Postponed from 2024 launch due to inflation control measures; future release date subject to Reserve Bank monetary policy assessment.
highlights.security: To be confirmed upon eventual BiG5 series release.

title: 200 ZiG (Zimbabwe Gold)
description: Zimbabwe 200 ZiG banknote — highest planned denomination — announced April 2024 but not yet issued; future release contingent on monetary stability.
body:
The Reserve Bank of Zimbabwe included the 200 ZiG as the highest denomination in the original eight-denomination ZiG banknote series announced on April 5, 2024. Its release was postponed on May 30, 2024, alongside the 50 and 100 ZiG notes, as the Reserve Bank determined that introducing large-value denominations during the currency's initial phase could fuel inflationary expectations and undermine the ZiG's market acceptance. This reflected monetary policy conservatism aimed at ensuring the gold-backed currency achieved credibility before high-value notes entered circulation.

The Reserve Bank's strategy for the ZiG involved a deliberate two-phase approach: first establishing the currency's credibility and public acceptance through limited denomination circulation, then expanding to higher values as economic indicators improved. The 200 ZiG represented the pinnacle of this expansion plan, requiring the highest level of monetary confidence before release.

As of 2026, the 200 ZiG has not been released and remains in planned status. The Reserve Bank has indicated that higher denominations including the 200 ZiG may eventually be introduced in the BiG5 or subsequent series, but only when macroeconomic conditions and currency stability metrics fully support such action. Zimbabwe's history of hyperinflation means any decision to release higher denominations is made with extraordinary caution.

highlights.design: Highest-denomination note within original ZiG series design family; specific design details to be confirmed upon release.
highlights.reason: Postponed from 2024 launch to prevent inflation; conditional future release pending demonstration of long-term monetary stability.
highlights.security: To be confirmed upon potential future release.

issuer: National Bank of the Republic of Abkhazia
title: 25 Apsar
description: Abkhazia 25 Apsar commemorative banknote issued May 22, 2023, celebrating the 30th anniversary of the Patriotic War victory.
body:
The National Bank of the Republic of Abkhazia issued the 25 Apsar commemorative banknote on May 22, 2023, in commemoration of the 30th anniversary of the Patriotic War of the People of Abkhazia (1992-1993). This historic conflict resulted in de facto Abkhazian independence from Georgia and remains central to national identity. The banknote measures 150 mm x 65 mm and was executed on white cotton paper with a limited mintage of 15,000 pieces not distributed for general circulation, maintaining its collector status.

The obverse side displays the Abkhazian coat of arms, a traditional sailing ship representing maritime heritage, ornamental border designs, a hawk in flight symbolizing national pride and freedom, stars representing the night sky, and a detailed hawk head in profile. The reverse depicts two standing soldiers in military uniform holding swords and a rifle, commemorating the combatants of the 1992-1993 conflict, alongside matching stars and traditional ornamental patterns. A QR code in the lower right of the reverse provides a link to the National Bank website with detailed security feature descriptions.

Abkhazia is a disputed territory on the eastern coast of the Black Sea with a population of approximately 240,000. The Patriotic War, which resulted in the expulsion of Georgian forces, is the defining event of modern Abkhazian statehood. This commemorative banknote employs distinctive orange, yellow, and green coloration and represents the banking institution's use of currency as a vehicle for national commemoration.

highlights.design: Obverse: Abkhazian coat of arms, sailing ship, hawk in flight, stars, hawk profile. Reverse: Two soldiers with swords and rifle, stars, ornamental patterns, QR code.
highlights.reason: Commemorative issuance for 30th anniversary of 1992-1993 Patriotic War victory; limited edition of 15,000 pieces not for general circulation.
highlights.security: Watermark with basket weave pattern, two types of protective fibers, color-shifting HMC hidden multi-colour security element, Orlov effect in offset and metallographic printing, graphic guilloche elements, QR code for verification.

title: 1.000 Kwanzas
description: Angola 1,000 Kwanzas pink polymer banknote issued November 2023 featuring Kalandula waterfalls.
body:
The Banco Nacional de Angola issued the 1,000 Kwanzas banknote in November 2023 as part of the ongoing modernization of Angola's currency with polymer substrate materials. The pink-colored note features Kalandula waterfalls, one of Africa's highest waterfalls located on the Lucala River in Malanje Province, with a fall height of approximately 105 meters and a width of 400 meters. These waterfalls represent Angola's natural landmarks and tourism assets.

The polymer construction ensures superior durability compared to traditional paper notes, extending the banknote's circulation lifespan to approximately four times longer than conventional fiber-based currency. The banknote incorporates two transparent windows cut into the polymer substrate: the first contains a semitransparent representation of O Pensador (The Thinker), a national cultural symbol representing Angolan intellectual heritage, with the denomination printed in translucent ink. The second window features a geometric figure representing the traditional musical instrument Omakola, reflecting Angolan cultural heritage and musical traditions.

Kalandula waterfalls, set within a national park of the same name, are the second largest waterfalls in Africa by volume of water and among the largest by width. Their inclusion on Angolan currency reflects the nation's pride in its spectacular natural geography. The polymer substrate and clear windows represent contemporary anti-counterfeiting advances adopted throughout Angola's modern banknote series.

highlights.design: Pink-colored polymer note. Obverse: Kalandula waterfalls (Malanje Province), transparent window with Pensador (Thinker) cultural figure. Reverse: Traditional Omakola musical instrument in geometric window.
highlights.reason: Part of Banco Nacional de Angola's currency modernization program, upgrading to durable polymer substrate with enhanced security features and natural landmark imagery.
highlights.security: Two transparent polymer windows (Pensador/Thinker figure, Omakola musical instrument), polymer substrate for enhanced durability and counterfeit resistance, translucent ink printing.

title: 2.000 Kwanzas
description: Angola 2,000 Kwanzas green polymer banknote issued November 2020 featuring Serra da Leba mountain pass.
body:
The Banco Nacional de Angola issued the 2,000 Kwanzas banknote on November 11, 2020, as part of the comprehensive redesign of Angola's banknote series using polymer substrates. The green-colored note features Serra da Leba, a dramatic mountain pass in Huila Province renowned for its spectacular hairpin turns and scenic vista over the Namibe Desert below. The Serra da Leba road, engineered in 1971, is considered one of the most beautiful mountain roads in Africa and is a major tourist attraction.

The polymer material provides enhanced durability, environmental resistance, and counterfeit prevention compared to traditional paper notes. The banknote design incorporates two distinct transparent windows cut into the polymer substrate. The primary window features a semitransparent representation of O Pensador (The Thinker), a significant cultural emblem within Angolan artistic and intellectual traditions, with the denomination printed in translucent ink. A second geometric window represents the Omakola, a traditional Angolan musical instrument, celebrating the nation's rich musical heritage.

Serra da Leba's inclusion on Angola's highest-value polymer banknote reflects the significance of the Huila Province landscape in Angolan national identity. The Angolan 2,000 Kwanzas represents the highest denomination in the polymer series launched in 2020, and the choice of a mountain landscape symbolizes both natural grandeur and the heights of national aspiration.

highlights.design: Green-colored polymer note. Obverse: Serra da Leba mountain pass in Huila Province. Transparent windows: Pensador (Thinker) cultural figure and Omakola traditional musical instrument.
highlights.reason: Issued November 11, 2020 as the highest denomination in Banco Nacional de Angola's polymer banknote series redesign, celebrating natural heritage and cultural identity.
highlights.security: Transparent polymer windows with cultural imagery (Pensador/Thinker figure, Omakola instrument), polymer substrate for durability and counterfeit resistance, translucent ink effects.

issuer: Banco Central de la República Argentina
title: 1.000 Pesos
description: Argentina 1,000 Pesos banknote (2023) featuring the Rufous Hornero, Argentina's national bird, from the Fourth Series.
body:
The Banco Central de la República Argentina continues to circulate the 1,000 Pesos banknote in its Fourth Series design featuring the Rufous Hornero (Furnarius rufus), Argentina's national bird. The vibrant red-orange-yellow colored note prominently features the hornero in its natural Pampas habitat alongside native plants, bird paw prints, bird's nests, branches, and flowers that create a naturalistic representation of Pampean ecology. The reverse side showcases the geographic landscape of the Pampas region with a cartographic representation of Argentina's national territory.

The denomination was first introduced in 2017 and continued through 2023 as the highest standard circulation denomination before new higher-value notes were introduced. The 1,000 Pesos note reflects Argentina's established tradition of featuring indigenous wildlife as national symbols on its currency, celebrating biodiversity and natural heritage. The hornero is particularly beloved in Argentine culture for building elaborate mud nests and is considered a symbol of hard work and industriousness.

The banknote incorporates multiple security features including a hologram security thread, advanced printing techniques, and tamper-evident design elements. The red-orange-yellow color scheme creates distinctive visual identification, making it easily recognizable for everyday transactions. Argentina's ongoing currency challenges — including significant inflation — led to the rapid introduction of higher denominations alongside continued circulation of the 1,000 Pesos note.

highlights.design: Red-orange-yellow colored note. Obverse: Rufous Hornero national bird, nest, paw prints, flora of Pampas. Reverse: Pampas landscape and map of Argentina.
highlights.reason: Continuing circulation of Argentina's Fourth Series featuring native wildlife; 2023 variant maintains design consistency while meeting circulation demand.
highlights.security: Hologram security thread, advanced printing techniques, tamper-evident design elements, precise registration features.

issuer: Banco Central de la República Argentina
title: 2.000 Pesos
description: Argentina 2,000 Pesos commemorative banknote issued May 22, 2023, celebrating the development of science and medicine in Argentina, featuring Dr. Cecilia Grierson and Dr. Ramon Carrillo.
body:
The Banco Central de la República Argentina issued the 2,000 Pesos banknote on May 22, 2023, as a commemorative note honoring the development of science and medicine in Argentina. This represented the highest denomination banknote ever circulated in Argentine currency history at the time of issue. The note measures 155 mm x 65 mm and features a dark gray and pink color scheme with signatures of C. Moreau and Pesce.

The obverse prominently displays portraits of two pioneering figures in Argentine medicine: Dr. Cecilia Grierson (1859-1934), the first woman to graduate as a physician in Argentina (1889) and founder of Latin America's first formally structured nursing school; and Dr. Ramon Carrillo (1906-1956), Argentina's first Minister of Health (1946-1954). Additional obverse imagery includes molecular representations, a caduceus symbol in green-to-blue optically variable ink, and modern hospital buildings. The reverse depicts the National Institute of Microbiology in Buenos Aires, a leading research institution.

Dr. Grierson's pioneering work in obstetrics and nursing education established foundational standards for healthcare professionalism in Latin America, while Dr. Carrillo's tenure as Health Minister eliminated malaria, eradicated endemic diseases, and dramatically reduced infant mortality through public health innovation. The commemorative nature of this banknote distinguished it within Argentina's currency family and reflected the nation's commitment to honoring scientific achievement.

highlights.design: Dark gray and pink colored note. Obverse: Dr. Cecilia Grierson and Dr. Ramon Carrillo portraits, molecule, caduceus in OVI, hospital buildings. Reverse: National Institute of Microbiology building in Buenos Aires.
highlights.reason: Commemorative issuance celebrating development of science and medicine in Argentina; highest-denomination Argentine banknote at issue date; honors two pioneering medical figures.
highlights.security: Watermark of Dr. Grierson and Dr. Carrillo, green-to-blue caduceus optically variable ink (OVI), identification code with tactile relief, microprinting, latent images.

title: 50 Dollars
description: Australia 50 Dollars banknote from the next-generation series (2023) featuring indigenous inventor David Unaipon and Edith Cowan with enhanced security features.
body:
The $50 banknote honors two pioneering figures who championed social change in Australia: David Unaipon, an Aboriginal inventor and the country's first published Aboriginal author from the Ngarrindjeri nation, and Edith Cowan, the first female member of an Australian parliament from Western Australia. The design incorporates cultural symbols including shields from Unaipon's Ngarrindjeri heritage and representations of miwi and navel cord exchange practices, alongside Acacia humifusa (Unaipon's totem) and the Black Swan, the official bird of Western Australia.

The reverse features a gumnut brooch symbolizing the difficulties women faced entering parliament, and the King Edward Memorial Hospital for Women, which Cowan helped establish. The banknote employs cutting-edge security technology to combat counterfeiting while maintaining ease of recognition through unchanged color and size. A top-to-bottom clear window displays dynamic features including a reversing number and a flying bird, representing a significant advancement in Australian banknote technology.

David Unaipon's image has appeared on the $50 note since 1995, while Edith Cowan has featured on the reverse since the same year. The next-generation series introduced significantly enhanced security features including a new clear window and microprinting of excerpts from Unaipon's Legendary Tales of the Ngarrindjeri and Cowan's first parliamentary speech, ensuring these stories remain literally woven into the fabric of Australian currency.

highlights.design: Obverse: David Unaipon with Ngarrindjeri shields and ngaitji symbols, Acacia humifusa. Reverse: Edith Cowan with gumnut brooch, King Edward Memorial Hospital, Black Swan, and Aboriginal cultural motifs.
highlights.reason: Security upgrade for next-generation banknote series implementing advanced anti-counterfeiting features while honoring indigenous and women's history.
highlights.security: Top-to-bottom clear window with dynamic reversing number and flying bird; patch with rolling-color effect; microprinting of excerpts from Unaipon's Legendary Tales and Cowan's first parliamentary speech.

title: 20 Taka
description: Bangladesh 20 Taka banknote from the new Historical and Archaeological Architecture of Bangladesh series, featuring the Kantaji Hindu temple.
body:
The 20 Taka banknote belongs to Bangladesh Bank's comprehensive redesign initiative reflecting the nation's cultural heritage and historical landmarks. The obverse features the Kantajew Hindu temple located in Dinajpur, one of Bangladesh's most significant architectural monuments built in the early 18th century and renowned for its intricate terracotta artwork, alongside a water lily symbolizing the country's natural beauty. The reverse depicts the Somapura Mahavihara, an important Buddhist monastery in Naogaon that represents the region's diverse religious heritage and historical significance.

This denomination measures 127 mm x 60 mm and is printed on 100 percent cotton paper, maintaining traditional banknote materials. The series represents a departure from previous designs that featured individual portraits, instead emphasizing Bangladesh's archaeological treasures, cultural landmarks, and environmental diversity. The note is primarily colored in blue, green, and brown tones reflecting the natural landscape and historical significance of the depicted structures.

The Kantajew temple, dedicated to the deity Kantaji (a form of Krishna), is celebrated for its remarkable terracotta panels depicting scenes from the Hindu epics Mahabharata and Ramayana. Somapura Mahavihara, a UNESCO World Heritage Site built in the 8th century during the Pala dynasty, was one of the greatest Buddhist centers of learning in the ancient world and attracted scholars from across Asia.

highlights.design: Obverse: Kantaji Hindu temple in Dinajpur with water lily motif. Reverse: Somapura Mahavihara Buddhist monastery (UNESCO World Heritage Site) in Naogaon.
highlights.reason: Comprehensive redesign to showcase Bangladesh's cultural heritage and historical architecture rather than individual figures.
highlights.security: Advanced security features consistent with Bangladesh Bank's new series standards including security thread and watermark.

title: 500 Taka
description: Bangladesh 500 Taka green banknote (2023) featuring Sheikh Mujibur Rahman and the National Martyrs' Monument, with agricultural and river scenes on the reverse.
body:
The 500 Taka banknote from 2023 is a green circulation note measuring 152 x 65 mm that honors Sheikh Mujibur Rahman, the founding father of Bangladesh, and celebrates the nation's independence and development. The obverse displays a portrait of Sheikh Mujibur Rahman alongside the National Martyrs' Monument in Savar, a solemn reminder of the sacrifices made during Bangladesh's struggle for independence in 1971. The signature on the note is that of Governor Abdur Rouf Talukder.

The reverse illustrates scenes depicting Bangladesh's agricultural and rural heritage: farmers plowing fields with oxen, crops being irrigated, fishermen carrying traditional bamboo nets, and boats on canals, representing the nation's commitment to rural development and sustainable livelihoods. These images celebrate Bangladesh's identity as a predominantly agrarian nation where the rivers, fields, and fishing traditions form the backbone of everyday life for millions of citizens.

The banknote incorporates traditional design elements alongside modern security technology. The 500 Taka note represents one of the higher-value denominations in Bangladesh's circulation, making its security features particularly important for protecting against counterfeiting. The note's green coloring reflects Bangladesh's lush landscape and agricultural identity.

highlights.design: Obverse: Sheikh Mujibur Rahman portrait with National Martyrs' Monument at Savar. Reverse: Agricultural scenes including oxen plowing, irrigation, fishermen with nets, and canal boats.
highlights.reason: Standard circulation note honoring the nation's founding father and celebrating economic and agricultural progress.
highlights.security: Segmented windowed security thread with holographic bank logo and denomination; watermark of Sheikh Mujibur Rahman with electrotype bank logo; Optically Variable Ink (OVI) in gold-to-green; intaglio printing for raised relief.

title: 50 Taka
description: Bangladesh 50 Taka commemorative banknote (2023) celebrating the Bangabandhu Sheikh Mujibur Rahman Tunnel under the Karnaphuli River.
body:
The 50 Taka banknote issued in 2023 serves as a commemorative note celebrating the Bangabandhu Sheikh Mujibur Rahman Tunnel, a major infrastructure achievement connecting Chittagong city with the industrial zone on the south bank of the Karnaphuli River. The tunnel, opened in October 2023, is the first underwater tunnel in Bangladesh and South Asia's longest road tunnel, representing a transformational milestone in the nation's infrastructure development. The design theme centers on the concept of connectivity creating opportunities.

The banknote commemorates the tunnel's significance in improving transportation and economic linkages across the nation's regions. The note incorporates imagery related to the tunnel's construction and its role in national development, including depictions of the Karnaphuli River and the broader connectivity vision. This commemorative emission reflects Bangladesh Bank's practice of issuing special editions to celebrate important national achievements and infrastructure milestones.

The Bangabandhu Sheikh Mujibur Rahman Tunnel, built with Chinese technical and financial assistance from China Communications Construction Company, stretches approximately 3.32 kilometers and will ultimately link Chittagong to Cox's Bazar. The commemorative 50 Taka note was issued to mark this achievement, acknowledging both the engineering feat and its economic significance for Bangladesh's continued development.

highlights.design: Commemorative design featuring the Bangabandhu Sheikh Mujibur Rahman Tunnel, Karnaphuli River, and connectivity infrastructure imagery.
highlights.reason: Commemorative emission celebrating the completion of Bangladesh's first underwater tunnel, opened October 2023 — a major national infrastructure achievement.
highlights.security: Standard Bangladesh Bank security features appropriate for commemorative circulation notes including windowed security thread and watermark.

title: 100 Pesos
description: Cuba 100 Pesos banknote (2023) featuring Carlos Manuel de Cespedes and the Jose Marti monument at the Anti-Imperialist Tribune in Havana.
body:
The Banco Central de Cuba issued a new 100 pesos banknote in 2023 featuring Carlos Manuel de Cespedes, regarded as the Father of the Homeland and a principal figure in Cuba's independence movement who launched the Ten Years' War against Spanish colonial rule in 1868. The obverse displays a portrait of Cespedes, while the reverse illustrates the iconic Jose Marti monument at Havana's Anti-Imperialist Tribune opposite the U.S. Embassy, representing Cuba's commitment to sovereignty and independence.

The banknote incorporates a watermark featuring the portrait of Celia Sanchez Manduley, a legendary revolutionary figure and longtime companion of Fidel Castro who was celebrated for her role in the Cuban Revolution. Measuring 150 x 70 mm, the note was printed by the Departamento Impresos Seguridad in Cuba. A notable design change distinguishes the 2023 version from previous emissions: it lacks tactile relief and Braille code for blind individuals, representing a departure from accessibility features included in earlier versions.

Carlos Manuel de Cespedes holds an iconic place in Cuban national identity as the man who freed his slaves and declared Cuban independence at La Demajagua on October 10, 1868, the date now celebrated as Cuban Independence Day. His portrait on the 100 pesos note underscores the continuity between Cuba's 19th century independence struggle and its 20th century revolution.

highlights.design: Obverse: Carlos Manuel de Cespedes portrait. Reverse: Jose Marti statue at Anti-Imperialist Tribune in Havana. Watermark: Celia Sanchez Manduley portrait.
highlights.reason: Updated security design and printing improvements; 2023 version removes tactile relief features present in previous editions.
highlights.security: Watermark featuring Cespedes and Sanchez Manduley; holographic security elements; serial number in lower margin tab; lithographic printing technology.

title: 5.000 Korun Českých
description: Czech National Bank 5,000 Korun Ceskych banknote (2023) featuring Tomas Garrigue Masaryk — a reprint with updated printing technology.
body:
The 5,000 korun banknote issued in 2023 by the Czech National Bank features Tomas Garrigue Masaryk, the founding statesman and first president of the Czechoslovak Republic, honoring his role in establishing an independent Czech state after World War I. The dark blue note measures 170 x 74 mm and bears the signature of Czech National Bank Governor Michal Ales. The 2023 reprint maintains the artistic design of the 2009 version, representing continuity in the nation's currency design while incorporating necessary printing technology updates.

The Czech National Bank announced that this reprint involved necessary changes in printing technology implemented at the State Printing Office to improve currency tracking and enhance security against counterfeiting, though the differences resulting from new printing technology are not recognizable to the public with the naked eye. The banknote was printed by Statni tiskarna cenin (State Printing Office) in Prague, which has been producing Czech security documents since 1919.

The 5,000 korun note is the highest denomination in Czech circulation and plays an important role in significant financial transactions. The ČNB offered to exchange older 5,000 korun notes for the 2023 version free of charge, with both older and new versions remaining valid for circulation. Masaryk's image on this highest-value denomination reflects his foundational importance to Czech national identity and democratic traditions.

highlights.design: Portrait of Tomas Garrigue Masaryk, founder of the Czechoslovak Republic, with Czech national symbols and historical imagery. Dark blue coloring, 170 x 74 mm.
highlights.reason: Necessary printing technology update for improved currency tracking and enhanced anti-counterfeiting measures while maintaining design continuity from 2009 version.
highlights.security: 3-mm puce-to-green windowed security thread with demetalized 5000 Kc; localized stepped watermark featuring Masaryk portrait, negative white line 5000 numeral, and sitting eagle; windowed thread with recurring hatched microtext CNB 5000 Kc.

title: 1.000 Korun Českých
description: Czech National Bank commemorative 1,000 Korun Ceskych banknote (February 2023) celebrating 30 years of Czech currency independence with a special overprint.
body:
The Czech National Bank issued a limited commemorative 1,000 korun banknote in February 2023 to celebrate the 30th anniversary of Czech currency independence, marking three decades since the Czechoslovak currency split following the dissolution of Czechoslovakia in 1993. The banknote is based on the standard 2008-dated 1,000 korun design featuring historian Frantisek Palacky on the obverse, renowned for his extensive historical writings about the Czech lands and their inhabitants, which earned him the title Father of the Nation.

The obverse also displays a linden leaf in green-to-gold Optically Variable Ink (OVI), a tree with exposed roots, and an open book symbolizing knowledge and historical scholarship. The reverse features a female eagle and Kromeriz Chateau alongside a stylized tree and the Czech coat of arms. What distinguishes this commemorative edition is the prominent 30th anniversary overprint in the lower left corner reading CNB 1993 (30) 2023, along with the official 30-year anniversary logo, the letters CNB, and the dates 1993 and 2023.

The Czech National Bank produced exactly 200,000 commemorative notes, released on February 8, 2023, with a purchase limit of 5 pieces per buyer. The overprint commemorates the peaceful separation of Czechoslovakia into the Czech Republic and Slovakia on January 1, 1993, known as the Velvet Divorce, and the introduction of the independent Czech koruna currency. This limited edition holds particular significance for Czech numismatists and reflects national pride in monetary sovereignty.

highlights.design: Obverse: Frantisek Palacky portrait with linden leaf in OVI, tree with roots, open book. Reverse: Female eagle, Kromeriz Chateau, stylized tree, Czech coat of arms. Special overprint: CNB 1993 (30) 2023 anniversary marking.
highlights.reason: Commemorative emission celebrating 30 years of Czech currency independence since the 1993 Czechoslovak dissolution. Limited edition of 200,000 pieces, maximum 5 per buyer.
highlights.security: 30th anniversary overprint with official logo; standard 1,000 korun security features including OVI linden leaf, security thread, and watermark.

title: 2 Dollars
description: East Caribbean States 2 Dollars commemorative polymer banknote (December 2023) honoring the 40th anniversary of the ECCB, featuring Sir Vivian Richards.
body:
The Eastern Caribbean Central Bank issued a historic commemorative $2 polymer banknote on December 1, 2023, to celebrate 40 years of the institution's establishment in 1983. The note honors Sir Isaac Vivian Viv Richards, the legendary West Indian cricketer known as The Master Blaster, whose portrait appears prominently on the obverse alongside a cricket bat and a conch shell representing the region's maritime heritage. The Eastern Caribbean Central Bank building in Basseterre, St. Kitts is also depicted on the obverse, anchoring the design to the institution itself.

The reverse features a vibrant coral reef scene with tropical fish and turtles, celebrating the Caribbean's natural marine ecosystems and biodiversity. Measuring 132 mm x 65 mm, the orange, blue, and black polymer banknote was signed by Timothy Antoine. The ECCB captured the prestigious 2023 Bank Note of the Year award from the International Bank Note Society (IBNS), as well as the Best Limited Circulation/Commemorative Banknote award from the International Association of Currency Affairs (IACA), recognizing its innovative design and cultural significance.

Sir Vivian Richards, born in Antigua and Barbuda and a member of the ECCB's member states, is widely considered one of the greatest cricket batsmen of all time. His appearance on the commemorative note reflects the Eastern Caribbean's pride in its sporting heritage and cultural icons. The polymer substrate ensures the commemorative note's durability as a lasting tribute to the ECCB's four decades of regional monetary service.

highlights.design: Obverse: Sir Vivian Richards with cricket bat, conch shell, and ECCB building in Basseterre, St. Kitts. Reverse: Coral reef scene with tropical fish and sea turtles, 40th anniversary logo.
highlights.reason: Commemorative emission celebrating 40 years of the Eastern Caribbean Central Bank's establishment and regional monetary service. Won IBNS Bank Note of the Year 2023.
highlights.security: Polymer substrate with transparent windows, holographic elements, microprinting, color-shifting inks.

title: 10 Pounds
description: Egypt 10 Pounds polymer banknote featuring the Al-Fattah Al-Alim Mosque and ancient Egyptian heritage imagery — Egypt's first polymer banknote.
body:
The Central Bank of Egypt introduced Egypt's first polymer banknote, the 10 pound note, on July 5, 2022, with continued circulation through 2023. This groundbreaking note was produced at Egypt's new state-of-the-art printing facility located in the New Administrative Capital east of Cairo — the largest print works in Egypt. The obverse features the Al-Fattah Al-Alim Mosque, a magnificent example of contemporary Islamic architecture located in the new capital, representing Egypt's modern development and architectural vision.

The reverse celebrates Egypt's ancient heritage, depicting the statue of Hatshepsut, the female pharaoh of the New Kingdom, alongside Queen Cleopatra imagery and the Pyramids of Giza, bridging the nation's Islamic and Pharaonic civilizations in a single design. Advanced intaglio printing techniques enhance the design elements, including the Central Bank of Egypt text, denomination value, and mosque imagery.

Under ultraviolet light, design elements appear in vibrant green, yellow, and orange hues, providing additional security and visual appeal. The Al-Fattah Al-Alim Mosque, which translates as the Mosque of the All-Knowing Conqueror, is one of the largest mosques in the world and serves as the centerpiece of Egypt's new administrative capital. Paper 10-pound notes continue to circulate alongside the polymer versions, which offer approximately four times greater durability.

highlights.design: Obverse: Al-Fattah Al-Alim Mosque in the New Administrative Capital. Reverse: Hatshepsut statue, Cleopatra imagery, and Great Pyramids of Giza bridging Islamic and Pharaonic heritage.
highlights.reason: Egypt's first polymer banknote — technological upgrade as part of Central Bank of Egypt's modernization initiative and security enhancement program.
highlights.security: Large holographic window with detailed mosque hologram (silver obverse, golden reverse); advanced intaglio printing; UV-reactive inks in green, yellow, and orange; Guardian polymer substrate.

title: 20 Pounds
description: Egypt 20 Pounds green polymer banknote (June 2023) featuring the Muhammad Ali Mosque and Queen Cleopatra — Egypt's first banknote with Braille accessibility features.
body:
The Central Bank of Egypt issued a new 20 Egyptian pound polymer banknote in June 2023, using cutting-edge polymer production technology at Egypt's new printing facility in the New Administrative Capital. The green polymer note measures 137 mm x 69 mm and bears the signature of Central Bank Governor Hassan Abdullah. This denomination holds the distinction of being the first Egyptian banknote to incorporate tactile features specifically designed for accessibility: ADA Braille dots crafted on the top-left corner enable blind and visually impaired individuals to identify the banknote's value by touch.

The obverse features the Great Mosque of Muhammad Ali Pasha, also known as the Alabaster Mosque, one of Cairo's most iconic Islamic architectural monuments dominating the Cairo Citadel skyline since its completion in 1848. The reverse depicts Queen Cleopatra alongside the Great Pyramid of Giza, Pharaoh Ramses II in a war chariot, and papyrus motifs, representing the grandeur of ancient Egypt. Security features include a transparent window displaying the mosque image with value inscriptions in Arabic script and tactile embossing for additional verification.

The Alabaster Mosque, commissioned by Muhammad Ali Pasha — the Ottoman viceroy who modernized Egypt in the early 19th century — remains a pilgrimage site for tourists and a symbol of Islamic architecture in Africa. Its placement on the 20 pound note alongside ancient Egyptian imagery creates a visual narrative bridging 3,000 years of Egyptian civilization. The previous paper 20-pound banknote remains valid in parallel circulation.

highlights.design: Obverse: Great Mosque of Muhammad Ali Pasha (Alabaster Mosque) in Cairo. Reverse: Queen Cleopatra, Great Pyramid of Giza, Ramses II in war chariot, and papyrus motifs.
highlights.reason: Polymer upgrade and accessibility innovation — first Egyptian banknote featuring tactile ADA Braille dots for visually impaired users.
highlights.security: Transparent window with mosque image and Arabic denomination inscription; tactile emboss for accessibility; ADA Braille dots; advanced intaglio printing; holographic elements; polymer substrate.

title: 100 Birr
description: Ethiopia 100 Birr banknote (2023) featuring the Enqulab Gemb fortress in Gondar and the Sof Omar Caves.
body:
The National Bank of Ethiopia released its 100 Birr note in 2023 as part of a modernized currency series. The note is dated Ethiopian Year 2015 (AD 2023) and bears the signature of Yinager Dessie. This blue denomination measures 147 mm x 72 mm and serves as a standard circulation banknote for the country's currency system.

The obverse side features a lion head and the Enqulab Gemb fortress located in Gondar, showcasing Ethiopia's rich medieval heritage. The fortress complex in Gondar was built in the 17th and 18th centuries and served as the royal seat of Ethiopian emperors, earning the city the nickname the Camelot of Africa. The reverse depicts the Sof Omar Caves and the historic walled city of Harar, emphasizing the nation's archaeological and architectural diversity across different regions.

The Sof Omar Caves form one of the most extensive cave systems in Africa, stretching over 15 kilometers through limestone mountains in the Bale region. The ancient walled city of Harar, a UNESCO World Heritage Site, is regarded as the fourth holiest city in Islam and home to over 80 mosques. Together these landmarks illustrate Ethiopia's extraordinary cultural and religious heritage spanning centuries.

highlights.design: Obverse: Enqulab Gemb fortress in Gondar and lion head. Reverse: Sof Omar Caves and historic gates of the walled city of Harar.
highlights.reason: Part of National Bank of Ethiopia's modernized currency series celebrating the nation's cultural and archaeological heritage.
highlights.security: 4-mm Rolling Star windowed security thread with demetalized NBE 100, castle and obelisk watermark.

title: 100 Cents
description: Fiji 100 Cents commemorative polymer banknote (August 2023) — world's first 100-cent denomination — celebrating the Chinese Lunar Year of the Dragon.
body:
The Reserve Bank of Fiji released a special 100 cents commemorative banknote on August 8, 2023, making it the world's first 100-cent Year of the Dragon commemorative banknote and the world's first plastic banknote with a 100-cent denomination. This non-circulating numismatic note is printed on SAFEGUARD polymer substrate by De La Rue and measures 145 mm x 70 mm. While the note has legal tender status, it is intended for collectors with a local selling price of FJD 30 and a limited production of 950,000 units.

The obverse features a hibiscus flower, the national coat of arms, and a gold-to-green optically variable ink (OVI) hibiscus flower. The reverse displays a stylized dragon with clouds, a red lantern, and the Reserve Bank of Fiji logo, celebrating the Chinese cultural tradition of the lunar calendar's Year of the Dragon. The note employs De La Rue's patented Spotlight, PureImage, and Depth technologies in its production.

A distinctive feature is the half-window foil strip containing a pseudo relief laser gold numeral, a coral fish that moves when tilted, and animated colorful decorative motifs. The Fiji 100 Cents note represents a creative approach to commemorative currency, using a denomination not typically associated with banknotes to create a unique collector's item bridging Pacific Island culture with Chinese heritage traditions.

highlights.design: Obverse: Hibiscus flower, national coat of arms, gold-to-green OVI hibiscus. Reverse: Stylized Year of the Dragon dragon with clouds, red lantern, Reserve Bank of Fiji logo.
highlights.reason: Commemorative issue celebrating Chinese Lunar Year of the Dragon; world's first 100-cent denomination banknote. Limited edition of 950,000 units.
highlights.security: Holographic foil strip with waves, leaves, banded iguana, red-throated lorikeet, fish, corals; De La Rue Spotlight, PureImage, and Depth technologies; SAFEGUARD polymer substrate.

title: 5 Dalasis
description: Gambia 5 Dalasis red polymer banknote (2023) featuring a giant kingfisher bird — lowest denomination of the 2019-2023 series.
body:
The Central Bank of The Gambia issued the 5 Dalasis banknote in 2023 as part of its 2019-2023 modernized currency series featuring native birds of The Gambia. This red polymer note measures 125 mm x 65 mm and bears the signatures of Jallow and Saidy. The denomination represents the lowest value in the current circulating series and includes enhanced security features consistent with modern banknote production standards.

The obverse side features a giant kingfisher bird perched on a tree branch and in flight, along with two standing bush fowl birds serving as registration marks and the national coat of arms. The giant kingfisher (Megaceryle maxima) is the largest kingfisher in Africa, commonly found along Gambia's rivers and waterways, making it a fitting symbol for the country's rich avian heritage. The reverse depicts cattle and a rancher on a tropical landscape, representing Gambia's agricultural traditions.

The Gambia is world-renowned as a premier birdwatching destination in West Africa, with over 600 bird species recorded in a country smaller than many national parks on the continent. The birds-themed banknote series celebrates this extraordinary biodiversity while providing a distinctive visual identity that sets Gambian currency apart internationally.

highlights.design: Obverse: Giant kingfisher bird in flight and perched, standing bush fowls as registration marks, national coat of arms. Reverse: Cattle and rancher on tropical landscape.
highlights.reason: Lowest denomination in Central Bank of The Gambia's modernized 2019-2023 currency series celebrating Gambia's exceptional bird diversity.
highlights.security: Holographic windowed security thread with demetalized bird imagery and denomination; two bush fowls as registration device; pearlescent stripe; crocodile head watermark with electrotype 5.

title: 10 Dalasis
description: Gambia 10 Dalasis green polymer banknote (2023) featuring a dove bird and the Kunta Kinteh ferry on the reverse.
body:
The Central Bank of The Gambia released the 10 Dalasis banknote in 2023 as the second denomination in its modernized 2019-2023 currency series. This green polymer note measures 127 mm x 65 mm and bears the signatures of Jallow and Saidy. The denomination continues the birds-themed series of Gambian banknotes, with each note showcasing native bird species representative of the country's remarkable avifauna.

The obverse features a dove bird perched on a tree branch and in flight, accompanied by two standing bush fowl birds as registration marks and the national coat of arms. The reverse depicts the Kunta Kinteh ferry, a significant Gambian vessel that connects Banjul to Barra across the River Gambia at its mouth, representing the nation's river transport heritage. The ferry crossing bears the name of Kunta Kinteh, the African slave whose story was told in Alex Haley's novel Roots.

The River Gambia is the defining geographical feature of the country, running its entire length from east to west. The ferry connection between Banjul and Barra represents one of the most important transport links in Gambia, connecting the capital with the northern regions of the country. Its depiction on the 10 Dalasis note underscores the river's central role in Gambian daily life, culture, and national identity.

highlights.design: Obverse: Dove bird in flight and perched, standing bush fowls, national coat of arms. Reverse: Kunta Kinteh ferry connecting Banjul and Barra across the River Gambia.
highlights.reason: Second denomination in Central Bank of The Gambia's 2019-2023 series featuring avian and cultural transport heritage themes.
highlights.security: Holographic windowed security thread with demetalized bird and denomination; pearlescent stripe with denomination; crocodile head watermark with electrotype 10.

title: 20 Dalasis
description: Gambia 20 Dalasis blue polymer banknote (2023) featuring the red-billed firefinch and national coat of arms.
body:
The Central Bank of The Gambia issued the 20 Dalasis banknote in 2023 as the third denomination in its modernized 2019-2023 currency series. This blue polymer note measures 125 mm x 65 mm and bears the signatures of Buah Saidy and Dr. Abodulie Sireh Jallow. The denomination continues the tradition of featuring indigenous Gambian birds, with the red-billed firefinch chosen as the primary design element.

The obverse displays the red-billed firefinch (Lagonosticta senegala), a small but vibrant bird common throughout sub-Saharan Africa and a familiar sight in Gambian gardens and agricultural areas, alongside the national coat of arms consisting of two lions holding an axe and hoe, a shield, a heraldic helmet, and an oil palm. The red-billed firefinch is notable for being the primary host of the village indigobird, a cuckoo-finch that mimics its songs and nesting habits.

The 20 Dalasis note sits in the middle of the denomination range and is one of the most commonly circulated notes in everyday Gambian transactions. The birds-themed design approach for Gambian currency was chosen to reflect the country's global reputation as one of Africa's top birdwatching destinations, supporting tourism and raising national pride in the country's extraordinary natural heritage.

highlights.design: Obverse: Red-billed firefinch and national coat of arms with two lions, shield, heraldic helmet, and oil palm. Reverse: Cultural heritage elements.
highlights.reason: Third denomination in Central Bank of The Gambia's 2019-2023 modernized currency series celebrating Gambian birdlife.
highlights.security: Holographic windowed security thread with demetalized bird imagery; pearlescent stripe with denomination; enhanced watermarks.

title: 50 Dalasis
description: Gambia 50 Dalasis purple polymer banknote (2023) featuring an osprey bird of prey and the Wassu stone circles archaeological site.
body:
The Central Bank of The Gambia released the 50 Dalasis banknote in 2023 as part of its 2019-2023 modernized currency series. This purple polymer note measures 125 mm x 65 mm and bears the signatures of Buah Saidy and Dr. Abodulie Sireh Jallow. The denomination represents the mid-to-higher value notes in the series and continues the birds-themed design approach.

The obverse side features an osprey (Pandion haliaetus) bird of prey perched on a tree branch and in flight, with two standing bush fowl birds as registration marks and the national coat of arms. The osprey is a widespread raptor found on Gambian rivers and coastline, known for its dramatic plunge-diving fishing technique. The reverse depicts the Wassu stone circles, an important archaeological site dating from the 8th century AD located in the North Bank Region of Gambia.

The Wassu stone circles are part of the Senegambian Stone Circles UNESCO World Heritage Site, comprising over 1,000 monuments consisting of stone circles, burial mounds, and tumuli spread across Senegal and Gambia. These circles were constructed between the 8th and 16th centuries AD by the ancestors of present-day inhabitants, representing one of the largest concentrations of megalithic sites on Earth and a significant marker of the region's pre-colonial civilizations.

highlights.design: Obverse: Osprey bird of prey in flight and perched, standing bush fowls, national coat of arms. Reverse: 8th-century Wassu stone circles (UNESCO World Heritage Site).
highlights.reason: Mid-to-higher denomination in Central Bank of The Gambia's 2019-2023 series highlighting raptor biodiversity and archaeological heritage.
highlights.security: Holographic green-to-red windowed security thread with demetalized bird and denomination 50; pearlescent stripe; crocodile head watermark with electrotype 50.

title: 100 Dalasis
description: Gambia 100 Dalasis teal polymer banknote (2023) featuring black-crowned cranes and traditional river fishing imagery.
body:
The Central Bank of The Gambia issued the 100 Dalasis banknote in 2023 as one of the highest denominations in its modernized 2019-2023 currency series. This teal polymer note measures 135 mm x 74 mm and was printed by De La Rue with signatures from Buah Saidy and Dr. Abodulie Sireh Jallow. The denomination represents significant purchasing power in Gambian currency circulation.

The obverse features a black-crowned crane (Balearica pavonina) standing and in flight, with two standing bush fowl birds as registration marks and the national coat of arms. The black-crowned crane is a striking and culturally significant bird in West Africa, known for its golden crown and elaborate courtship dances. The reverse depicts three men in a traditional dugout canoe casting a fishing net on the River Gambia, illustrating the traditional fishing practices that have sustained Gambian communities for centuries.

The River Gambia fishery is central to the nation's food security and economy, with both artisanal and commercial fishing operations employing a significant portion of the population. The black-crowned crane has historical significance in Gambian culture and is listed as vulnerable due to habitat loss across its West African range. Its prominent placement on the 100 Dalasis note reflects conservation awareness alongside cultural celebration.

highlights.design: Obverse: Black-crowned crane standing and in flight, standing bush fowls, national coat of arms. Reverse: Three men in canoe casting fishing net on the River Gambia.
highlights.reason: High-value denomination in Central Bank of The Gambia's 2019-2023 series emphasizing wildlife conservation and traditional fishing heritage.
highlights.security: Holographic green-to-red windowed security thread with demetalized bird and denomination 100; pearlescent stripe with denomination; two bush fowls as registration device.

title: 200 Dalasis
description: Gambia 200 Dalasis orange polymer banknote (2023) — highest denomination — featuring bush fowl birds and rice harvesting.
body:
The Central Bank of The Gambia released the 200 Dalasis banknote in 2023 as the highest denomination in its modernized 2019-2023 currency series. This orange polymer note measures 139 mm x 77 mm and was printed by De La Rue, bearing the signatures of Jallow and Saidy (signature 19). The denomination represents the largest circulating value in current Gambian currency.

The obverse features two standing double-spurred spurfowl (bush fowl) birds, a gold-to-green SPARK Orbital optically variable ink patch shaped like a bush fowl, two standing bush fowls as registration devices, and the national coat of arms. The bush fowl serves as the visual anchor of the entire denomination series, appearing as registration marks on all notes and taking center stage on the highest value. The reverse depicts two workers harvesting rice by hand, representing Gambia's agricultural economy and rural traditions.

Rice farming is central to Gambian food culture, with rice being the country's staple food and a primary agricultural activity particularly among women farmers in the rural regions. The SPARK Orbital OVI on the 200 Dalasis note is one of the most advanced security features in the series, creating a shimmering gold-to-green orbital effect when the note is tilted, providing both visual appeal and anti-counterfeiting protection for this highest-value denomination.

highlights.design: Obverse: Double-spurred spurfowl (bush fowl) birds, gold-to-green SPARK Orbital OVI patch, national coat of arms. Reverse: Two workers harvesting rice by hand.
highlights.reason: Highest denomination in Central Bank of The Gambia's 2019-2023 series celebrating agricultural traditions and Gambian avifauna.
highlights.security: Holographic green-to-red windowed security thread with demetalized bird and denomination 200; gold-to-green SPARK Orbital OVI; pearlescent stripe with denomination.

title: 5 Pounds
description: Great Britain 5 Pounds polymer banknote (2023) featuring King Charles III and Winston Churchill — first UK notes with King Charles III portrait.
body:
The Bank of England issued the 5 Pounds polymer banknote dated 2023, marking the introduction of King Charles III's portrait on UK circulation currency following the death of Queen Elizabeth II in September 2022. This blue-turquoise note measures 125 mm x 65 mm and bears the signature of Andrew Bailey, Governor of the Bank of England. Although the copyright date on the note reads 2023, banknotes bearing King Charles III's portrait were officially first issued into circulation on June 5, 2024, with notes remaining in co-circulation with Queen Elizabeth II designs.

The obverse features King Charles III's portrait (based on a photograph taken by Chris Levine) alongside the Bank of England's Threadneedle Street headquarters and the Elizabeth Tower (Big Ben) over a transparent window. The reverse continues to display Sir Winston Churchill, the former Prime Minister who led Britain through the Second World War, along with the Houses of Parliament and the Elizabeth Tower. Churchill has appeared on the 5 Pounds note since 2016.

The introduction of King Charles III on UK banknotes represents a significant historical transition, the first change of monarch on British currency since the 1950s. The Bank of England announced that existing Queen Elizabeth II notes would remain legal tender indefinitely, allowing for a gradual natural replacement without disrupting circulation. The polymer substrate, introduced for the 5 Pound note in 2016, provides durability approximately 2.5 times greater than paper notes.

highlights.design: Obverse: King Charles III portrait, Bank of England headquarters, Elizabeth Tower transparent window. Reverse: Winston Churchill, Houses of Parliament, Elizabeth Tower.
highlights.reason: First UK banknote series featuring King Charles III following the death of Queen Elizabeth II; co-circulates with previous Queen Elizabeth II design.
highlights.security: Holographic stripe with alternating Five/Pounds text when tilted; metallic window image (gold obverse, silver reverse); round green foil BLENHEIM feature; polymer substrate.

title: 10 Pounds
description: Great Britain 10 Pounds polymer banknote (2023) featuring King Charles III and Jane Austen — part of the new royal series.
body:
The Bank of England released the 10 Pounds polymer banknote dated 2023, introducing King Charles III's portrait in the continuation of the established Jane Austen design. This note measures 132 mm x 69 mm and features King Charles III on the obverse, with the 2023 design copyright date reflecting production updates. Like the 5 Pounds note, banknotes with King Charles III's portrait officially began circulation on June 5, 2024, operating in parallel with existing Queen Elizabeth II designs.

The reverse displays Jane Austen, the renowned English novelist celebrated for her wit and social commentary, alongside illustrations from her most famous work. The design includes a quotation from Pride and Prejudice and imagery evoking the Regency era depicted in her novels. Jane Austen has appeared on the 10 Pounds note since 2017, replacing Charles Darwin. Security features include a distinctive book-shaped copper foil patch containing the letters JA, color-shifting text that alternates between Ten and Pounds when tilted, and a coloured quill that changes from purple to orange when tilted.

Jane Austen (1775-1817) is one of England's most beloved literary figures, whose six major novels — including Pride and Prejudice, Sense and Sensibility, and Emma — continue to attract global readership and cultural adaptations. Her placement on the 10 Pounds note was chosen through public consultation and reflects her enduring significance to English language and culture. Two clusters of raised dots in the top left corner provide accessibility for blind and partially sighted users.
