highlights.design: Obverse: King Charles III, Bank of England architecture. Reverse: Jane Austen, literary scenes from Pride and Prejudice, Regency-era imagery and quotations.
highlights.reason: First 10 Pounds note featuring King Charles III; Jane Austen reverse design continues from 2017, emphasizing English literary heritage.
highlights.security: Book-shaped JA copper foil patch; tilting Ten/Pounds color-shifting text; quill changing purple to orange; metallized micro-text; two accessibility dot clusters for visually impaired users.

title: 20 Pounds
description: Great Britain 20 Pounds polymer banknote (2023) featuring King Charles III and J.M.W. Turner's The Fighting Temeraire.
body:
The Bank of England issued the 20 Pounds polymer banknote dated 2023, continuing the Turner series with the introduction of King Charles III's portrait. Banknotes bearing King Charles III's portrait officially entered circulation on June 5, 2024, co-circulating with existing Queen Elizabeth II designs. The obverse features King Charles III's portrait alongside the denomination and the Bank of England name. The reverse celebrates the landscape artist J.M.W. Turner and features his famous work The Fighting Temeraire, along with Turner's quote Light is therefore colour from his 1818 Royal Academy lecture.

Joseph Mallord William Turner (1775-1851) is widely regarded as one of England's greatest painters, celebrated for his expressive colorization and imaginative landscapes. The Fighting Temeraire (1839), depicting the warship that served at the Battle of Trafalgar being towed to its final berth, was voted the greatest painting in Britain in a public poll. Turner's placement on the 20 Pounds note since 2020 reflects the Bank of England's commitment to celebrating British cultural heritage through its currency.

The polymer substrate, introduced for the 20 Pounds note in 2020, offers durability approximately 2.5 times greater than paper notes and incorporates significantly more advanced security features. The note measures 139 mm x 73 mm and is recognized as a significant milestone in British cultural currency design, honoring both the new monarch and one of Britain's greatest artistic legacies.

highlights.design: Obverse: King Charles III portrait. Reverse: J.M.W. Turner's The Fighting Temeraire with Turner's quote 'Light is therefore colour'.
highlights.reason: First 20 Pounds note featuring King Charles III; Turner design continues from 2020, celebrating Britain's artistic heritage.
highlights.security: Holographic stripe alternating Twenty/Pounds when tilted; see-through window with metallic foil (gold obverse, silver reverse); color-shifting security thread; polymer substrate.

title: 5 Pounds
description: Guernsey 5 Pounds banknote (January 2023) featuring Queen Elizabeth II with new PUREIMAGE holographic security thread.
body:
The States of Guernsey introduced an updated 5 Pounds banknote on January 19, 2023, featuring enhanced security measures to combat counterfeiting. The note displays Queen Elizabeth II on the obverse, along with traditional Guernsey design elements including the Euphrosyne Kendal lifeboat, Guernsey lily, and Town Church in Saint Peter Port. The banknote is printed in pink, blue, green, brown, red, and yellow colors and features the seal of the Bailiwick of Guernsey flanked by olive branches.

This update was produced by De La Rue in conjunction with the States of Guernsey as part of a comprehensive modernization of all Guernsey pound denominations. The primary security advancement is the introduction of the PUREIMAGE holographic windowed thread, which displays three demetalized leopards and the denomination 5 that shift dynamically from dark to light when the note is tilted in any direction, making counterfeiting significantly more difficult.

Guernsey is a British Crown dependency in the English Channel with its own currency, the Guernsey pound, which circulates at parity with the British pound sterling. The Guernsey lily (Nerine sarniensis) depicted on the note has been associated with the island since the 17th century and is one of Guernsey's most recognized cultural symbols. The note was produced as part of the same series that launched earlier denominations featuring the Queen's portrait, which continued in use following her death in September 2022.

highlights.design: Obverse: Queen Elizabeth II portrait. Design elements: Euphrosyne Kendal lifeboat, Guernsey lily, Town Church in Saint Peter Port, Bailiwick seal with olive branches.
highlights.reason: Enhanced security update while maintaining established design continuity; all Guernsey denominations updated simultaneously on January 19, 2023.
highlights.security: PUREIMAGE holographic windowed security thread with three demetalized leopards and 5 denomination shifting dark to light when tilted.

title: 10 Pounds
description: Guernsey 10 Pounds banknote (January 2023) featuring Queen Elizabeth II with new IGNITE holographic security thread.
body:
The States of Guernsey introduced an updated 10 Pounds banknote on January 19, 2023 as part of the comprehensive security modernization of all Guernsey pound denominations. The note features Queen Elizabeth II on the obverse with Guernsey's distinctive design elements, maintaining visual continuity with earlier issues while incorporating significantly advanced security technology produced by De La Rue.

The primary security advancement for this denomination is the introduction of the IGNITE windowed security thread, which shifts dynamically from blue to green with a pulsing pattern that shows visible movement effects when the note is tilted in any direction. This represents a significant upgrade from previous security thread technology and provides an easily verifiable authentication feature for everyday users. The IGNITE thread technology is part of De La Rue's portfolio of advanced optical security features designed to stay ahead of counterfeiting methods.

Guernsey's 10 Pounds denomination plays an important role in the island's everyday commerce, and the security upgrade was timed to coincide with updates to the 5 and 20 Pounds denominations for a coherent modernization of the complete banknote family. The States of Guernsey's decision to maintain Queen Elizabeth II's portrait on the updated notes reflected established practice of issuing currency during production cycles rather than transitioning immediately to a new monarch's portrait.

highlights.design: Obverse: Queen Elizabeth II portrait with Guernsey cultural design elements maintaining established visual identity.
highlights.reason: Comprehensive security modernization across all Guernsey denominations; advanced thread technology to prevent counterfeiting.
highlights.security: IGNITE windowed security thread that shifts from blue to green with pulsing pattern showing dynamic movement when tilted in any direction.

title: 20 Pounds
description: Guernsey 20 Pounds banknote (January 2023) featuring Queen Elizabeth II with new NEXUS embedded holographic stripe.
body:
The States of Guernsey introduced an updated 20 Pounds banknote on January 19, 2023 with enhanced security features designed to combat counterfeiting. The note displays Queen Elizabeth II's portrait on the obverse and features traditional Guernsey design elements including the Guernsey lily, St. James Concert Hall in Saint Peter Port, and the seal of the Bailiwick flanked by olive branches. The banknote is printed in red, blue, green, brown, orange, and yellow, creating a distinctive multicolor appearance.

The primary security advancement for this highest-denomination note is the introduction of the NEXUS embedded stripe — an 18 mm wide embedded holographic element with integrated security effects visible on both the obverse and reverse. This represents one of the most sophisticated security features in the updated Guernsey series, appropriate for the highest-value denomination. The NEXUS technology provides security effects that are clearly visible to the general public while remaining extremely difficult to reproduce.

St. James Concert Hall, depicted on the reverse, is one of Guernsey's most beloved cultural venues, converted from a former Anglican church and hosting regular classical music performances and cultural events. Its inclusion on the 20 Pounds note alongside the Guernsey lily reflects the island's commitment to celebrating both its natural and cultural heritage on its highest denomination.

highlights.design: Obverse: Queen Elizabeth II portrait. Design elements: Guernsey lily, St. James Concert Hall in Saint Peter Port, Bailiwick seal with olive branches. Multicolor: red, blue, green, brown, orange, yellow.
highlights.reason: Enhanced security for highest denomination; NEXUS technology provides most advanced protection appropriate for the 20 Pounds note.
highlights.security: NEXUS embedded stripe 18 mm wide with integrated holographic effects visible on both obverse and reverse; advanced anti-counterfeiting consistent with highest denomination requirements.

title: 20 Pounds
description: Guernsey 20 Pounds banknote second variant (January 2023) featuring Queen Elizabeth II — distinct signature or print variant within the 2023 security update.
body:
This represents a second catalog variant of the Guernsey 20 Pounds banknote from the January 19, 2023 security update, distinguished from e209 by a different signature combination or printing specification. The States of Guernsey note retains the same Queen Elizabeth II portrait and established design elements including the Guernsey lily, St. James Concert Hall, and Bailiwick seal, while the variant designation reflects differences in signatories or minor printing specifications within the same denomination and security update program.

Guernsey banknotes are signed by the Treasurer of the States, and as Treasurers change over time, notes bearing different signature combinations are assigned separate catalog numbers even when the design and security features remain consistent. The 2023 update encompassed both existing and transitional signature combinations, resulting in multiple catalog variants for the same denominations. Both variants share the same NEXUS embedded stripe security advancement introduced across the denomination on January 19, 2023.

The Guernsey pound, issued by the States of Guernsey since 1921, circulates alongside the British pound sterling at parity. With a population of approximately 63,000, Guernsey issues relatively small quantities of banknotes compared to larger jurisdictions, making each variant of particular interest to collectors of Channel Islands currency.

highlights.design: Obverse: Queen Elizabeth II portrait with Guernsey lily, St. James Concert Hall, and Bailiwick seal — identical design to the primary 2023 variant (e209).
highlights.reason: Variant designation within the 2023 security update reflecting different Treasurer signature or printing specification.
highlights.security: NEXUS embedded stripe and updated security features consistent with the broader 2023 Guernsey 20 Pounds security modernization.

title: 50 Gourdes
description: Haiti 50 Gourdes banknote (2023) from the commemorative bicentennial independence series.
body:
The Banque de la République d'Haïti issued the 50 Gourdes banknote in 2023 as part of a commemorative series marking milestones in Haiti's independence heritage. Haiti declared independence from France on January 1, 1804, becoming the first Black republic and the first nation born from a successful slave revolt — a defining achievement in world history. The 50 Gourdes denomination carries imagery reflecting Haiti's revolutionary heritage and national identity.

The note features designs and imagery celebrating Haitian heroes and historical events associated with the independence struggle. Haiti's currency has historically honored the founding figures of the nation, including Toussaint Louverture, Jean-Jacques Dessalines, and other revolutionary leaders who fought for freedom. The 2023 issue reflects the Banque de la République d'Haïti's ongoing commitment to commemorating the nation's extraordinary history through its currency.

Haiti's 50 Gourdes denomination serves everyday transactions in one of the Western Hemisphere's most historically significant nations. Despite ongoing economic and political challenges, Haitian currency continues to reflect immense national pride and the profound legacy of the country's founding generation, whose struggle for freedom inspired independence movements across the Americas and the Caribbean.

highlights.design: Commemorative design celebrating Haiti's independence heritage with imagery of historical figures and revolutionary symbolism.
highlights.reason: Commemorative series honoring Haiti's independence history; the 50 Gourdes serves everyday circulation needs.
highlights.security: Standard Banque de la République d'Haïti security features including security thread and watermark.

title: 100 Gourdes
description: Haiti 100 Gourdes banknote (2023) featuring Henry Christophe and the Citadelle Laferrière fortress.
body:
The Banque de la République d'Haïti issued the 100 Gourdes banknote in 2023 featuring Henry Christophe (1767-1820), a pivotal military leader and statesman who played a crucial role in Haiti's independence struggle. Christophe served alongside Toussaint Louverture and Jean-Jacques Dessalines in the Haitian Revolution, later becoming President of the State of Haiti in the north before proclaiming himself King Henri I in 1811. His reign oversaw major cultural and infrastructure developments, most notably the construction of the Citadelle Laferrière.

The reverse depicts the Citadelle Laferrière (Henry Citadel) near Milot in northern Haiti, a massive military fortress built between 1805 and 1820 by Henri Christophe using forced labor. The Citadelle is the largest fortress in the Americas and is now a UNESCO World Heritage Site, representing one of the most extraordinary architectural achievements in the Caribbean. Built on a mountaintop at over 900 meters elevation, it was designed to defend Haiti against potential French re-invasion following independence.

The Citadelle Laferrière, together with the Sans-Souci Palace nearby, forms the National History Park of Haiti and remains a source of profound national pride. The combination of Christophe's portrait with the fortress he built creates a powerful narrative of Haitian resilience, architectural ambition, and the determination to preserve the hard-won independence of the world's first Black republic.

highlights.design: Obverse: Henry Christophe (1767-1820), revolutionary leader and King Henri I. Reverse: Citadelle Laferrière fortress near Milot (UNESCO World Heritage Site).
highlights.reason: Honors a major figure in Haiti's independence struggle and his monumental architectural legacy; part of the commemorative series.
highlights.security: Modern Banque de la République d'Haïti security features including security thread and watermark.

title: 250 Gourdes
description: Haiti 250 Gourdes banknote (2023) featuring Jean-Jacques Dessalines — Haiti's founding father and first head of state.
body:
The Banque de la République d'Haïti issued the 250 Gourdes banknote in 2023 featuring Jean-Jacques Dessalines (1758-1806), the revolutionary leader who proclaimed Haiti's independence from France on January 1, 1804, and served as the nation's first ruler. Dessalines, born into slavery, rose to become a military general who led the decisive campaigns of the Haitian Revolution, culminating in the Battle of Vertieres in November 1803 — the final major battle that secured Haitian independence. He declared himself Emperor Jacques I in 1805 before being assassinated in 1806.

The reverse depicts Fort Décidé near Marchand Dessalines (now Dessalines), representing Haiti's military heritage and the defensive fortifications that protected the nation's newfound independence. Fort Décidé was among the structures associated with Dessalines' efforts to consolidate Haitian sovereignty and defend against potential European re-colonization. The banknote is printed in blue on tan and brown underprint, creating a visually distinctive appearance for this highest-denomination commemorative note.

Jean-Jacques Dessalines is revered as the Father of the Haitian Nation, and his legacy remains central to Haitian national identity. His image on the 250 Gourdes note, the highest denomination in the commemorative series, reflects his foundational status as the man who translated the Haitian Revolution's military victory into the formal declaration of a sovereign nation, ending over 300 years of European colonial rule.

highlights.design: Obverse: Jean-Jacques Dessalines (1758-1806), Haiti's founding father and first ruler. Reverse: Fort Décidé near Marchand Dessalines. Blue on tan and brown underprint.
highlights.reason: Highest denomination in the commemorative series honoring Haiti's founding father and independence proclaimer.
highlights.security: Modern Banque de la République d'Haïti security features; distinctive color scheme differentiating the highest denomination.

issuer: Central Bank of the Islamic Republic of Iran
title: 1.000.000 Rials
description: Iran 1,000,000 Rials cheque (2023) featuring the Fatima Masumeh Shrine in Qom and the South Pars gas field.
body:
The Central Bank of the Islamic Republic of Iran issued the 1,000,000 Rials high-denomination cheque in 2023. This instrument features the sacred Fatima Masumeh Shrine in Qom on the obverse, one of the most important pilgrimage sites in Shi'a Islam, visited by millions of pilgrims annually. The shrine is the burial place of Fatimah bint Musa, sister of the eighth Imam Ali ibn Musa al-Ridha, and is considered the second holiest site in Iran after the Imam Reza shrine in Mashhad. The reverse depicts the South Pars / North Dome gas-condensate field, the world's largest natural gas field shared between Iran and Qatar, symbolizing Iran's immense energy wealth.

The note is predominantly blue with dimensions of 157 x 71 mm and is printed on cotton fiber paper. The 1,000,000 Rials is officially classified as a cheque rather than a traditional banknote, a distinction Iran uses for its highest-denomination instruments while they function identically to banknotes in circulation. The denomination is equivalent to 100 Toman in modern Iranian currency notation, reflecting the practical renaming of currency units that Iranians use in everyday transactions to simplify high-value amounts.

Iran's high-denomination currency instruments reflect the country's long-running inflation challenges, which have periodically required the issuance of ever-higher denominations for everyday transactions. The juxtaposition of a major Shi'a shrine with Iran's energy infrastructure on the same banknote reflects the dual pillars of the Islamic Republic — religious heritage and resource-based economic power.

highlights.design: Obverse: Fatima Masumeh Shrine in Qom — second holiest site in Iran. Reverse: South Pars / North Dome gas field symbolizing Iran's energy wealth. Predominantly blue, 157 x 71 mm.
highlights.reason: High-denomination cheque for significant transactions; celebrates Iran's religious heritage and natural resource wealth.
highlights.security: Segmented color-changing windowed security thread (2 mm) with metallic I.R.IRAN text; watermark of shrine entrance gate with electrotype 100; fluorescent serial numbers in Farsi and western numerals; microprinting.

issuer: Central Bank of the Islamic Republic of Iran
title: 2.000.000 Rials
description: Iran 2,000,000 Rials cheque (2023) featuring the Karoun 3 hydroelectric dam and Persian calligraphy.
body:
The Central Bank of the Islamic Republic of Iran issued the 2,000,000 Rials high-denomination cheque in 2023. The obverse prominently displays the Karoun 3 hydroelectric dam, a 205-meter-high arch dam located on the Karun River in Khuzestan province — the tallest dam in Iran and one of the most significant engineering achievements in the country's modern history. The dam, completed in 2005, generates approximately 2,000 megawatts of electricity and represents Iran's commitment to renewable energy infrastructure and technical self-sufficiency.

The reverse features elaborate Persian calligraphy surrounded by traditional Islamic geometric patterns, reflecting Iran's extraordinary heritage in the art of calligraphy and decorative geometric design that has flourished for over a millennium. The banknote is printed in light blue, green, and yellow colors and measures 160 x 75 mm. Equivalent to 200 Toman in modern Iranian currency notation, this high-denomination cheque is produced by TAKAB (Iran's banknote printing company) for significant financial transactions.

The watermark features Ferdowsi (Hakim Abu'l-Qasim Firdawsi Tusi, 940-1020 AD), the celebrated Persian poet and author of the Shahnameh (Book of Kings), the epic poem of approximately 50,000 couplets that forms the foundation of Persian language and literature. Ferdowsi's presence as a watermark on this highest-denomination instrument symbolizes the profound cultural significance of Persian literary heritage to Iranian national identity alongside the modern infrastructure depicted in the primary design.

highlights.design: Obverse: Karoun 3 hydroelectric dam in Khuzestan province (205 meters, tallest in Iran). Reverse: Persian calligraphy with Islamic geometric patterns. Light blue, green, and yellow, 160 x 75 mm.
highlights.reason: Celebrates Iran's modern engineering achievements and classical literary heritage on the highest denomination cheque.
highlights.security: Windowed security thread with demetalized 2,000,000 denomination numerals; solid security thread with Farsi script; Ferdowsi watermark; multiple anti-counterfeiting elements.

title: 5.000 Dinars
description: Iraq 5,000 Dinars banknote (2023) featuring the Gali Ali Beg waterfall in Kurdistan and the Al-Ukether desert fortress.
body:
The Central Bank of Iraq issued the 5,000 dinar banknote in 2023 (AH1445) as part of its ongoing modernized circulation series. This purple, blue, and orange note measures 159 mm x 78 mm and bears the signature of Mustafa Ghaleb Mokhif. The obverse side showcases a green-to-blue SPARK feature alongside the iconic Gali Ali Beg waterfall in Kurdistan, which cascades dramatically through a narrow limestone gorge in the Bradost Mountains of Erbil Governorate. The reverse depicts the desert fortress of Al-Ukether, an important Abbasid-era historical structure in Iraq's Wasit Governorate.

Gali Ali Beg is one of Kurdistan's most celebrated natural landmarks, with the waterfall plunging approximately 13 meters into a turquoise pool surrounded by lush mountain vegetation. The inclusion of this Kurdish landscape on the national currency reflects Iraq's recognition of the region's natural heritage as part of the country's shared identity. Al-Ukether fortress, dating from the early Islamic period, represents Iraq's layered historical legacy from ancient Mesopotamia through the Islamic caliphate era.

The note incorporates modern security features designed to prevent counterfeiting while maintaining accessibility for visually impaired users. Tactile marks allow blind users to identify the denomination by touch, reflecting the Central Bank's commitment to inclusive currency design. The SPARK security element provides a dynamic color-shifting effect visible to the naked eye, enabling rapid authentication in everyday transactions.

highlights.design: Obverse: Gali Ali Beg waterfall in Kurdistan with green-to-blue SPARK element. Reverse: Al-Ukether desert fortress (Abbasid period).
highlights.reason: Standard circulation denomination in the Central Bank of Iraq's updated banknote series reflecting the nation's geographic and cultural diversity.
highlights.security: Green-to-blue RollingStar Cube windowed security thread; tactile marks for visually impaired users; horse head watermark.

title: 50.000 Dinars
description: Iraq 50,000 Dinars banknote (2023) — highest denomination — featuring Mesopotamian heritage with an Assyrian jar, water wheel, and marshland scenes.
body:
The Central Bank of Iraq released the 50,000 dinar banknote in 2023 (AH1445), representing the highest denomination for significant transactions. Measuring 156 mm x 65 mm, this brown, green, yellow, and blue note features the signature of Ali Mohsen Ismail Al-Alaq. The obverse presents an Assyrian jar with a blue-to-green SPARK element, a traditional water wheel (nooria) on the Euphrates River, and the Gali Ali Beg waterfall, connecting Iraq's modern currency to its ancient Mesopotamian civilization that stretches back over 5,000 years.

The reverse depicts fishermen poling traditional mashoof canoes through the southern marshes, water buffaloes wading in the water, and the distinctive reed houses (mudhif) of the Marsh Arabs, representing the traditional life of the Mesopotamian marshlands. The Iraqi Marshes, located at the confluence of the Tigris and Euphrates rivers, were recognized as a UNESCO World Heritage Site in 2016 for their cultural and ecological significance. This ancient ecosystem, once nearly destroyed by drainage projects, has been partially restored and remains home to communities maintaining traditions unchanged for millennia.

The banknote is printed on hybrid substrate, blending traditional artistic elements with contemporary security technology. The Assyrian jar depicted on the obverse draws from Iraq's pre-Islamic heritage and archaeological richness, symbolizing cultural continuity from ancient Mesopotamia through to contemporary Iraq.

highlights.design: Obverse: Assyrian jar with SPARK element, traditional Euphrates water wheel, Gali Ali Beg waterfall. Reverse: Fishermen in mashoof canoes, water buffaloes, reed houses in Mesopotamian marshlands.
highlights.reason: Highest-denomination circulation note emphasizing Iraq's ancient Mesopotamian heritage and the living traditions of the Iraqi Marshes.
highlights.security: Purple-to-green RollingStar Cube windowed security thread; Assyrian palm tree registration device; horse head watermark.

title: 10.000 Tenge
description: Kazakhstan 10,000 Tenge commemorative banknote (November 2023) celebrating the 30th anniversary of the Kazakhstani tenge currency.
body:
The National Bank of Kazakhstan issued a limited edition commemorative 10,000 tenge banknote on November 15, 2023, honoring the 30th anniversary of the introduction of the national currency. A total of 3,000,000 pieces were produced. This purple and blue note measures 149 mm x 79 mm and bears the signature of Timur Suleimenov. The obverse features the national emblem, a Mobius strip symbolizing continuity, and a map of Kazakhstan with regions designed in traditional Saka cultural style — referencing the ancient nomadic civilization that inhabited the Kazakh steppe from the 7th to 3rd century BCE. The reverse showcases an outline of the Eurasian continent, a commemorative tenge coin, and another Mobius strip element.

The tenge was introduced on November 15, 1993, replacing the Soviet ruble following Kazakhstan's independence, marking a crucial milestone in the nation's economic sovereignty. The commemorative banknote is legal tender that must be accepted at face value throughout Kazakhstan. It incorporates 19 advanced security features, including a prominent coin displaying the golden three-dimensional State Emblem and optically changeable tenge symbols.

The varifeye ColourChange window feature provides a holographic element visible at the center of the transparent area, while the SPARK Flow PRIME element transitions from magenta to green creating a dynamic movement effect. The Mobius strip imagery throughout the design symbolizes Kazakhstan's continuous development and the enduring strength of its currency over three decades of independence.

highlights.design: Obverse: National emblem, Mobius strip, traditional Saka-style map of Kazakhstan. Reverse: Eurasian continent outline, commemorative tenge coin, Mobius strip.
highlights.reason: Limited edition commemorative issue (3,000,000 pieces) celebrating 30 years of the Kazakhstani tenge introduced November 15, 1993.
highlights.security: 19 advanced security features including varifeye ColourChange window, SPARK Flow PRIME element transitioning magenta to green, windowed security thread with demetalized tenge symbol, tactile relief elements for visually impaired.

title: 5.000 Tenge
description: Kazakhstan 5,000 Tenge banknote (December 2023) — inaugural denomination of the new Saka Style series celebrating ancient nomadic heritage.
body:
The National Bank of Kazakhstan introduced the 5,000 tenge banknote on December 25, 2023, as the inaugural denomination in its new Saka Style series. This red and purple note measures 140 mm x 70 mm and represents a paradigm shift in Kazakhstan's currency design philosophy, drawing inspiration from the ancient Saka civilization that flourished on the Kazakh steppe from the 6th to 3rd century BCE. The obverse features a golden eagle detail and ornament from the Taldy II Kurgan burial mound, the Tree of Life sacred branch with hovering bird from 3rd-4th century BCE headgear, the famous Golden Man from the Issyk kurgan, a DNA spiral symbolizing continuity, and the national emblem. The reverse showcases Charin Canyon outlined in an infinity ornament, the golden eagle from the Berel Mounds, and circular Kazakh symbols.

This is the first banknote globally to feature Giesecke+Devrient's RollingStar Patch technology, which provides a gold-to-jade color shift with radiating solar rays creating a striking visual authentication feature. The Golden Man (Altyn Adam), discovered in 1969 at the Issyk Kurgan near Almaty, is one of Kazakhstan's most iconic archaeological treasures — a Saka warrior buried in a golden suit of approximately 4,000 gold pieces, dating from the 4th-3rd century BCE.

The design connects Kazakhstan's modern nation-building with its ancient nomadic heritage, celebrating the artistic and cultural sophistication of the Saka people. The RAPID Vision Micro-Optic Thread introduces dynamic ripple effects in red and blue, creating a sophisticated security feature that also references the flowing patterns characteristic of Saka artistic tradition.

highlights.design: Obverse: Golden eagle from Taldy II Kurgan, Tree of Life, Golden Man from Issyk kurgan, DNA spiral, national emblem. Reverse: Charin Canyon in infinity ornament, Berel golden eagle, circular Kazakh symbols.
highlights.reason: Inaugural denomination of the new Saka Style series honoring Kazakhstan's ancient nomadic civilization; world's first banknote with RollingStar Patch technology.
highlights.security: RollingStar Patch with gold-to-jade color shift and radiating solar rays; RAPID Vision Micro-Optic Thread with dynamic red and blue ripple effects; windowed security thread with demetalized tenge symbol; Samruk bird watermark.

issuer: National Bank of the Kyrgyz Republic
title: 20 Som
description: Kyrgyzstan 20 Som banknote (2023) featuring poet and educator Togolok Moldo and the ancient Silk Road caravansarai Tash-Rabat.
body:
The National Bank of the Kyrgyz Republic released the 20 Som banknote in 2023, with circulation beginning on February 15, 2024, as part of the second phase of the national currency modernization program. This red banknote measures 120 mm x 58 mm. The obverse features a portrait of Togolok Moldo (1860-1942), a celebrated Kyrgyz writer, poet, and folk philosopher who played a crucial role in preserving and transmitting Kyrgyz oral literary traditions. His contributions helped maintain the continuity of Kyrgyz cultural identity during a period of major social transformation.

The reverse showcases the Tash-Rabat caravansarai, a remarkably well-preserved 15th-century stone structure located in the At-Bashy district at an altitude of 3,200 meters, serving as a staging post on a branch of the ancient Silk Road that connected Central Asia with China. The caravansarai provided shelter for traders, pilgrims, and travelers traversing the high mountain passes, and its survival reflects the durability of the medieval architecture of the Karakhanid or Timurid period.

The 20 Som is the lowest denomination in the second phase of modernization and incorporates Crane Currency's RAPID HD Detect technology, which provides dynamic visual security effects. The Bioguard varnish with anti-bacterial and anti-viral properties adds a public health dimension to the note's specifications, a feature particularly valued in the post-pandemic context.

highlights.design: Obverse: Togolok Moldo (1860-1942), Kyrgyz poet and educator. Reverse: Tash-Rabat caravansarai (15th century) on the Great Silk Road, At-Bashy district.
highlights.reason: Lowest denomination in second phase of Kyrgyzstan's fifth banknote series modernization, honoring literary heritage and Silk Road history.
highlights.security: RAPID HD Detect security features (Crane Currency); local multi-tonal watermark with portrait and numbers; micro-optic security thread with denomination and National Bank diamond logo; Bioguard anti-bacterial and anti-viral varnish.

issuer: National Bank of the Kyrgyz Republic
title: 50 Som
description: Kyrgyzstan 50 Som banknote (2023) featuring stateswoman Kurmanjan Datka and the Uzgen architectural complex.
body:
The National Bank of the Kyrgyz Republic issued the 50 Som banknote in 2023, entering circulation on February 15, 2024, as part of the second phase of the national currency modernization initiative. This orange note measures 126 mm x 61 mm. The obverse displays a portrait of Kurmanjan Datka (1811-1907), widely known as the Alai Tsaritsa (Queen of the Alai), an outstanding stateswoman and political leader who navigated the complex transition of Kyrgyz tribal society during the Russian Empire's expansion into Central Asia in the 19th century. Her diplomatic acumen and leadership earned her the title of Datka — the highest administrative rank in the Khanate system — from multiple rulers.

The reverse features the Uzgen architectural complex, one of the most important historical monuments along the Great Silk Road, comprising an 11th-12th century minaret and three mausoleums of the Karakhanid dynasty. Located in Osh Region, the complex represents the golden age of Islamic architecture in Central Asia and demonstrates the sophisticated artistic traditions of the Karakhanid rulers who governed much of the region between the 10th and 13th centuries.

Kurmanjan Datka's placement on the 50 Som note reflects Kyrgyzstan's recognition of female historical figures in its national narrative. Her story of negotiating Kyrgyz survival and cultural preservation under Russian colonial pressure has made her an enduring symbol of resilience and wisdom in Kyrgyz national consciousness.

highlights.design: Obverse: Kurmanjan Datka (1811-1907), Alai Tsaritsa and stateswoman. Reverse: Uzgen architectural complex — 11th-12th century Karakhanid minaret and mausoleums.
highlights.reason: Second phase denomination honoring Kyrgyzstan's most celebrated female historical figure and Silk Road architectural heritage.
highlights.security: RAPID HD Detect security features; local multi-tonal watermark with portrait and numbers; micro-optic security thread with denomination and National Bank logo; Bioguard anti-bacterial, anti-fungal, and anti-viral varnish.

issuer: National Bank of the Kyrgyz Republic
title: 100 Som
description: Kyrgyzstan 100 Som banknote (2023) featuring akyn Toktogul Satylganov and the Kürpsay hydroelectric dam.
body:
The National Bank of the Kyrgyz Republic released the 100 Som banknote in 2023, with official circulation beginning on February 15, 2024, as part of the second phase of the national banknote modernization series. This blue note measures 132 mm x 63 mm. The obverse features a portrait of Toktogul Satylganov (1864-1933), the most celebrated of the Kyrgyz akyns (traditional bard-storytellers), whose improvised epic poetry preserved and enriched Kyrgyz oral literary traditions during a period when written Kyrgyz literature was only beginning to develop. He composed and performed thousands of verses at festivals and gatherings across the Tian Shan mountains.

The reverse displays the Kürpsay hydroelectric dam surrounded by the dramatic Naryn River gorge and mountain landscape, representing Kyrgyzstan's modern infrastructure development. The Kürpsay Dam, completed in 1981, is part of the Naryn-Syr Darya cascade of hydroelectric power plants on the Naryn River and has a capacity of 800 megawatts — a significant contribution to Kyrgyzstan's electricity generation, which relies almost entirely on hydropower.

Toktogul Satylganov is so revered in Kyrgyz culture that the country's largest reservoir and the city of Toktogul are named after him. His portrait on the 100 Som note, paired with modern hydropower infrastructure, creates a visual dialogue between Kyrgyzstan's ancient bardic traditions and its contemporary development ambitions.

highlights.design: Obverse: Toktogul Satylganov (1864-1933), celebrated Kyrgyz akyn and bard. Reverse: Kürpsay hydroelectric dam on the Naryn River with mountain landscape.
highlights.reason: Second phase denomination celebrating Kyrgyzstan's oral literary heritage and modern energy infrastructure.
highlights.security: RAPID HD Detect security features with dynamic visual effects; local multi-tonal watermark with portrait and numbers; micro-optic security thread with denomination and National Bank diamond logo; machine-readable features.

issuer: National Bank of the Kyrgyz Republic
title: 200 Som
description: Kyrgyzstan 200 Som banknote (2023) featuring writer Alykul Osmonov and the Issyk Kul lake — first phase of the modernization series.
body:
The National Bank of the Kyrgyz Republic released the 200 Som banknote in 2023, with circulation beginning on May 10, 2023, coinciding with the 30th anniversary of the Kyrgyzstani Som. This yellow note measures 138 mm x 66 mm and represents the first phase of modernization bringing updated designs and enhanced security features to the banknote series. The obverse displays a portrait of Alykul Osmonov (1915-1950), a poet recognized as the founder of modern Kyrgyz written poetry, who died young at age 35 but left an extraordinary literary legacy that bridges classical oral traditions with 20th century written forms.

The reverse showcases the stunning Issyk Kul lake, one of the world's largest alpine lakes by volume at approximately 1,730 cubic kilometers of water, located in northeastern Kyrgyzstan at an altitude of 1,607 meters. Despite its high altitude, the lake never freezes due to its depth of over 600 meters and slight salinity — a characteristic that gave it the Kyrgyz name meaning warm lake. The reverse also features an excerpt from Osmonov's writing, creating an artistic connection between the landscape and its poetic interpretation.

Osmonov's work transformed Kyrgyz poetry by introducing individual lyric expression within a literary culture previously dominated by collective epic forms. The combination of his portrait with Issyk Kul's timeless landscape on the 200 Som note reflects Kyrgyzstan's pride in both its literary achievements and its extraordinary natural geography.

highlights.design: Obverse: Alykul Osmonov (1915-1950), founder of modern Kyrgyz written poetry. Reverse: Issyk Kul alpine lake with excerpt from Osmonov's poetry.
highlights.reason: First phase of Kyrgyzstan's fifth banknote series modernization, issued May 10, 2023 on the 30th anniversary of the Som.
highlights.security: RAPID HD Detect security features with dynamic visual effects when tilted; local multi-tonal watermark with portrait and numbers; micro-optic security thread with denomination and National Bank logo.

issuer: National Bank of the Kyrgyz Republic
title: 500 Som
description: Kyrgyzstan 500 Som banknote (2023) featuring storyteller Sayakbay Karalaev and the Manas Ordo mausoleum.
body:
The National Bank of the Kyrgyz Republic issued the 500 Som banknote in 2023 as part of the first phase of banknote modernization, beginning circulation on May 10, 2023. This purple-violet note measures 144 mm x 68 mm. The obverse features a portrait of Sayakbay Karalaev (1894-1971), the most renowned manaschi (narrator of the Manas epic) in Kyrgyz history, who could recite the complete Manas epic — one of the world's longest oral epics at over 500,000 lines — from memory. Karalaev was recognized by UNESCO and is considered the greatest keeper of the Kyrgyz oral tradition.

The reverse displays the Manas Ordo mausoleum complex near Talas, associated with the legendary hero Manas who is the central figure of the Kyrgyz national epic. The Manas epic traces the story of a Kyrgyz warrior who united his people against invaders, and has been passed down through oral tradition for over a thousand years. The Manas Ordo complex is a site of profound cultural and spiritual significance, with the 14th-century Gumbez Manas mausoleum as its centerpiece.

The 500 Som note, with its focus on the oral epic tradition, reflects the central importance of the Manas cycle to Kyrgyz national identity. In 1995, Kyrgyzstan celebrated the 1,000th anniversary of the Manas epic as a defining moment of post-Soviet national renaissance, placing the epic at the heart of the country's cultural self-understanding.

highlights.design: Obverse: Sayakbay Karalaev (1894-1971), greatest manaschi (Manas epic narrator). Reverse: Manas Ordo mausoleum complex near Talas.
highlights.reason: First phase modernization denomination celebrating Kyrgyzstan's UNESCO-recognized Manas epic oral tradition.
highlights.security: RAPID HD Detect security features with dynamic background movement and pulsating National Bank logo when tilted; local multi-tonal watermark with portrait and numbers; micro-optic security thread with denomination and National Bank diamond logo.

issuer: National Bank of the Kyrgyz Republic
title: 1.000 Som
description: Kyrgyzstan 1,000 Som banknote (2023) — highest first-phase denomination — featuring medieval philosopher Yusuf Balasaguni and Takhti Sulaiman.
body:
The National Bank of the Kyrgyz Republic released the 1,000 Som banknote in 2023 as the highest denomination of the first phase of banknote modernization, entering circulation on May 10, 2023. This gray-green note measures 150 mm x 71 mm. The obverse displays a portrait of Yusuf Balasaguni (1020-1070 AD), the medieval Karakhanid poet and philosopher born in Balasagun (near present-day Tokmok), whose masterwork Kutadgu Bilig (Wisdom that Brings Happiness) is one of the oldest surviving monuments of Turkic literature. Written in 1069-1070 and dedicated to the Karakhanid ruler, the work blends Islamic philosophical thought with pre-Islamic Turkic traditions.

The reverse showcases the Takhti Sulaiman monument, also known as the Throne of Solomon, an ancient sacred site in the Sulaiman Mountains in Osh that has been a place of worship for thousands of years and was designated a UNESCO World Heritage Site in 2009. The mountain contains numerous petroglyphs, shrines, and archaeological remains spanning from the Paleolithic era through the 16th century, when the Timurid ruler Babur constructed a mosque on its summit. Pilgrims from across Central Asia visit the site seeking healing and spiritual blessing.

The pairing of Yusuf Balasaguni with Takhti Sulaiman creates a profound cultural statement about Kyrgyzstan's medieval intellectual heritage and sacred geography. The 1,000 Som note's gray-green color scheme conveys the gravitas appropriate for the highest denomination in the first modernization phase.

highlights.design: Obverse: Yusuf Balasaguni (1020-1070 AD), Karakhanid poet and philosopher. Reverse: Takhti Sulaiman (UNESCO World Heritage Site) in Osh — sacred mountain with ancient petroglyphs and Timurid mosque.
highlights.reason: Highest denomination in first phase of Kyrgyzstan's fifth banknote series modernization, celebrating medieval intellectual and sacred heritage.
highlights.security: RAPID HD Detect security features with rapid background movement and pulsating National Bank logo when tilted; local multi-tonal watermark with portrait and numbers; micro-optic security thread with denomination and National Bank logo; machine-readable features.

issuer: National Bank of the Kyrgyz Republic
title: 5.000 Som
description: Green banknote featuring portrait of renowned actor Suymenkul Chokmorov and the historic Ala-Too Theater on the reverse.
body: The 5,000 Som banknote was introduced as part of Kyrgyzstan's fifth currency series modernization and entered circulation on May 10, 2024. The obverse features Suymenkul Chokmorov (1939-1992), a celebrated Kyrgyz actor and artist, with the inscription on the obverse. The reverse depicts the iconic Ala-Too Theater, a symbol of Kyrgyz cultural heritage. Measuring 156 x 73 mm on white paper, this denomination represents the highest value note in the new series.

highlights.design: Color-shifting ink ornament on left side of portrait shifts from gold to jade when tilted, combined with the Theater imagery on reverse.
highlights.reason: Part of the fifth series modernization to enhance currency security and replace older denominations in circulation.
highlights.security: Windowed Rapid HD Detect security thread with 5000, SPARK Live colour-changing vignette (gold to green), iridescent ink stripe on reverse, and latent image of Som symbol.

issuer: Banco da China and Banco Nacional Ultramarino
title: 10 Patacas
description: Red, orange, and green commemorative banknote celebrating the Year of the Rabbit with stylized rabbit imagery and traditional Macanese architecture.
body: The 10 Patacas 2023 Year of the Rabbit commemorative is a special-issue banknote issued by Banco da China to celebrate the Chinese zodiac animal for 2023. The obverse features a stylized red rabbit, Chinese lantern, and SPARK 10 design elements. The reverse displays the iconic Banco da China building in Macau, the historic A-Ma Temple, and additional rabbit imagery representing lunar prosperity. Measuring 138 x 69 mm, this note is part of a comprehensive zodiac series issued from 2012-2023 celebrating each lunar year.

highlights.design: Stylized rabbit imagery integrated with Chinese zodiac symbols and Macanese cultural landmarks including A-Ma Temple and Banco da China headquarters.
highlights.reason: Commemorative issuance celebrating the Year of the Rabbit in the Chinese lunar calendar as part of Macau's annual zodiac banknote series.
highlights.security: 4-mm wide windowed security thread with demetalized 10 and lotus flower watermark with electrotype 10.

title: 100 Rupees
description: Orange and brown banknote honoring the nation's founding father, featuring Sir Seewoosagur Ramgoolam and the historic Bank of Mauritius building.
body: The 100 Rupees banknote features Sir Seewoosagur Ramgoolam KCMG (1900-1985), known as the 'Father of the Nation' and Mauritius' first Prime Minister, prominently displayed at the left. The design incorporates the Mauritius Coat of Arms at lower left, classical facades of the old Bank of Mauritius building in the underprint, and a figure of Blindfolded Lady Justice holding balance scales and a sword. The banknote includes tri-lingual text in English, Hindi, and Tamil, and incorporates imagery of a dodo bird head.

highlights.design: Portrait of national hero Ramgoolam combined with symbolic representations of justice and the extinct dodo bird, an emblem of Mauritius.
highlights.reason: 2023 issuance honors Seewoosagur Ramgoolam's pivotal role in Mauritius' path to independence and establishing the nation's democratic institutions.
highlights.security: Windowed security thread with demetalized BANK OF MAURITIUS and watermark of dodo bird with cornerstones; printed by De La Rue.

title: 200 Rupees
description: Brown and cream banknote depicting Sir Charles Gaetan Duval, a prominent lawyer and political leader of the Parti Mauricien Social Democrate.
body: The 200 Rupees banknote honors Sir Charles Gaetan Duval, Q.C. (1930-1996), a lawyer, statesman, and influential politician who led the Parti Mauricien Social Democrate (PMSD) during Mauritius' critical development period. His portrait appears at the left of the obverse, alongside the Mauritius Coat of Arms at lower left. The design includes facades of the historic Bank of Mauritius building in the underprint and features Blindfolded Lady Justice holding balance scales and a sword, symbolizing the rule of law and justice in the nation. The note measures 150 x 72 mm and includes tri-lingual inscriptions in English, Hindi, and Tamil.

highlights.design: Portrait of political leader Duval integrated with classical Bank of Mauritius architecture and allegorical Justice imagery.
highlights.reason: 2023 issuance commemorates Charles Gaetan Duval's contributions as a jurist and statesman during Mauritius' path toward independence and democratic governance.
highlights.security: Windowed security thread with demetalized BANK OF MAURITIUS, watermark of dodo bird with cornerstones; printed by De La Rue on paper measuring 150 x 72 mm.

title: 500 Rupees
description: Orange-hued banknote featuring independence activist Sookdeo Bissoondoyal and the University of Mauritius on the reverse.
body: The 500 Rupees banknote honors Sookdeo Bissoondoyal (1908-1977), a Mauritian politician and influential figure in the independence movement who shaped the nation's early political development. His portrait is prominently featured on the obverse. The reverse displays the University of Mauritius building and palm trees in Reduit, symbolizing the nation's commitment to education and development. The note includes the Mauritius Coat of Arms, bank building facade, and Standing Justice with sword and scales. The banknote features a silver foil oval security element and tri-lingual text in English, Hindi, and Tamil.

highlights.design: Bissoondoyal's portrait combined with the University of Mauritius symbol, representing the link between political leadership and educational advancement.
highlights.reason: 2023 issuance recognizes Sookdeo Bissoondoyal's pivotal role in Mauritius' struggle for independence and nation-building efforts.
highlights.security: Silver foil oval security element, demetalized security features, and watermark elements; printed by De La Rue on paper measuring 155 x 74 mm.

title: 100 Pesos
description: Red polymer banknote honoring Sor Juana Ines de la Cruz with monarch butterfly imagery representing Mexico's natural heritage.
body: The 100 Pesos 2023 banknote celebrates Sor Juana Ines de la Cruz, regarded as the most important poet and writer of nuevo Hispanic literature, on the obverse. The design features the colonial-era patio of San Ildefonso College, symbolizing Mexico's intellectual heritage during the colonial period. The reverse depicts monarch butterflies (Danaus plexippus) and the Monarch Butterfly Biosphere Reserve, highlighting Mexico's biodiversity and commitment to environmental conservation. Measuring 132 x 65 mm on red polymer substrate with vertical orientation.

highlights.design: Integration of Sor Juana's intellectual legacy with the iconic monarch butterfly migration, symbolizing Mexico's cultural and natural treasures.
highlights.reason: 2023 issuance honors Sor Juana Ines de la Cruz's pioneering contributions to Mexican letters and celebrates the monarch butterfly's significance to Mexico's ecosystem.
highlights.security: Simulated security thread with printed 100 PESOS and butterflies; transparent window on Bank of Mexico Gate with butterfly imagery and raised number 100.

title: 1.000 Pesos
description: Gray and blue banknote featuring three independence-era figures — Carmen Serdan, Hermila Galindo, and Francisco I. Madero — with jaguar and Mayan temple imagery.
body: The 1,000 Pesos 2023 banknote features three pivotal figures from Mexico's independence and revolution: Carmen Serdan, Hermila Galindo, and Francisco I. Madero. This historic note is notable as the first Mexican banknote to feature three characters — two women and a man — in equal prominence. The obverse displays a steam locomotive alongside the three portraits, symbolizing Mexico's modernization. The reverse depicts a jaguar and the Mayan temple of Calakmul, connecting Mexico's pre-Hispanic heritage with its post-revolutionary identity. Dated 07 February 2023, and measuring 153 x 65 mm.

highlights.design: Unprecedented representation of three historical figures on a Mexican banknote, with jaguar and archaeological heritage symbolizing Mexico's indigenous roots.
highlights.reason: 2023 issuance commemorates Mexico's independence movement and celebrates the contributions of women (Serdan, Galindo) and Francisco I. Madero to Mexican history.
highlights.security: Windowed security thread with demetalized 1000, watermark of Francisco I. Madero with electrotype 1000; printed on cotton substrate.

title: 20.000 Tögrög
description: Yellow, purple, and green banknote featuring Chinggis Khan and enhanced security features upgraded from 2018 counterfeiting concerns.
body: The 20,000 Tugrik 2023 banknote features Chinggis Khan, the legendary founder of the Mongol Empire, on the obverse alongside a paiza (royal tablet) and the national coat of arms of Mongolia. The reverse displays the Flags of the Golden Horde, commemorating Mongol historical legacy. Measuring 152 x 72 mm and dated 2023, this denomination represents part of the Bank of Mongolia's comprehensive security upgrade targeting the three highest denominations (5,000, 10,000, and 20,000 Togrog) which were most vulnerable to counterfeiting.

highlights.design: Chinggis Khan portrait combined with paiza and Golden Horde flags, emphasizing Mongolia's historical dominance and empire-building legacy.
highlights.reason: 2023 issuance includes advanced security features to prevent counterfeiting of high-denomination notes critical to Mongolia's currency circulation.
highlights.security: Solid security thread with modified design, watermark of Chinggis Khan with electrotype, representing upgraded security standards implemented across the highest denominations.

title: 20 Dirhams
description: Purple banknote celebrating Morocco's architectural heritage, featuring King Mohammed VI and the Al Quaraouiyine University in Fez.
body: The 20 Dirhams 2023 banknote celebrates Morocco's rich architectural and cultural heritage. The obverse features a portrait of King Mohammed VI, the Moroccan coat of arms, and architectural details inspired by traditional Moroccan portals alongside Al Quaraouiyine University in Fez — the world's oldest continuously functioning university. The reverse showcases symbols of Moroccan modernity including the Grand Theatre of Rabat, the Mohammed VI Museum of Modern and Contemporary Art, and a stylized view of the Kasbah of Ait-Ben-Haddou. Measuring 130 x 70 mm, the note was introduced on August 21, 2024.

highlights.design: Al Quaraouiyine University imagery linked with contemporary cultural institutions (Grand Theatre, Modern Art Museum), bridging historical and modern Morocco.
highlights.reason: 2023 issuance highlights Morocco's architectural heritage and socio-cultural development while emphasizing the nation's commitment to preserving historical monuments.
highlights.security: Dynamic color-shifting security thread, iridescent stripe on reverse, tactile relief features for visually impaired individuals, and progressive numbering; designed and produced by Dar As-Sikkah.

title: 50 Dirhams
description: Green banknote highlighting King Mohammed VI's sustainability vision, featuring Ouzoud Falls and renewable energy projects across Morocco.
body: The 50 Dirhams 2023 banknote emphasizes Morocco's commitment to renewable energy and environmental sustainability under King Mohammed VI's leadership. The obverse features the portrait of King Mohammed VI, the Moroccan coat of arms, and the stunning Ouzoud Falls — Morocco's most spectacular waterfall. The reverse showcases Morocco's pioneering renewable energy initiatives, including the Noor Ouarzazate solar station (one of the world's largest concentrated solar power complexes), the Ouirgane Dam, and the desalination station in Agadir. Saffron flowers and wind turbines complete the environmental narrative. Measuring 137 x 70 mm, dated 2023, introduced on July 30, 2024.

highlights.design: Ouzoud Falls paired with renewable energy infrastructure (solar station, wind turbines, desalination facility) symbolizing Morocco's green energy transition.
highlights.reason: 2023 issuance celebrates King Mohammed VI's environmental vision and Morocco's position as a leader in renewable energy and sustainable development in Africa.
highlights.security: Blue-to-green windowed security thread with demetalized five-pointed star; watermark of King Mohammed VI with electrotype 50; dynamic color-shifting effect, iridescent band on reverse, front-to-back registration, and raised patterns for visually impaired.

title: 100 Dirhams
description: A blue banknote featuring King Mohammed VI and the Hassan II Mosque archways, highlighting Moroccan Saharan heritage and development.
body: Issued on November 24, 2023, this redesigned 100-Dirham note showcases Morocco's cultural heritage and socio-economic development. The obverse displays a portrait of King Mohamed VI alongside arcades of the Hassan II Mosque in Casablanca, with architectural details of traditional Moroccan doors. The reverse depicts the Tiznit-Dakhla expressway, Place Mechourar in Laayoune, and the Feast of Moussem de Tan-Tan. Measuring 144 x 70 mm, the note was produced by Dar As-Sikkah and features advanced security including a segmented thread with shifting colors and optical variable ink.

highlights.design: Portrait of King Mohamed VI paired with Hassan II Mosque arcades and traditional Moroccan architectural elements.
highlights.reason: Celebrating Morocco's cultural heritage and socio-economic progress, particularly in the Saharan regions.
highlights.security: Wide segmented thread with shifting colors, optical variable ink with magnetic orientation on denomination, UV and IR printed details.

title: 200 Dirhams
description: A brown and orange banknote showcasing King Mohammed VI alongside the Mohammed VI cable-stayed bridge and modern Moroccan infrastructure.
body: Launched on January 12, 2024, as part of the 2023 series, this 200-Dirham note was timed to commemorate the 80th anniversary of Morocco's Independence Manifesto. The obverse features a portrait of King Mohammed VI and the Kingdom's coat of arms with the iconic Mohammed VI cable-stayed bridge. The reverse displays a stylized view of the Mohammed VI Tower and Marrakech-Menara International Airport, symbolizing Morocco's industrial and digital development. The note incorporates three-dimensional dynamic security threads, color-changing magnetic ink, and geometric patterns on both sides for enhanced security.

highlights.design: King Mohammed VI with the Mohammed VI bridge, tower, and Marrakech-Menara Airport symbolizing economic modernization.
highlights.reason: Celebrating Morocco's economic and industrial development under King Mohammed VI, timed with the 80th anniversary of the Independence Manifesto.
highlights.security: Three-dimensional dynamic security thread, color-changing magnetic ink, geometric patterns, and recognition system for visually impaired users.

title: 20.000 Kyats
description: A green banknote featuring a sacred white elephant, issued to commemorate the Maravijaya Buddha Image completion and the birthday of Rattha Nandaka.
body: Introduced on July 31, 2023, this 20,000 Kyat note was issued in commemoration of the completion of the Maravijaya Buddha Image and the one-year birthday celebration of Rattha Nandaka, a white elephant calf born on July 23, 2022. The obverse features a prominent elephant, while the reverse showcases the Sagaing Bridge and Irrawaddy Bridge. Measuring 150 x 70 mm, the note incorporates a windowed security thread and a watermark featuring the head of the Yadana white elephant with electrotype denomination. Distribution was limited through exchange with older banknotes at Central Bank branches.

highlights.design: Sacred white elephant on obverse with dual bridge architecture (Sagaing and Irrawaddy Bridges) on reverse.
highlights.reason: Commemorating the Maravijaya Buddha Image completion and celebrating the first birthday of sacred white elephant Rattha Nandaka.
highlights.security: Windowed security thread and watermark of Yadana white elephant head with electrotype 20000.

title: 100 Namibia Dollars
description: A pink, green, and yellow note featuring Kaptein Hendrik Witbooi and the Namibian Parliament building, with three standing oryx antelopes on the reverse.
body: This 2023 circulation banknote issued by the Bank of Namibia measures 147 x 70 mm. The obverse features Kaptein Hendrik Witbooi, the Parliament building, and the bank logo rendered in green-to-gold SPARK coloring. The reverse displays three standing oryx antelopes (Oryx gazella), symbolizing Namibia's iconic wildlife heritage. Security features include a BON diaphan registration device, a green-to-magenta/blue Janus windowed security thread with demetalized BON 100 denomination, and a watermark of Hendrik Witbooi with electrotype 100.

highlights.design: Kaptein Hendrik Witbooi with Parliament building and three oryx antelopes representing national wildlife.
highlights.reason: Standard circulation currency featuring an important Namibian historical figure and the iconic national antelope.
highlights.security: Color-changing Janus security thread, demetalized denomination, watermark of Hendrik Witbooi with electrotype 100.

title: 20 Rupees
description: A banknote featuring Mount Everest on the obverse and two swamp deer on the reverse, celebrating Nepal's natural landmarks and wildlife heritage.
body: Introduced by Nepal Rastra Bank in 2023, this 20 Rupee note features Mount Everest (Sagarmatha) prominently on the obverse, symbolizing Nepal's geographical identity. The reverse showcases two swamp deer, highlighting the country's diverse wildlife. The design includes 'NEPAL RASTRA BANK' printed in English at the top and a Laliguras (rhododendron) flower watermark for security. The 2023 series continues Nepal's tradition of celebrating its iconic natural landmarks and biodiversity.

highlights.design: Mount Everest on obverse paired with two swamp deer on reverse, representing Nepal's natural heritage.
highlights.reason: Continuing Nepal's currency tradition of celebrating iconic geographical landmarks and native wildlife species.
highlights.security: Laliguras flower watermark for enhanced security identification.

title: 20 Pounds Sterling
description: A predominantly blue polymer banknote featuring inventor Harry Ferguson on the obverse with Belfast City Hall's pediment on the reverse.
body: Danske Bank's polymer 20 Pound note honors Harry Ferguson, born in County Down in 1884, renowned for developing the modern tractor and its hydraulic rear linkage system still used worldwide today. The obverse displays a portrait of Ferguson alongside a tractor motif and gold foil accents, while the reverse features the pediment of Belfast City Hall. The polymer version measures slightly smaller than earlier paper issues and includes a see-through security section, holographic foil, and a tactile feature assisting visually impaired users. A printed magnetic stripe runs vertically through the note.

highlights.design: Portrait of Harry Ferguson with tractor imagery and Belfast City Hall's iconic pediment.
highlights.reason: Honoring an important Northern Irish inventor whose contributions revolutionized agricultural technology and tractor design.
highlights.security: See-through window section, holographic foil, tactile feature for visually impaired identification, and vertical printed magnetic stripe.

title: 75 Rupees
description: A blue and green commemorative note honoring the 75th anniversary of the State Bank of Pakistan, featuring the SBP building and Muhammad Ali Jinnah.
body: Issued on July 4, 2023, by State Bank of Pakistan Governor Jameel Ahmed, this 75 Rupees commemorative banknote celebrates the central bank's 75th anniversary (1948-2023). The blue and green note measures 139 x 65 mm. The obverse features the State Bank of Pakistan building in Karachi and a portrait of Muhammad Ali Jinnah (Quaid-e-Azam), the founder of Pakistan. The reverse displays wind turbines symbolizing renewable energy development and a portrait of Mohtrama Fatima Jinnah. Security features include a Pureimage windowed holographic security thread with demetalized denomination and a watermark of Muhammad Ali Jinnah with electrotype 75.

highlights.design: Muhammad Ali Jinnah and SBP building paired with wind turbines and Fatima Jinnah, linking founding history with modern development.
highlights.reason: Commemorating the 75th anniversary of the State Bank of Pakistan's founding and its role in guiding Pakistan's monetary policy.
highlights.security: Pureimage windowed holographic security thread with demetalized 75, watermark of Muhammad Ali Jinnah with electrotype 75.

title: 2 Kina
description: A green polymer banknote featuring the Bird of Paradise on a ceremonial spear with a traditional Kundu drum, representing Papua New Guinea's national emblem and cultural artifacts.
body: This multicolored green, yellow, and teal polymer 2 Kina note measures 125 x 70 mm. The obverse prominently features a stylized Bird of Paradise sitting atop a ceremonial spear, accompanied by a traditional Kundu drum. The national coat of arms and the National Parliament building in Port Moresby are also displayed. A solid security thread runs vertically over the bird's left wing, visible only under backlight. The reverse depicts Mount Hagan axes, a Kula armband from Milne Bay Province, an engraved dogtooth necklace from Bougainville, Sepik clay pottery, and traditional tapa cloth patterns.

highlights.design: Stylized Bird of Paradise on ceremonial spear with Kundu drum and regional cultural artifacts from across PNG's provinces.
highlights.reason: Representing Papua New Guinea's rich cultural heritage and diverse national symbols of unity across the nation's many provinces.
highlights.security: Solid security thread visible under backlight, embedded over the bird's left wing on the polymer substrate.

title: 100 Kina
description: A high-denomination polymer banknote honoring Sir Julius Chan, Papua New Guinea's founding financial architect and former Prime Minister.
body: This commemorative 100 Kina note honors Sir Julius Chan, the statesman whose vision shaped Papua New Guinea's financial destiny. Chan played a pivotal role in establishing the Bank of Papua New Guinea and introducing the kina and toea currency in 1975 as the nation asserted financial independence from the Australian dollar. The polymer substrate ensures durability and counterfeiting protection. The design recognizes Chan's foundational contributions to PNG's economic sovereignty and his leadership during the critical transition to national financial independence.

highlights.design: Commemorative portrait of Sir Julius Chan honoring his role as founding financial architect of Papua New Guinea.
highlights.reason: Recognizing Sir Julius Chan's instrumental role in establishing PNG's currency system and central bank at independence in 1975.
highlights.security: Guardian polymer substrate providing enhanced durability and counterfeiting protection.

title: 50 Kina
description: An orange polymer commemorative note celebrating the 50th anniversary of the Bank of Papua New Guinea, honoring Grand Chief Sir Michael Somare.
body: Launched in November 2023, this commemorative 50 Kina polymer banknote celebrates the 50th anniversary of the Bank of Papua New Guinea, which was established on November 1, 1973. The orange note honors Grand Chief Sir Michael Somare, Papua New Guinea's first Prime Minister, with his portrait prominently displayed. The obverse features the coat of arms with bird of paradise and the National Parliament building in Port Moresby alongside a golden '50 YEARS' logo. The reverse displays cultural treasures including spears from Sepik, shields and masks from New Ireland, Tubuan face masks from New Britain, headdress from the Highlands, and costumes from the Sepik River.

highlights.design: Grand Chief Sir Michael Somare's portrait with Parliament House and 50th anniversary emblem, reverse featuring diverse cultural artifacts from across PNG.
highlights.reason: Commemorating 50 years of the Bank of Papua New Guinea's operation and honoring Grand Chief Sir Michael Somare's founding leadership.
highlights.security: Guardian polymer substrate providing enhanced protection and durability against counterfeiting.

title: 2.000 Guaraníes
description: The 2,000 Guaraníes polymer note honours Adela and Celsa Speratti, Paraguayan sisters who devoted their lives to building the country's modern educational system.
body: Issued by the Banco Central del Paraguay, this purple and green polymer banknote bears the date 2023. The obverse depicts portraits of the Speratti sisters alongside an open book, the national coat of arms, and a BCP registration device. The reverse shows a patriotic parade of young men waving Paraguayan flags, celebrating civic pride and national identity.

highlights.design: Portraits of educators Adela and Celsa Speratti on the obverse with a parade scene on the reverse.
highlights.reason: Continuing circulation issue of the polymer Guaraníes series honouring distinguished Paraguayan educators.
highlights.security: Polymer substrate with transparent window, tactile elements, and colour-shifting optical features.

title: 50 Piso
description: Remark.
body:
The Philippine 50 Piso paper banknote from the New Generation Currency (NGC) series, with 2023-2025 date variants, features President Sergio Osmeña (1878-1961), the fourth president of the Philippines from 1944 to 1946, on its red obverse. The note depicts historical elements including the First Philippine Assembly in 1907 and General Douglas MacArthur's landing in Leyte. The reverse side showcases Taal Lake (also known as Bombón Lake) in Batangas province and the giant trevally fish (Caranx ignobilis), also known locally as maliputo. The 2023 version bears the signatures of President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. and BSP Governor Felipe Medalla.

This banknote represents the New Generation Currency series, an enhanced version of Philippine paper currency with improved security features and accessibility improvements for visually impaired users, including embossed tactile marks. The note measures 160 mm x 66 mm and includes a solid security thread and watermark featuring Sergio Osmeña.

highlights.reason: Honors President Sergio Osmeña and commemorates Philippine democratic heritage through historical imagery of the First Parliament and Liberation.
highlights.design: Features Sergio Osmeña and First Parliament on obverse; Taal Lake and giant trevally (maliputo) fish on reverse.
highlights.security: Includes solid security thread, watermark of Sergio Osmeña with electrotype 50, enhanced color-changing security threads, and embossed tactile marks for visually impaired users.

title: 100 Piso
description: The 100 Piso note features former President Manuel Roxas on the obverse and the iconic Mayon Volcano alongside a whale shark on the reverse.
body: This violet cotton-abacá paper note is part of the New Generation Currency series introduced by the Bangko Sentral ng Pilipinas. The obverse bears a portrait of Manuel Roxas, the first President of the independent Third Republic, together with the 1949 Central Bank building and a scene from the Proclamation of the Third Republic in 1946. The reverse highlights the Philippines' natural heritage with an image of Mayon Volcano in Albay, a whale shark, and a Bicol Region textile weave design.

highlights.design: Mayon Volcano and whale shark on the reverse representing Philippine natural heritage.
highlights.reason: Regular circulation note of the New Generation Currency series representing Philippine history and natural wonders.
highlights.security: 4 mm RollingStar windowed security thread with demetalized BSP/100 text and a Manuel Roxas watermark with electrotype 100.

title: 100 Piso
description: Remark.
body:
The Philippine 100 Piso paper banknote from the New Generation Currency (NGC) series, with 2023-2025 date variants, features President Manuel A. Roxas on its violet obverse. The front also includes imagery of the Central Bank of the Philippines headquarters in Manila and the inauguration of the Third Republic on July 4, 1946, commemorating Roxas's presidency and role in Philippine independence. The reverse side features the Mayon Volcano in Legazpi City, Albay, a distinctive and iconic active stratovolcano, along with the whale shark (Rhincodon typus), the world's largest fish species. The reverse design also incorporates Bicol Region textile patterns.

This paper banknote version features enhanced security including a color-changing security thread added in 2020 and includes embossed tactile features for the visually impaired. The note measures 160 mm x 66 mm and incorporates a watermark of Manuel A. Roxas with electrotype 100, representing the continuation of the NGC series with modernized security features.

highlights.reason: Honors President Manuel A. Roxas and the founding of the Third Republic of the Philippines through historical and natural imagery.
highlights.design: Features Manuel A. Roxas and Third Republic imagery on obverse; Mayon Volcano and whale shark on reverse with regional textile design.
highlights.security: Includes watermark, color-changing security threads, enhanced indigenous pattern security elements, and embossed tactile marks for visually impaired accessibility.

title: 20 Złotych
description: The circulating 20 Złotych note features King Bolesław I Chrobry, the first crowned King of Poland, surrounded by imagery evoking the early Piast dynasty.
body: Issued by the Narodowy Bank Polski, the 20 Złotych note portrays King Bolesław I Chrobry at the centre of the obverse, flanked by a Romanesque portal and an oak branch motif taken from the famous Gniezno Doors. The reverse depicts a silver denarius coin struck under Bolesław I bearing the inscription PRINCES POLONIE, together with the Church of St Nicholas in Cieszyn and a lion detail from the Gniezno Cathedral doors. The note's design celebrates the medieval foundations of Polish statehood and the Piast dynasty's cultural achievements.

highlights.design: Portrait of Bolesław I Chrobry with Romanesque architectural motifs and a medieval denarius coin on the reverse.
highlights.reason: Regular circulation note honouring Poland's first crowned king and the origins of the Polish state.
highlights.security: Solid security thread with demetalized 20 ZŁ, a watermark portrait of Bolesław I Chrobry with electrotype 20, and a latent image showing the numeral 20.

title: 1.000 Rubley
description: The redesigned 1,000 Ruble note shifts the theme from Yaroslavl to Nizhny Novgorod and the Volga Federal District, showcasing the Nikolskaya Tower of the Nizhny Novgorod Kremlin.
body: Unveiled by the Bank of Russia on 16 October 2023, this modified 1,000 ruble banknote dedicates its imagery to Nizhny Novgorod and the broader Volga Federal District, replacing the former Yaroslavl theme. The obverse features the Nikolskaya Tower of the Nizhny Novgorod Kremlin and the confluence of the Oka and Volga rivers. The reverse depicts the Saratov road bridge, the Meteor high-speed hydrofoil, and the Palace of Farmers in Kazan, representing the diversity of the Volga region.

highlights.design: Nikolskaya Tower of the Nizhny Novgorod Kremlin on the obverse and Volga Federal District landmarks on the reverse.
highlights.reason: Modernisation of the ruble series replacing Yaroslavl imagery with Nizhny Novgorod and the Volga region.
highlights.security: Windowed holographic security thread, updated colour-shifting ink, and improved anti-counterfeiting elements over the previous series.

title: 5.000 Rubley
description: The redesigned 5,000 Ruble note replaces Khabarovsk with Yekaterinburg and the Ural Federal District, centred on the iconic Europe-Asia stele on the obverse.
body: Also introduced on 16 October 2023, this predominantly red modified 5,000 ruble banknote is dedicated to Yekaterinburg and the Ural Federal District. The obverse prominently features the Europe-Asia boundary stele in Yekaterinburg, symbolising Russia's geographical position spanning two continents. The reverse displays the Tale of the Urals monument in Chelyabinsk and the 66th Parallel Arctic Circle stele in Salekhard, reflecting the vast cultural and geographical reach of the Ural region.

highlights.design: Europe-Asia boundary stele in Yekaterinburg on the obverse and Ural Federal District monuments on the reverse.
highlights.reason: Modernisation of the ruble series replacing Khabarovsk imagery with Yekaterinburg and the Ural Federal District.
highlights.security: 5.5 mm winged windowed holographic security thread with demetalized 5000 CBRF, watermark of post office building with electrotype 5000, and dual-type coloured security fibres.

title: 60 Tala
description: The 60 Tala commemorative note celebrates Samoa's 60th anniversary of independence, featuring Prime Minister Fiame Naomi Mata'afa on the obverse and the historic flag-lowering ceremony on the reverse.
body: Issued by the Central Bank of Samoa and printed by De La Rue, this limited-edition legal tender note was released into circulation on 31 May 2023. The obverse displays the 60th-anniversary logo, the Samoan flag, the national coat of arms, and a portrait of Prime Minister Fiame Naomi Mata'afa, the first female prime minister of Samoa. The reverse depicts former Prime Minister Fiame Mata'afa Faumuina Mulinuu II lowering the New Zealand flag at independence, a defining moment in Samoa's history.

highlights.design: Portrait of Samoa's first female prime minister on the obverse and the 1962 independence flag-lowering ceremony on the reverse.
highlights.reason: Commemorating the 60th anniversary of Samoan independence (1962-2022).
highlights.security: 4.5 mm blue/green IGNITE security thread depicting a Samoan fue fly-whisk that pulses when tilted, windowed security thread with demetalized 60, and a ginger flower watermark with CBS electrotype.

title: 5 Tala
description: The new polymer 5 Tala note depicts one of Samoa's pristine sandy beaches on the obverse and Villa Vailima, the former home of author Robert Louis Stevenson, on the reverse.
body: Released on 18 December 2023 by the Central Bank of Samoa, this red polymer banknote upgrades the earlier cotton-based paper version using De La Rue's SAFEGUARD substrate. The obverse showcases one of Samoa's iconic sandy beaches, highlighting the country's tourism resources, while the reverse features Villa Vailima — now the Robert Louis Stevenson Museum near Apia — reflecting the island's literary and colonial heritage. Six tactile embossed dots on the note assist visually impaired users in identifying the denomination.

highlights.design: Samoan beach scene on the obverse and Villa Vailima, the Robert Louis Stevenson Museum, on the reverse.
highlights.reason: Upgrade of the circulating 5 Tala denomination from cotton paper to durable polymer substrate.
