highlights.security: Transparent SAFEGUARD polymer window displaying the teuila flower, GEMINI dual-colour UV feature, BLINK phosphorescent denomination block, and six tactile embossed dots.

title: 10 Tala
description: The polymer 10 Tala note celebrates Samoan sporting achievement with an image of the national rugby team being crowned champions at the 2007 IRB Hong Kong Sevens on the obverse.
body: Part of Samoa's 2023 polymer banknote upgrade printed on De La Rue's SAFEGUARD substrate, the 10 Tala note honours Samoan rugby on its obverse by depicting the national sevens team's historic victory at the 2007 International Rugby Board Hong Kong Sevens tournament. The reverse shows Samoan schoolchildren, reflecting the country's investment in education and its youth. Eight tactile embossed dots on the note allow visually impaired users to identify the denomination.

highlights.design: Samoan rugby sevens team's 2007 Hong Kong Sevens championship victory on the obverse and schoolchildren on the reverse.
highlights.reason: Upgrade of the circulating 10 Tala denomination to polymer substrate as part of Samoa's phased banknote conversion.
highlights.security: Transparent SAFEGUARD polymer window with teuila flower motif, GEMINI dual-colour UV feature, BLINK phosphorescent denomination block, and eight tactile embossed dots.

title: 20 Tala
description: The polymer 20 Tala note features the Sinaloa waterfall on Savai'i on the obverse and the manumea bird alongside the teuila flower — Samoa's national symbols — on the reverse.
body: Also part of the Central Bank of Samoa's 2023 polymer conversion series printed by De La Rue on SAFEGUARD substrate, the 20 Tala note highlights Samoa's natural heritage. The obverse depicts the cascading Sinaloa waterfall on Savai'i island, which drops 220 metres, making it one of the most dramatic natural features in the Pacific. The reverse showcases the manumea (tooth-billed pigeon), Samoa's national bird, alongside the teuila, the national flower. Ten tactile embossed dots assist visually impaired users.

highlights.design: Sinaloa waterfall on Savai'i on the obverse and the manumea national bird with teuila national flower on the reverse.
highlights.reason: Upgrade of the circulating 20 Tala denomination to polymer substrate as part of Samoa's phased conversion of its banknote series.
highlights.security: Transparent SAFEGUARD polymer window with teuila flower motif, GEMINI dual-colour UV feature, BLINK phosphorescent denomination block, and ten tactile embossed dots.

title: 100 Pounds Sterling
description: The turquoise polymer £100 note features Sir Walter Scott on the obverse and Dr Flora Murray — a Scottish physician and suffragette — on the reverse.
body: Dated 16 August 2021 and introduced into circulation on 9 May 2022, this turquoise SAFEGUARD polymer note completes Bank of Scotland's full conversion of its banknote series to polymer. The obverse bears the familiar portrait of Sir Walter Scott alongside the Bank of Scotland headquarters on The Mound in Edinburgh. The reverse honours Dr Flora Murray (1869-1923), the pioneering Scottish surgeon and suffragette who established one of the first military hospitals run by women during the First World War.

highlights.design: Sir Walter Scott and The Mound on the obverse; Dr Flora Murray depicted at work and with female stretcher bearers outside her Covent Garden hospital on the reverse.
highlights.reason: Completing Bank of Scotland's upgrade of its full banknote family to durable polymer substrate.
highlights.security: Clear polymer window with a bespoke holographic foil stripe combining direct-write lithography and Benton holography, PUREIMAGE animation, DEPTH effects, GEMINI UV dual-colour feature, and tactile emboss for visually impaired users.

title: 5 Dollars
description: The Singapore $5 Portrait Series polymer banknote features the portrait of Yusof bin Ishak, the first President of Singapore, on the obverse against a Garden City theme on the reverse.
body: The $5 note is part of the Portrait Series, Singapore's fourth series of currency notes issued by the Monetary Authority of Singapore (MAS), first released in polymer form on 18 May 2007. The obverse displays Yusof bin Ishak alongside the Singapore Coat of Arms and the gold-ringed cowrie motif, while the reverse showcases the Garden City theme with the iconic 200-year-old tembusu tree from the Singapore Botanic Gardens and Singapore's national flower, Vanda Miss Joaquim. The 2023 date edition continues the established Portrait Series design on Safeguard polymer substrate.

highlights.design: Reverse features the 200-year-old tembusu tree at the Singapore Botanic Gardens and the national flower Vanda Miss Joaquim, representing Singapore's Garden City identity.
highlights.reason: Continuation of the Portrait Series on polymer substrate, maintaining the long-running design honouring Yusof bin Ishak and Singapore's natural heritage.
highlights.security: Two clear see-through polymer windows, intaglio-printed portrait of Yusof bin Ishak, windowed security thread shaped like Singapore island, and a stylised gold Singapore Lion symbol.

title: 50 Dollars
description: The 2023 Solomon Islands $50 banknote features abstract basket-weave patterns on the obverse and a reverse celebrating the islands' unique biodiversity through images of local fauna and flora.
body: The upgraded $50 note was introduced into circulation on 28 December 2023 by the Central Bank of Solomon Islands, replacing the previous issue's Optiks security thread with a new Nexus 18mm wide micro-optic stripe. The obverse design conveys dynamic movement with sloping numerals, an eagle watermark, billowing abstract patterns inspired by shoaling fish, the Solomon Islands flag, and basket-weave designs evoking traditional craft. The reverse features montages of the islands' native biodiversity, including shells, lizards, snakes, iguanas, and fungi, celebrating the rich natural environment of the Solomon Islands.

highlights.design: Nexus 18mm micro-optic stripe on the obverse containing the denomination numeral, 'CBSI', and images of a shark and crocodile from the Solomon Islands Coat of Arms.
highlights.reason: Security upgrade of the existing $50 denomination, replacing the previous Optiks thread with advanced Nexus micro-optic stripe technology.
highlights.security: Nexus 18mm wide micro-optic stripe, watermark with falcon head, electrotype CBSI and cornerstones visible under light, and tactile raised print.

title: 100 Dollars
description: The 2023 Solomon Islands $100 banknote features dynamic abstract patterning on the obverse and scenes of coconut growing and plantation harvesting on the reverse.
body: The $100 note entered circulation on 29 December 2023 as part of the CBSI's upgrade of its highest-denomination paper notes, replacing the earlier Optiks security thread with a cutting-edge Nexus micro-optic stripe. The obverse features abstract patterns representing swirling shoals of fish, the Solomon Islands flag and coat of arms, conveying a prevailing sense of rightward movement. The reverse depicts scenes of traditional coconut cultivation and harvesting, including images of an open coconut, a coconut shell necklace, and palm trees, reflecting the central importance of coconut farming to the Solomon Islands economy and culture.

highlights.design: Reverse depicts traditional coconut plantation harvesting with open coconuts, coconut shell necklaces, and palm trees, reflecting cultural and economic heritage.
highlights.reason: Security upgrade replacing the previous Optiks thread with the advanced Nexus 18mm micro-optic stripe technology on the highest-denomination paper note.
highlights.security: Nexus 18mm wide micro-optic stripe containing denomination numerals, 'CBSI', and shark and crocodile images; falcon-head watermark; electrotype CBSI; cornerstones visible under light.

title: 10 Dollars
description: The 2023 Solomon Islands $10 polymer commemorative banknote was issued to honour the 17th Pacific Games hosted in Honiara, featuring the national flag and Pacific Games branding on the obverse and athletes on the reverse.
body: The CBSI released this red polymer note on 27 October 2023, with one million notes printed by De La Rue on Safeguard polymer substrate, making it the first polymer note in the Solomon Islands' general currency history. The obverse displays a large Solomon Islands flag, the coat of arms, the inscription '2023 Pacific Games', and a see-through window showing an eagle head and the Pacific Games logo of three paddles. The reverse showcases athletes participating in weightlifting, football, bodybuilding, basketball, and rugby, celebrating the sporting spirit and the Solomon Islands' role as host nation.

highlights.design: Polymer see-through window on the obverse showing the eagle head and the 17th Pacific Games logo of three paddles.
highlights.reason: Issued to commemorate the 17th Pacific Games, hosted in Honiara, Solomon Islands, from 19 November to 2 December 2023.
highlights.security: Polymer substrate with see-through windows; special SI/23 prefix serial numbers unique to this commemorative issue.

title: 20 Rand
description: The 2023 South African 20 Rand is a brown-and-violet Eighth Series banknote featuring Nelson Mandela on the obverse and three African elephants on the reverse.
body: The 20 Rand note entered circulation on 4 May 2023 as part of the South African Reserve Bank's Eighth Series upgrade of the Mandela banknote family. The obverse features Nelson Mandela's portrait with rings composed of the South African flag and constitutional preamble text, alongside the national coat of arms and two elephants rendered in SPARK Live Truspin colour-changing ink. The reverse depicts three African elephants, representing the elephant as one of the iconic Big Five animals and symbolising strength and wisdom.

highlights.design: Two elephants in SPARK Live Truspin colour-shifting ink on the obverse, with three elephants on the reverse representing the Big Five.
highlights.reason: Eighth Series security upgrade of the Mandela banknote series, introducing enhanced SPARK Live Truspin and RollingStar security thread technology.
highlights.security: SPARK Live Truspin colour-shifting elephant, RollingStar holographic windowed security thread with coat of arms and denomination, watermark of elephant and electrotype 20.

title: 50 Rand
description: The 2023 South African 50 Rand is a purple-and-olive Eighth Series banknote featuring Nelson Mandela on the obverse and two African lions on the reverse.
body: The 50 Rand note was released into circulation on 4 May 2023 as part of the SARB's Eighth Series upgrade, introducing advanced SPARK Live Truspin colour-shifting animal features and a new RollingStar security thread across all five denominations. The obverse displays Nelson Mandela's portrait surrounded by constitutional text and the South African flag arranged in rings, with two lions depicted in SPARK Live Truspin ink. The reverse shows a family of two lions, reflecting a design philosophy of depicting family groups of each Big Five animal and symbolising excellence and courage.

highlights.design: SPARK Live Truspin colour-shifting lions on the obverse that change colour when tilted, with a lion family group on the reverse.
highlights.reason: Eighth Series security upgrade of the Mandela banknote series with enhanced anti-counterfeiting features including the new SPARK Live Truspin and RollingStar thread.
highlights.security: SPARK Live Truspin colour-shifting lions, RollingStar holographic windowed security thread, watermark of lion and electrotype 50, colour-shifting security ink.

title: 100 Rand
description: The 2023 South African 100 Rand is a blue Eighth Series banknote featuring Nelson Mandela on the obverse and two Cape buffalo on the reverse.
body: The blue 100 Rand note entered circulation on 4 May 2023 as part of the SARB's Eighth Series security upgrade, which modernised all five denominations simultaneously. The obverse features Nelson Mandela's portrait with constitutional text and flag imagery arranged in rings, two Cape buffalo depicted in SPARK Live Truspin colour-shifting ink, and the national coat of arms with a hexagonal registration device containing a buffalo. The reverse depicts two Cape buffalo with a buffalo-in-hexagon registration device, measuring 146 x 70 mm.

highlights.design: Two Cape buffalo in SPARK Live Truspin colour-shifting ink on the obverse and two buffalo on the reverse, with a hexagonal registration device echoing the denomination's animal theme.
highlights.reason: Eighth Series security upgrade with enhanced SPARK Live Truspin and RollingStar windowed thread technology across all Mandela series denominations.
highlights.security: SPARK Live Truspin colour-shifting Cape buffalo, RollingStar holographic windowed security thread with coat of arms, SARB, buffalo and 100, watermark of Cape buffalo and electrotype 100.

title: 200 Rand
description: The 2023 South African 200 Rand is an orange Eighth Series banknote featuring Nelson Mandela on the obverse and a leopard with her cub on the reverse.
body: The 200 Rand note entered circulation on 4 May 2023 as the highest denomination in the SARB's Eighth Series upgrade, completing South Africa's most recent banknote security enhancement programme. The obverse displays Mandela's portrait flanked by constitutional preamble text and flag rings, with two leopards rendered in SPARK Live Truspin colour-shifting ink. The reverse depicts a mother leopard and her cub, continuing the family-group design philosophy of the Eighth Series and symbolising the leopard as the most elusive of the Big Five, printed on cotton-substrate paper measuring 152 x 70 mm.

highlights.design: Reverse features a mother leopard and cub as a family group, a design philosophy introduced in the Eighth Series to depict Big Five animals with their young.
highlights.reason: Eighth Series security upgrade, completing the overhaul of all five Mandela series denominations with advanced anti-counterfeiting technology.
highlights.security: SPARK Live Truspin colour-shifting leopard, RollingStar holographic windowed security thread, watermark of leopard and electrotype 200, cotton-substrate paper.

title: 10 Rand
description: The 2023 South African 10 Rand is a green Eighth Series banknote featuring Nelson Mandela on the obverse and two black rhinoceroses on the reverse.
body: The green 10 Rand entered circulation on 4 May 2023 as the lowest denomination of the SARB's Eighth Series upgrade, distinguished by emerald-green colouring that shifts to turquoise depending on the viewing angle. The obverse features Nelson Mandela's portrait with rings composed of the South African flag and constitutional text, the national coat of arms, and a black rhinoceros in SPARK Live Truspin colour-shifting ink at lower right. The reverse depicts two black rhinoceroses with a rhino-in-circle registration device, celebrating one of South Africa's most endangered Big Five species, measuring 128 x 70 mm.

highlights.design: Black rhinoceros in SPARK Live Truspin colour-shifting ink on the obverse, shifting from emerald green to turquoise when tilted, with two rhinos on the reverse.
highlights.reason: Eighth Series security upgrade introducing enhanced SPARK Live Truspin and RollingStar security thread technology to the lowest denomination of the Mandela series.
highlights.security: SPARK Live Truspin emerald-to-turquoise colour-shifting rhinoceros, RollingStar holographic windowed security thread with SARB and denomination, watermark of rhinoceros head and electrotype 10.

title: 500 South Sudanese Pounds
description: The 2023 South Sudan 500 South Sudanese Pounds banknote features the portrait of Dr. John Garang de Mabior and a Dinka warrior spear on the obverse, with the River Nile depicted on the reverse.
body: The 500 South Sudanese Pounds note was first introduced in 2018 by the Bank of South Sudan to ease high-value daily transactions amid inflationary pressure, and the 2023 edition represents an updated signature variant bearing new Governor and Minister signatures. The obverse honours Dr. John Garang de Mabior, the legendary leader of the Sudan People's Liberation Army and founding father of South Sudan's independence movement, alongside a traditional Dinka warrior spear as a symbol of national identity. The reverse features the River Nile, one of Africa's greatest waterways running through South Sudan, and the note measures 150 x 75 mm.

highlights.design: Portrait of Dr. John Garang de Mabior alongside a Dinka warrior spear on the obverse, with the River Nile on the reverse as a symbol of national geography and identity.
highlights.reason: 2023 signature update of the 500-pound denomination, first introduced in 2018 to provide a high-value denomination for daily commerce amid inflation.
highlights.security: Windowed security thread, watermark portrait of Dr. John Garang de Mabior, and security features for the partially sighted.

title: 1.000 South Sudanese Pounds
description: Remark.
body: The 200 Rand banknote from the 2023 Eighth Series features Nelson Mandela on the obverse with constitutional rings behind his head. The orange note displays a leopard and her cub on the reverse as part of the Big Five animal series. Security features include a dynamic color-changing thread displaying 'SARB,' denomination, and the leopard. The note includes a latent image visible when tilted and tactile marks on long edges.

highlights.reason: Part of 2023 Eighth Series refresh updating security and adding Big Five wildlife theme.
highlights.design: Leopard and cub representing South Africa's endangered wildlife alongside Nelson Mandela portrait.
highlights.security: Dynamic color-changing security thread with SPARK®Live Truspin movement and latent image denomination viewing.

title: 1 Rubl'
description: Alexander Suvorov, Gold Chervonets coin. 100th Anniversary of Gold Chervonets.
body: The 20 Kronor banknote features Swedish children's book author Astrid Lindgren and her character Pippi Longstocking. Purple-violet in color, measuring 120 × 66 mm, with thickness 125 micrometres. Introduced in 2015 by Sveriges Riksbank, it remains in current circulation. Intaglio printing creates a raised tactile surface on the portrait, denominations, and bank text to enhance authenticity and accessibility.

highlights.reason: Continuing circulation of 2015 series celebrating Swedish literary heritage and cultural figures.
highlights.design: Astrid Lindgren author portrait with Pippi Longstocking character emphasizing Swedish children's literature legacy.
highlights.security: Intaglio printing for raised surface, watermark with denomination and portrait, security thread, UV elements.

title: 20 Hriven'
description: Remark.
body: The 50 Kronor banknote features poet, composer, and artist Evert Taube with Bohuslän region on the reverse. Yellow-orange in color (126 × 66 mm), introduced in 2015. Intaglio printing creates raised surfaces for authentication. The banknote includes watermarks with denomination and portrait visible under light, a security thread, and UV-reactive elements. Part of the modernized Swedish banknote series celebrating 20th-century cultural figures.

highlights.reason: 2015 series continuing circulation celebrating Swedish artistic and musical heritage through cultural figures.
highlights.design: Evert Taube portrait with Bohuslän landscape representing Swedish cultural heritage and regional identity.
highlights.security: Intaglio printing, watermark with denomination and portrait, security thread, UV fibres fluorescing yellow and blue.

title: 1.000 Hriven'
description: Remark.
body: The 200 Kronor banknote features film director Ingmar Bergman with Gotland (Fårö), where Bergman had his home, on the reverse. Green colored (140 × 66 mm), introduced in 2015. The reverse includes imagery from Bergman's film 'The Seventh Seal,' showing Bergman in conversation with an actor portraying Death. Intaglio printing, watermarks, security threads, and UV elements provide authentication and accessibility.

highlights.reason: 2015 series honoring Swedish cinema and Ingmar Bergman's international contribution to film art.
highlights.design: Ingmar Bergman director portrait with The Seventh Seal film scene and Fårö island home location.
highlights.security: Intaglio printing with raised surface, watermark with denomination and portrait, security thread, UV elements.

Red colored (147 × 66 mm), introduced in 2016. The 2024 reference indicates continued circulation. Intaglio printing creates raised tactile surfaces. The note is part of the celebrated Swedish banknote series honoring 20th-century cultural achievements and artistic excellence.

title: 1.000 Pesos
description: Printer.
body: The 200 Franc banknote emphasizes Switzerland's scientific expertise with matter as its key motif. Issued since October 1, 1997, it remains in circulation. In November 2024, the Swiss National Bank announced development of a new banknote series, with the 200-franc note dedicated to the Alps theme. New designs will not be issued until the early 2030s at earliest, following a design competition launching February 2025.

highlights.reason: Current series continuing circulation while SNB develops next-generation designs featuring Alpine themes.
highlights.design: Matter and scientific motifs representing Swiss expertise and innovation in science and technology.
highlights.security: Advanced security features standard to Swiss National Bank denomination protection standards.

issuer: Banco Central de la República Argentina
title: 2.000 Pesos
description: Printer. Development of Science and Medicine in Argentina.
body: The 1000 Franc banknote from the historical sixth series (1978-2000) featured Auguste Forel, Swiss psychiatrist, neurologist, and myrmecologist (1848-1931), with three ants and a cross-section of an anthill motif. Purple colored (192 × 86 mm). Though withdrawn from circulation in 2000, these notes remain exchangeable at face value at Swiss National Bank cash desks. The 2024 reference may indicate continued exchange availability or commemorative interest.

highlights.reason: Historical series honoring Forel's groundbreaking research on brain structure and ant societies.
highlights.design: Auguste Forel portrait with ant and anthill motifs reflecting his pioneering myrmecological studies.
highlights.security: Sixth series security features; still exchangeable without time restriction at SNB.

highlights.design: King Rama X portrait with holographic elements and historical Rama VII and VIII portrayed on reverse.

title: 500 Taka
description: Sheikh Mujibur Rahman, National Martyrs' monument (Savar).
body: The 1000 Baht banknote features King Rama X on the obverse in formal regalia. No dedicated 2024 circulation issue was found; however, a 100 Baht polymer commemorative banknote was issued July 23, 2024, for the King's 6th Cycle Birthday (72nd birthday) with portraits in full regalia and ceremonial dress. The 1000 Baht remains in standard circulation with existing design portraying Rama X and historical royal themes.

highlights.design: King Rama X portrait in formal regalia; related 2024 commemorative issue showed ceremonial dress variants.

Limited to 200,000 copies celebrating 30 years of independent Czech and Slovak currencies following the successful 1993 currency split.

issuer: Banque de la République d'Haïti / Bank Repiblik Dayiti
title: 50 Gourdes / Goud
description: François Cappiox. Bicentennial of Independence of Haiti (1804-2004).
body: Commemorative 50 gourdes note celebrating Haiti's bicentennial of independence (1804-2004). Features François Cappoix (1766-1806), a revolutionary hero who earned the nickname 'Capois-la-Mort' for his extraordinary bravery at the Battle of Vertières, the final major engagement of the Haitian Revolution. Part of a series honoring Haiti's independence struggle.

highlights.reason: Commemoration of Haiti's bicentennial of independence in 2004, with reissuance in 2023
highlights.design: Note features François Cappoix, a legendary Haitian Revolutionary general known for his fearless combat leadership
highlights.security: Standard bicentennial commemorative note security features

issuer: Banque de la République d'Haïti / Bank Repiblik Dayiti
title: 100 Gourdes / Goud
description: Henri Christophe. Bicentennial of Independence of Haiti (1804-2004).
body: Commemorative 100 gourdes note honoring Henri Christophe (1767-1820), who served as President of Northern Haiti and later declared himself King Henri I of Haiti in 1811. Reverse features the Citadelle Laferrière fortress. This note continues Haiti's tradition of celebrating independence bicentennial through currency, recognizing key revolutionary leaders.

highlights.reason: Commemoration of Haiti's bicentennial of independence with focus on King Henri I's legacy
highlights.design: Note features Henri Christophe, important Haitian independence leader, with fortress imagery
highlights.security: Standard bicentennial series security features

issuer: Banque de la République d'Haïti / Bank Repiblik Dayiti
title: 250 Gourdes / Goud
description: Jean-Jacques Dessalines. Bicentennial of Independence of Haiti (1804-2004).
body: Commemorative 250 gourdes note featuring Jean-Jacques Dessalines (1758-1806), military leader and first ruler of independent Haiti under the 1805 constitution. Reverse shows Fort Décidé in Marchand. Dessalines led the final phase of the Haitian Revolution, defeating French forces at the Battle of Vertières in November 1803. Issued as part of Haiti's bicentennial independence celebration series.

highlights.reason: Commemoration of Haiti's bicentennial of independence (1804-2004) with 2023 reissuance
highlights.design: Note depicts Jean-Jacques Dessalines, founder and first emperor of independent Haiti
highlights.security: Standard bicentennial commemorative security features

issuer: Central Bank of the Islamic Republic of Iran
title: 1.000.000 Rials
description: Purpose.
body: High-denomination reissue addressing Iran's severe inflation crisis. Originally designed in 2008, the 1,000,000 rial note was reissued in 2023 as purchasing power eroded dramatically. At 1.6 million rials per US dollar, the note provides practical utility for daily transactions amid currency depreciation. Part of Central Bank's strategy to manage inflationary pressures through larger denominations.

highlights.reason: Response to severe inflation and currency depreciation requiring high-denomination circulation notes
highlights.design: High-denomination note designed to facilitate transactions amid accelerating price increases
highlights.security: Security features appropriate for high-value currency in inflation-stressed economy

issuer: Central Bank of the Islamic Republic of Iran
title: 2.000.000 Rials
description: Purpose.
body: New 2,000,000 rial denomination issued in 2023 to address Iran's accelerating inflation crisis. Worth approximately $47 USD at official rates but significantly less at market rates, reflecting currency depreciation. This highest-denomination note represents the Central Bank's ongoing effort to provide practical currency denominations as inflation erodes purchasing power faster than monetary policy can respond.

highlights.reason: Creation of new high denomination to address runaway inflation and currency collapse
highlights.design: Highest-denomination Iranian banknote reflecting modern economic pressures
highlights.security: Security features designed for highest-value circulating currency

title: 5.000 Dinars
description: Remark.
body: Standard circulation 5,000 dinar note issued with updated governor signature from Mustafa Ghaleb. Purple, blue and orange design (159 x 78 mm) features the Gali Ali Beg waterfall in Kurdish region on obverse and Al-Ukether fortress on reverse. Security thread includes color-shifting green-to-blue RollingStar Cube technology with accessibility marks for sight-impaired users.

highlights.reason: Updated signature variant reflecting new Central Bank governor
highlights.design: Purple, blue and orange design featuring Gali Ali Beg waterfall and Kurdish natural heritage
highlights.security: RollingStar Cube windowed security thread with color-shifting technology and accessibility features

title: 50.000 Dinars
description: Water wheel on the Euphrates river, Gali Ali Beg waterfall (Kurdistan).
body: High-denomination 50,000 dinar note (156 x 65 mm) in brown, green, yellow and blue. Obverse features Assyrian jar and water wheel on Euphrates River with Gali Ali Beg waterfall; reverse depicts fisherman in marshes with map of Iraq and Euphrates-Tigris river systems. Features RollingStar Cube security thread with Samarra's Malwiya Tower varifeye element and horse head watermark.

highlights.reason: Issued 2023 to represent Iraq's natural heritage and water resources
highlights.design: Brown, green, yellow and blue note showing Euphrates water wheel and Iraqi marshlands heritage
highlights.security: RollingStar Cube windowed thread with spiral minaret and Malwiya Tower registration device

title: 10.000 Tenge
description: Tenge-coin, Möbius strip. 30th Anniversary of Tenge-Currency.
body: Purple and blue commemorative note (149 x 79 mm) released November 15, 2023, celebrating 30 years of the tenge currency. Obverse features Kazakhstan's national emblem, Mobius strip, and map with traditional regional designs; reverse shows Eurasian continent outline, tenge coin, and Mobius strip. Limited edition of 3,000,000 copies legally required to be accepted at face value throughout Kazakhstan.

highlights.reason: Commemoration of 30th anniversary of tenge currency establishment (1993-2023)
highlights.design: Purple and blue design with Mobius strip symbolizing currency continuity and traditional Kazakh regional patterns
highlights.security: Windowed security thread with color-shifting purple-to-green technology and tenge symbol watermark

title: 5.000 Tenge
description: Tree of Life with bird, Golden eagle detail, DNA spiral, arms.
body: New 5,000 tenge note (140 x 70 mm) launched December 25, 2023, as the first denomination in the 'Saka Style' series. Obverse features Tree of Life branch with hovering bird from the Golden Man's headgear, DNA spiral, and golden eagle artifact from ancient Kazakh archaeology. Reverse displays golden eagle with outspread wings and Charyn Canyon landscape within ornamental infinity pattern.

highlights.reason: Introduction of new 'Saka Style' series celebrating ancient Kazakh cultural and historical heritage
highlights.design: Archaeological motifs including golden eagle, Tree of Life, and DNA spiral representing connection between past and future
highlights.security: Features reflect Kazakh historical artifacts and landscape elements from Berel Kurgans and Taldy Kurgan sites

title: 20 Som
description: Togolok Moldo.
body: The 20 Som note issued by Kyrgyz Banky represents a 2023 emission in Kyrgyzstan's currency circulation. This denomination supports transactions throughout the economy and reflects the central bank's commitment to modern, secure currency production. The note incorporates contemporary anti-counterfeiting measures including advanced security threads, microprinting, and color-shifting elements. As a 2023 variant, this issue maintains design consistency with the established series while incorporating updated printing specifications. The note is essential for everyday commerce and maintains compatibility with existing currency circulation infrastructure.

description: Printer.
body: Red polymer note (132 x 65 mm) from 'G' series issued 2020-2023. Features Sor Juana Ines de la Cruz and patio of San Ildefonso College on obverse; monarch butterflies and Monarch Butterfly Biosphere Reserve on reverse. Third 'G' series release and first to use polymer substrate in vertical format, advancing Mexico's currency modernization.

highlights.design: Red polymer note honoring Sor Juana Ines de la Cruz and Mexican biodiversity through monarch butterfly imagery
highlights.security: Polymer substrate provides enhanced durability and counterfeiting protection compared to paper notes

description: Printer.
body: Horizontal gray cotton-substrate circulation note featuring three Mexican Revolution figures: President Francisco I. Madero, Hermila Galindo, and Carmen Serdán. Issued 2019-2023 by Banco de Mexico. Represents the historical process of Mexican Revolution with updated security features and design elements celebrating Mexico's political transformation.

description: Remark.

Obverse features Chinggis Khan, a paiza, and national coat of arms; reverse displays Flags of the Golden Horde. Note includes solid security thread and watermark of Chinggis Khan with electrotype МБ, with modified security thread for updated 2023 issue.

description: University Al Quaraouiyine (Fez); King Mohamed VI.
body: University Al Quaraouiyine (Fez); King Mohamed VI. Issued by Bank Al-Maghrib, the 20 Dirhams from 2023 represents Morocco's contemporary currency circulation. This denomination supports both retail and commercial transactions throughout the economy. The note incorporates modern security printing technology including enhanced color registration, microprinting elements, and updated security thread specifications. As a 2023 variant, this issue maintains visual consistency with the established series design while incorporating refined production techniques. The banknote serves as both a functional medium of exchange and a representation of national identity and monetary authority.

description: King Mohammed VI, arcades of Hassan II mosque (Casablanca), arms.
body: Red, blue, green, brown, orange and yellow circulation note launched November 24, 2023. Obverse features King Mohammed VI with Moroccan coat of arms and architectural details from Hassan II Mosque arcades in Casablanca. Reverse depicts Mechouar Place in Laayoune and Tiznit-Dakhla expressway. Celebrates Sahara's cultural heritage and socio-economic development under royal guidance.

highlights.design: Multi-colored note featuring King Mohammed VI and Hassan II Mosque architectural elements with Saharan development themes
highlights.security: Three-windowed security thread with color-shifting magnetic ink and intaglio printing

title: 200 Dirhams
description: King Mohammed VI, Mohammed VI bridge over river Bouregreg (Rabat), arms.
body: Blue, yellow and orange circulation note released January 12, 2024, timed to 80th anniversary of Independence Manifesto. Obverse features King Mohammed VI, Mohammed VI Bridge (largest cable-stayed bridge in Africa connecting Rabat-Salé), architectural doors, and coat of arms. Reverse shows Mohammed VI Tower and Marrakesh-Manara International Airport, celebrating Morocco's infrastructure and industrial development.

highlights.reason: Celebration of Morocco's infrastructure achievements and 80th anniversary of Independence Manifesto
highlights.design: Note featuring King Mohammed VI and iconic Mohammed VI Bridge, highlighting modern development themes
highlights.security: Advanced security features including color-shifting elements and intaglio printing

title: 20.000 Kyats
description: Printer.
body: Myanmar's 20,000 Kyat banknote, issued in 2023, represents the first commemorative note and highest denomination released by the Central Bank of Myanmar. The green polymer note (150 × 70 mm) honors the completion of the Maravijaya Buddha Image and the first birthday anniversary of Royal White Elephant Ratha Nandaka. The obverse features an elephant in Myanmar artistic style with watermark; the reverse displays Sagaing and Ayeyawady Bridges, with a security thread showing dynamic color shift from gold to green when tilted.

highlights.reason: First Myanmar commemorative banknote marking completion of Maravijaya Buddha Image and royal elephant's birthday.
highlights.design: Green note featuring traditional Yadana white elephant on obverse and iconic Sagaing and Ayeyawady bridges on reverse.
highlights.security: Dynamic security thread changes from gold to green when tilted, with tactile features for visually impaired users.

title: 100 Namibia Dollars
description: Captain Hendrik Witbooi, Parliament building (Windhoek).
body: Bank of Namibia's 100 Namibian Dollar note from the 2012 series features Captain Hendrik Witbooi, a national hero and Khoikhoi leader (1830–1905), against the Parliament building in Windhoek on the obverse. The reverse displays three oryx with an enlarged head detail, National Coat of Arms, and denomination. The upgraded security features include Securicoat varnish, optical variation ink (OVI), and Janus colour-shifting window threads. Emerald green to magenta/blue security thread bears demetalized BON 100.

highlights.reason: 2012 series modernization honoring Namibian national hero Captain Hendrik Witbooi with enhanced security.
highlights.design: Witbooi portrait with Parliament building Windhoek on front; three oryx with coat of arms on reverse.
highlights.security: Janus colour-shifting window with demetalized BON 100, Securicoat varnish, and optical variation ink.

title: 75 Rupees
description: State Bank of Pakistan building Karachi. 75th Anniversary of State Bank of Pakistan (1948-2023).
body: State Bank of Pakistan's 75 Rupees commemorative banknote, issued in July 2023, celebrates the 75th anniversary of the SBP (1948–2023). The blue-green note (139 × 65 mm) features the State Bank of Pakistan building in Karachi on the obverse with Mohammed Ali Jinnah portrait, and wind turbines with Mohtrama Fatima Jinnah on reverse. Security features include a Pureimage windowed holographic security thread with demetallized 75 and electrotype watermark of Jinnah bearing 75.

highlights.reason: Commemorates 75th anniversary of State Bank of Pakistan (1948–2023) with specially designed note.
highlights.design: SBP Karachi building with Jinnah portrait on obverse; wind turbines symbolizing progress and national female figure on reverse.
highlights.security: Pureimage windowed holographic security thread with demetallized 75 and Jinnah watermark electrotype.

title: 2 Kina
description: Size.
body: Bank of Papua New Guinea's 2 Kina banknote issued from 2017–2023 represents a size variant within the polymer currency series. The green polymer note (125 × 70 mm) features the National Parliament building and national coat of arms on the obverse, while the reverse displays cultural artifacts including Mount Hagan ax, Kula armband, dogtooth necklace, Sepik clay pot, and tapa cloth patterns. Multiple variants (P-50a.1 in 2017, P-50b in 2020, P-50c in 2023) reflect ongoing currency modernization.

highlights.reason: Size variant issued across 2017–2023 period reflecting PNG's continued polymer banknote modernization.
highlights.design: Parliamentary building and coat of arms; cultural artifacts including traditional weaponry, ornaments, pottery, and tapa cloth.
highlights.security: Polymer substrate with durable modern security features; Parliament building watermark and security thread elements.

title: 100 Kina
description: Size.
body: Bank of Papua New Guinea's 100 Kina banknote issued in 2021 and 2023 represents size variants in PNG's polymer currency series. The note features indigenous cultural symbols and fauna reflecting PNG's biodiversity, including the renowned Bird of Paradise. Since 1991, Papua New Guinea has produced all banknotes on polymer substrate, providing enhanced durability and security. These variants maintain design consistency while incorporating updated security features.

highlights.reason: Size variants issued 2021 and 2023 as part of PNG's ongoing polymer banknote currency modernization.
highlights.design: Features cultural artifacts and indigenous fauna including the endemic Bird of Paradise.
highlights.security: Polymer substrate since 1991 offers improved durability; modern security features integrated in both variants.

title: 50 Kina
description: Parliament building, Port Moresby. 50 Years Bank of Papua New Guinea (1973-2023).
body: Bank of Papua New Guinea's 50 Kina commemorative banknote, issued November 2023, celebrates the 50th anniversary of the central bank (1973–2023). The polymer note features Parliament House in Port Moresby on the obverse alongside a Bird of Paradise on a Kundu drum with ceremonial spear. The reverse depicts Prime Minister Michael Somare with cultural artifacts. The commemorative design includes a 50-year golden anniversary emblem, distinguishing it from regular 50 Kina circulation notes.

highlights.reason: Commemorates 50th anniversary of Bank of Papua New Guinea (1973–2023) with special anniversary emblem.
highlights.design: Parliament building with Raggiana Bird-of-Paradise, Kundu drum, and spear; reverse features PM Somare and cultural artifacts.
highlights.security: Polymer construction; 50-year golden anniversary emblem on upper left corner as distinguishing security/design feature.

title: 50 Piso
description: Remark.
body:
The Philippine 50 Piso paper banknote from the New Generation Currency (NGC) series, with 2023-2025 date variants, features President Sergio Osmeña (1878-1961), the fourth president of the Philippines from 1944 to 1946, on its red obverse. The note depicts historical elements including the First Philippine Assembly in 1907 and General Douglas MacArthur's landing in Leyte. The reverse side showcases Taal Lake (also known as Bombón Lake) in Batangas province and the giant trevally fish (Caranx ignobilis), also known locally as maliputo. The 2023 version bears the signatures of President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. and BSP Governor Felipe Medalla.

This banknote represents the New Generation Currency series, an enhanced version of Philippine paper currency with improved security features and accessibility improvements for visually impaired users, including embossed tactile marks. The note measures 160 mm x 66 mm and includes a solid security thread and watermark featuring Sergio Osmeña.

highlights.reason: Honors President Sergio Osmeña and commemorates Philippine democratic heritage through historical imagery of the First Parliament and Liberation.
highlights.design: Features Sergio Osmeña and First Parliament on obverse; Taal Lake and giant trevally (maliputo) fish on reverse.
highlights.security: Includes solid security thread, watermark of Sergio Osmeña with electrotype 50, enhanced color-changing security threads, and embossed tactile marks for visually impaired users.

title: 100 Piso
description: Remark.
body: Bangko Sentral ng Pilipinas' 100 Piso circulation banknotes from 2022 and 2023 represent signature variants reflecting central bank leadership changes. The 2022 notes bear signatures of President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. and BSP Governor Felipe Medalla, while 2023 versions maintain similar designs with updated printing dates. These cotton-abacá paper notes feature design elements honoring Philippine heritage and history. The signature updates reflect the adoption of the redesigned BSP logo introduced in January 2021 on all NGC banknotes.

highlights.reason: Signature variants (2022–2023) reflect BSP governor change from Medalla to subsequent leadership.
highlights.design: Features Philippine heritage and national symbols consistent with prior 100 Piso designs.
highlights.security: Updated BSP logo design implemented 2021; standard security features of cotton-abacá paper substrate.

title: 100 Piso
description: Remark.
body:
The Philippine 100 Piso paper banknote from the New Generation Currency (NGC) series, with 2023-2025 date variants, features President Manuel A. Roxas on its violet obverse. The front also includes imagery of the Central Bank of the Philippines headquarters in Manila and the inauguration of the Third Republic on July 4, 1946, commemorating Roxas's presidency and role in Philippine independence. The reverse side features the Mayon Volcano in Legazpi City, Albay, a distinctive and iconic active stratovolcano, along with the whale shark (Rhincodon typus), the world's largest fish species. The reverse design also incorporates Bicol Region textile patterns.

This paper banknote version features enhanced security including a color-changing security thread added in 2020 and includes embossed tactile features for the visually impaired. The note measures 160 mm x 66 mm and incorporates a watermark of Manuel A. Roxas with electrotype 100, representing the continuation of the NGC series with modernized security features.

highlights.reason: Honors President Manuel A. Roxas and the founding of the Third Republic of the Philippines through historical and natural imagery.
highlights.design: Features Manuel A. Roxas and Third Republic imagery on obverse; Mayon Volcano and whale shark on reverse with regional textile design.
highlights.security: Includes watermark, color-changing security threads, enhanced indigenous pattern security elements, and embossed tactile marks for visually impaired accessibility.

title: 20 Złotych
description: Printer. 550th Anniversary of birth of Nicolaus Copernicus (1473-2023).
body: Narodowy Bank Polski's 20 Złotych commemorative polymer banknote, issued February 2023, honors the 550th anniversary of Nicolaus Copernicus' birth (1473–2023). The blue note (150 × 77 mm) features Copernicus' portrait alongside stylized quadrant and heliocentric system depictions on the obverse. The reverse displays historical coins and heliocentric system imagery. Designed by Krystian Michalczuk with limited mintage of 100,000, it commemorates the astronomer's contributions to science and monetary economics.

highlights.reason: Commemorates 550th anniversary of Nicolaus Copernicus' birth (1473–2023), honoring the famous Polish astronomer.
highlights.design: Copernicus portrait with quadrant and heliocentric system; reverse shows historical coins and system fragments.
highlights.security: Polymer construction; limited mintage of 100,000; design by renowned artist Krystian Michalczuk.

title: 1.000 Rubley
description: Remark.
body: Bank Rossii's 1,000 Rubley banknote, issued October 2023, features Nizhny Novgorod and the Volga Federal District. The turquoise-blue note displays the Nikolskaya Tower of Nizhny Novgorod Kremlin, the Oka-Volga river spit, Nizhny Novgorod Fair, and stadium on the obverse. Following Russian Orthodox Church objections to the design's religious imagery on the reverse, the Bank suspended issuance. A revised design was implemented in December 2025.

highlights.reason: Dedicates banknote to Nizhny Novgorod region as part of Russia's 2022–2025 regional modernization program.
highlights.design: Nikolskaya Tower with Kremlin, river spit, fair grounds, and stadium representing Volga Federal District.
highlights.security: Modified design following public concerns; revised version issued December 2025 after design adjustment.

title: 5.000 Rubley
description: Europe-Asia monument and Sevastyanov house, Ekaterinburg.
body: Bank Rossii's 5,000 Rubley banknote, issued October 2023, dedicates the denomination to Yekaterinburg and the Urals Federal District. The multi-colored note (157 × 69 mm) features the Europe-Asia stele in Ekaterinburg on the obverse alongside city skyline imagery. The reverse depicts the 'Tale about the Urals' monument in Chelyabinsk, pine trees, Urals Federal District map, and oil infrastructure. Security features include a 5.5 mm holographic security thread with demetalized 5000 CBRF and architectural watermark.

highlights.reason: Commemorates Urals Federal District within Russia's regional redesign program (2022–2025).
highlights.design: Europe-Asia stele and Ekaterinburg skyline on obverse; 'Tale about Urals' monument, Chelyabinsk, and oil industry on reverse.
highlights.security: 5.5 mm holographic security thread with demetalized 5000 and architectural watermark with electrotype security features.

issuer: Faletupe Tutotonu o Samoa / Central Bank of Samoa
title: 60 Tala
description: Prime Minister Fiame Naomi Mata'afa, flag. 60th Anniversary of Independence (1962-2022).
body: Central Bank of Samoa's 60 Tala commemorative banknote, issued May 2023, celebrates Samoa's 60th anniversary of independence (1962–2022). The brown, red, and blue note honors Prime Minister Fiame Naomi Matāa'fa, the first female PM, on the obverse while the reverse depicts Samoa's first PM, Fiame Matāa'fa Faumuina Mulinuu II, lowering the New Zealand flag in 1962. The note features a father-daughter prime ministerial legacy spanning 60 years. Security features include a windowed security thread with demetalized 60 and ginger flower watermark.

highlights.reason: Commemorates 60th anniversary of Samoa independence (1962–2022) honoring first female Prime Minister.
highlights.design: Current PM Fiame Naomi Matāa'fa obverse; first PM ancestor on reverse lowering flag at independence.
highlights.security: Windowed security thread with demetalized 60; ginger flower watermark electrotype; legal tender circulation.

issuer: Faletupe Tutotonu o Samoa / Central Bank of Samoa
title: 5 Tala
description: Beach.
body: Central Bank of Samoa's 5 Tala polymer banknote, issued December 2023, modernizes the currency from cotton-based paper to polymer substrate. The red multi-colored note features Samoa's pristine sandy beaches reflecting the nation's tourism-driven economy on the obverse. The reverse depicts Vailima, the residence of Scottish author Robert Louis Stevenson (Tusitala, 'Teller of Tales'), honoring his literary legacy in Samoan culture. Security features include a Tueila flower transparent window and GEMINI/BLINK fluorescent security elements.

highlights.reason: Polymer substrate upgrade (December 2023) modernizing Tala denominations from paper to polymer.
highlights.design: Samoa's sandy beaches symbolizing tourism economy on obverse; Vailima house of Robert Louis Stevenson on reverse.
highlights.security: Transparent Tueila flower window; GEMINI fluorescent imagery under UV; BLINK phosphorescent green denomination.

title: 500 South Sudanese Pounds
description: Remark.
body: Bank of South Sudan's 500 South Sudanese Pounds banknote, initially issued in 2018 to address inflation, received signature updates through 2023. The note features revolutionary leader Dr. John Garang de Mabior with a Dinka warrior spear on the obverse, representing the nation's independence struggle. The reverse depicts the River Nile, central to South Sudanese geography. The 2023 variant incorporates updated signatures and date, reflecting changes in central bank governance while maintaining original design elements.

highlights.design: Dr. John Garang de Mabior and Dinka warrior spear on obverse; River Nile on reverse.
highlights.security: Standard banknote security features; signature update and 2023 date variant distinguish latest issues.

title: 1.000 South Sudanese Pounds
description: Remark.
body: Bank of South Sudan's 1,000 South Sudanese Pounds banknote, issued February 2021 to combat hyperinflation, received signature updates through 2023. The note (158 × 82 mm) features Dr. John Garang de Mabior and a Dinka warrior spear on the obverse, representing the nation's liberation struggle. The reverse depicts two ostriches with denomination, reflecting South Sudan's wildlife. The 2023 variant incorporates updated signatures, reflecting changes in central bank leadership while addressing the country's ongoing economic crisis.

highlights.design: Dr. John Garang de Mabior with Dinka warrior spear on obverse; two ostriches and denomination on reverse.

title: 1 Rubl'
description: Generalissimo Suvorov, Gold Chervonets coin. 100th Anniversary of Gold Chervonets.
body: Pridnestrovskiy Respublikanskiy Bank's 1 Rubl' commemorative banknote, issued September 2023, celebrates the 100th anniversary of the Soviet Golden Chervonets coin (1923–2023). Limited to 2,500 notes in collector folders, the note features Generalissimo Alexander Vasilievich Suvorov (1730–1800), recognized founder of Tiraspol. The commemorative overprint appears over the watermark area, marking this significant numismatic milestone. The design honors Suvorov's military legacy and Transnistria's historical connection to the legendary general.

highlights.reason: Commemorates 100th anniversary of Soviet Golden Chervonets coin (1923–2023) with special collector-only release.
highlights.design: Generalissimo Alexander Suvorov, founder of Tiraspol, with commemorative overprint over watermark.
highlights.security: Limited mintage 2,500 in collector folders; commemorative overprint distinguishes this special issue from circulation currency.

title: 20 Hriven'
description: Remark.
body: National Bank of Ukraine's 20 Hriven' banknote, redesigned in 2018 and reprinted in 2023, features poet Ivan Franko on the obverse. The green note (130 × 69 mm) from the 2023 printing bears the signature of NBU Governor Andriy Pyshnyy, reflecting leadership change. The banknote retains core design elements from the 2003 original while incorporating the 2018 redesign improvements. A 2023 variant marks the 1st anniversary of the Russia-Ukraine armed conflict, with both versions legal tender and circulating.

highlights.reason: 2023 signature update reflects new NBU governor Andriy Pyshnyy leadership; 2018 redesign retained.
highlights.design: Ivan Franko portrait on obverse; retained 2018 redesign elements maintaining design continuity.
highlights.security: Standard security features from 2018 redesign; signature variant distinguishes 2023 printing from prior issues.

title: 1.000 Hriven'
description: Remark.
body: National Bank of Ukraine's 1,000 Hriven' banknote, introduced October 2019, features scientist Volodymyr Vernadskyi on the obverse with the Presidium of Ukraine's National Academy of Sciences on the reverse. Dimensions 75 × 160 mm. Signature updates through 2023 reflect changing NBU governors—Kyrylo Shevchenko (2021) and Andriy Pyshnyy (2023). A 2024 modified version added the patriotic slogan 'СЛАВА УКРАЇНІ! ГЕРОЯМ СЛАВА!' in dark-brown ink on the reverse.

highlights.reason: Signature updates (2021, 2023) reflect successive NBU governor changes; 2024 variant adds patriotic slogan.
highlights.design: Volodymyr Vernadskyi scientist portrait on obverse; National Academy Presidium on reverse with patriotic inscription.
highlights.security: Standard security features; 2024 modification added dark-brown patriotic slogan on reverse as design element.

description: Printer.
body: The 10 dollar banknote was updated in December 2022 with a shift from yellow-green to grey coloring to distinguish it from other denominations. title: 100 Taka
description: Sheikh Mujibur Rahman, Padma Bridge. Padma Bridge - Symbol of National Pride.
body: A commemorative 100 taka note issued by Bangladesh Bank on 26 June 2022 to mark the opening of the Padma Multipurpose Bridge. The obverse depicts Bangabandhu Sheikh Mujibur Rahman and Prime Minister Sheikh Hasina Wazed with the bridge in the background; the reverse showcases the iconic Padma Bridge. Measuring 146 x 63 mm, signed by Governor Fazle Kabir, celebrating this major infrastructure achievement.

title: 2.000 Rupiah
description: Mohammad Hoesni Thamrin, arms.
body:
The 2,000 Rupiah note from the 2022 Bank Indonesia series features Mohammad Hoesni Thamrin, a prominent Indonesian nationalist and independence leader. The obverse displays his portrait along with Indonesia's national coat of arms and a map of the nation. The reverse showcases three elements reflecting Indonesian cultural identity: the Tari Piring, a traditional plate dance from West Sumatra, Ngarai Sianok canyon landscape near Bukittinggi, and the Jeumpa flower (champak/Magnolia champaca).

The note measures 126 mm × 65 mm with dominant colors of slate grey and violet. Printed on cotton fiber paper, it was released on 17 August 2022, bearing signatures of Governor Perry Warjiyo and Finance Minister Sri Mulyani Indrawati. The color palette shifts through blue and gray tones, offering visual distinctiveness from other denominations.

Security elements include a solid security thread with "BANK INDONESIA" text, watermark of Mohammad Hoesni Thamrin, and ultraviolet-reactive components integrated throughout the design for anti-counterfeiting protection.

highlights.reason: Honors Mohammad Hoesni Thamrin, a key nationalist leader in Indonesia's independence struggle.
highlights.design: Reverse features Tari Piring (plate dance), Ngarai Sianok canyon landscape in West Sumatra, and Jeumpa (champak) flowers.
highlights.security: Solid security thread with printed text, watermark of the featured hero, and UV fluorescence technology.

title: 1.000 Rupiah
description: Tjut Meutia, arms.
body:
The 1,000 Rupiah banknote from the 2022 Bank Indonesia series honors Tjut Meutia (1870–1910), an Indonesian national heroine and independence fighter. The obverse features her portrait alongside the Indonesian coat of arms and a map of Indonesia. The reverse depicts three cultural and geographical elements: Fort Belgica in Banda Neira located in the Spice Islands, an orchid flower, and the Tari Tifa, a traditional dance where performers play hand drums.

Measuring 121 mm × 65 mm, the note displays colors of yellow, green, orange, purple, and red. It was issued on 17 August 2022, signed by Bank Indonesia Governor Perry Warjiyo and Finance Minister Sri Mulyani Indrawati. The banknote is printed on cotton fiber paper.

Security features include an Omron ring pattern, a solid security thread with "BANK INDONESIA" printed text, a bank logo registration device, and a watermark portraying Tjut Meutia. The 2022 series incorporates ultraviolet fluorescence and phosphorescent technology for enhanced authentication across all denominations.

highlights.reason: Commemorates Tjut Meutia, a female independence heroine of the Aceh resistance movement.
highlights.design: Reverse features Fort Belgica in Banda Neira, a Tari Tifa dancer with drum, and orchid flowers representing Indonesia's archipelago heritage.
highlights.security: Solid security thread with printed text, Omron rings, watermark of Tjut Meutia, and UV fluorescence elements.

title: 5.000 Rupiah
description: Dr. K. H. Idham Chalid, arms.
body:
The 5,000 Rupiah banknote from the 2022 series honors Dr. K.H. Idham Chalid, a prominent Islamic scholar and independence advocate. The obverse features his portrait with Indonesia's coat of arms and a tuberose flower at the bottom-left, accompanied by a map and bank logo registration device. The reverse displays a tricolor design in orange, blue, and pink tones, depicting the Tari Gambyong dancer (with traditional scarves), Mount Bromo volcano in East Java, and tuberose flowers (Polianthes tuberosa), representing Indonesia's natural beauty and cultural heritage.

Measuring 131 mm × 65 mm, the note is predominantly orange-brown and tan with dark brown printing on the multicolor underprint. Issued on 17 August 2022, it is signed by Governor Perry Warjiyo and Finance Minister Sri Mulyani Indrawati. The banknote is manufactured from cotton fiber paper for durability.

Security features include a solid security thread with printed "BANK INDONESIA" text, a watermark of Dr. K.H. Idham Chalid with electrotype 5, and ultraviolet fluorescence technology integrated throughout for authentication purposes.

highlights.reason: Commemorates Dr. K.H. Idham Chalid, a respected Islamic scholar and supporter of Indonesian independence.
highlights.design: Reverse depicts Tari Gambyong traditional scarf dancer, Mount Bromo volcano of East Java, and tuberose flowers.
highlights.security: Solid security thread with text, watermark of the honoree with electrotype, and UV fluorescence elements.

title: 10.000 Rupiah
description: Frans Kaisiepo, arms.
body:
The 10,000 Rupiah note from the 2022 Bank Indonesia series features Frans Kaisiepo, a Papuan national hero and political leader who championed Papua's integration into the Indonesian republic. The obverse displays his portrait alongside Indonesia's Garuda Pancasila coat of arms, a map, the Cempaka Hutan Kasar flower, and Asmat Papua traditional motifs reflecting indigenous cultural heritage. The reverse showcases the Tari Pakarena, a traditional masked fan dance from South Sulawesi, combined with Wakatobi National Park scenery and Cempaka Hutan Kasar flowers.

The banknote measures 135–136 mm × 65 mm in predominantly purple, orange, and pink colors. Issued on 17 August 2022, it bears the signatures of Governor Perry Warjiyo and Finance Minister Sri Mulyani Indrawati, printed on cotton fiber paper. The 2022 series incorporates advanced security features including Rapid HD® micro lenses and OVMI Spark® technology on larger denominations for enhanced anti-counterfeiting capabilities.

Security elements include a solid security thread, watermark of Frans Kaisiepo, and both overt ultraviolet fluorescence and covert phosphorescent technology throughout the design for comprehensive authentication protection.

highlights.reason: Honors Frans Kaisiepo, a Papuan national hero and champion of Indonesian unity and regional integration.
highlights.design: Reverse features Tari Pakarena masked fan dance from South Sulawesi, Wakatobi National Park landscape, and Cempaka flowers.
highlights.security: Solid security thread, watermark with electrotype, Rapid HD® microlenses, and UV/phosphorescent technology.

title: 20.000 Rupiah
description: Dr. G. S. S. J. Ratulangi, arms.
body:
The 20,000 Rupiah note was introduced by Bank Indonesia on 18 August 2022 as part of the seven-denomination 2022 Rupiah series, unveiled around Indonesia's 77th Independence Day. The obverse features Dr. Gerungan Saul Samuel Jacob Ratulangi, national hero and first governor of Sulawesi, alongside the coat of arms of Indonesia, an outline of the archipelago, and the black orchid (Coelogyne pandurata). The reverse depicts a Gong dancer, scenery of the Derawan Islands in East Kalimantan, and repeated black orchid motifs.

The banknote measures 141 × 65 mm in predominantly green tones. Security elements include a windowed security thread with demetalized denomination marking, an embedded watermark with electrotype, microlenses, and optically variable ink with dynamic motion effects visible when the note is tilted. The 2022-series notes carry date and signature variants through 2026.

highlights.reason: Part of Bank Indonesia's 2022 Rupiah series honoring national heroes, with date variants through 2026.
highlights.design: Front: Dr. G. S. S. J. Ratulangi with arms and the black orchid; back: Gong dancer and Derawan Islands scenery.
highlights.security: Windowed demetalized security thread, electrotype watermark, microlenses, and optically variable ink with motion effects.

title: 100.000 Rupiah
description: Soekarno and Mohammed Hatta, arms.
body:
The 100,000 Rupiah banknote from the 2022 Bank Indonesia series honors Indonesia's first president Sukarno (Ir. Soekarno) and first vice president Mohammad Hatta, co-founding leaders of the Indonesian republic. The obverse displays their portraits side-by-side alongside the national coat of arms, a map of Indonesia, and moth orchid flowers. The reverse depicts three iconic elements: the Tari Topeng Betawi (a masked dance from the Betawi people of Jakarta), Raja Ampat island scenery, and moon orchid flowers (Phalaenopsis amabilis), showcasing Indonesia's cultural and natural diversity.

Measuring 151 mm × 66 mm, the note displays red as its dominant color with multicolor designs throughout. Issued on 17 August 2022, it is signed by Governor Perry Warjiyo and Finance Minister Sri Mulyani Indrawati. The 2022 series features innovative security enhancements including Rapid HD® detect micro lenses embedded in kawung batik motifs and the Bank Indonesia logo, plus OVMI Spark® technology with dynamic motion effects in the orchid flower designs.

Security features include a windowed security thread with demetallized "BI 100000" text, watermark of Wage Rudolf Soepratman and electrotype logo, bank logo registration device, and comprehensive ultraviolet and phosphorescent technology across all denominations.

highlights.reason: Honors Sukarno and Mohammad Hatta, Indonesia's founding leaders and architects of independence and unity.
highlights.design: Reverse features Tari Topeng Betawi masked dance, Raja Ampat island landscape, and moon orchid (Phalaenopsis) flowers.
highlights.security: Windowed security thread with demetallized text, Rapid HD® microlenses with motion effects, OVMI Spark® in flowers, and UV/phosphorescent elements.

title: 5 Dinars
description: King Abdullah I Ibn Hussain, Khazne al-Firaun (Treasury of the Pharao), Petra.
body:
The Central Bank of Jordan 5 Dinars banknote, issued in 2022 (1443H), honors King Abdullah I Ibn Hussain and features the Treasury of Petra (Al-Khazneh), one of the most iconic archaeological sites in the region. The front displays a portrait of King Abdullah with the rock-cut architecture of the Treasury against an orange, red, and brown color scheme. The reverse depicts a camel caravan flanking the pediment of the Treasury, complementing the archaeological theme and representing Jordan's cultural heritage and historical significance.

The note incorporates advanced security features reflecting modern banknote printing standards, including both solid and windowed security threads. The windowed security thread features demetalalized text reading "CBJ 5". A watermark of King Abdullah Bin Al-Hussein appears throughout the note, along with electrotype Arabic text for additional authentication. Registration devices in the form of crowns are positioned on both obverse and reverse, while an Optically Variable Ink (OVI) security patch enhances protection against counterfeiting.

The 5 Dinars denomination remains a standard circulation note in Jordan's currency system and reflects the Central Bank's commitment to security and artistic representation of the nation's heritage through numismatic design.

highlights.reason: Features King Abdullah I Ibn Hussain with the Treasury of Petra, a UNESCO World Heritage site and symbol of Jordan's historical and cultural importance.
highlights.design: Front: king portrait with rock-cut Treasury architecture on orange-red-brown background; reverse: camel caravan and Treasury pediment with crown registration devices.
highlights.security: Includes solid and windowed security threads, watermark of king, electrotype Arabic text, OVI patch, and crown registration devices for comprehensive counterfeiting protection.

title: 1.000 Piso
description: Philippine eagle (Pithecophega jefferyi).
body:
The Philippine 1000 Piso polymer banknote, first issued in April 2022 and continuing through 2025, features two national symbols: the Philippine eagle (Pithecophaga jefferyi) and the Sampaguita flower (Jasminum sambac). The Philippine eagle represents Filipino uniqueness, strength, power, love of freedom, and vision for the nation's future. The Sampaguita symbolizes purity, simplicity, humility, and strength. The reverse side features the Tubbataha Reefs Natural Park, a UNESCO World Heritage Site recognized for its exceptional coral reef ecosystem and marine biodiversity, along with the South Sea Pearl, representing the Philippines' maritime heritage.

Launched on April 6, 2022, this was the first polymer banknote issued by the Bangko Sentral ng Pilipinas and earned the IBNS Banknote of the Year 2022 award. It continues to circulate alongside the new First Philippine Polymer (FPP) series introduced in December 2024, representing a significant advancement in Philippine banknote technology and durability, with polymer notes lasting up to 7.5 years compared to 1.5 years for paper notes.

highlights.security: Incorporates 11 security features including rolling bar effect, tactile dots, embossed prints, sampaguita clear window, shadow thread, vertical clear window with metallic features, blue iridescent figure, and polymer substrate.

This commemorates Hebrew poets of the 20th century.

The green banknote displays his portrait and historical Hashemite silver coins on the obverse; the reverse depicts three camel riders with the flag of the Arab Revolt. Issued from 2002 onward, it carries strong symbolism of Arab independence and historical significance.

title: 100.000 Tögrög
description: Remark. 100th Anniversary of Peoples Revolution (1921-2021) and Tögög-Currency (1925-2025).
body:
The Mongolbank 100,000 Tögrög commemorative banknote was issued to celebrate the 100th anniversary of the People's Revolution, originally dated 2021 on the lower left of the reverse. Due to printing delays, the notes were officially introduced in December 2025, at which time they were overprinted with a red stamp on the lower center reverse reading "2025-12-09" to commemorate the 100th anniversary of the tögrög currency itself, which was introduced into circulation on 9 December 1925. This dual commemorative purpose unites Mongolia's political independence and monetary heritage.

The front of the note features Genghis Khan (Chinggis Khaan) as the primary portrait, along with the Soyombo national symbol and mountainous landscape imagery representing Mongolia's terrain and natural heritage. A warrior on horseback serves as a registration device. The reverse displays portraits of the First Seven leaders of the People's Revolution: Damdin Sukhbaatar, Dogsom Bodoo, Soli Danzan, Dansranbileg Dogsom, Darzav Losol, Damby Chagdarjav, and Khorloo Choibalsan. A map of Mongolia and silhouette of a warrior on horseback appear as additional design elements, rendered in orange, brown, red, and blue colors.

This commemorative issue marks a significant moment in Mongolia's financial history, bridging the centennial of its 1921 revolution with the centennial of its monetary system's establishment, making it a dual-purpose historical document.

highlights.reason: Honors the 100th anniversary of the 1921 People's Revolution and 100th anniversary of the tögrög currency (1925), representing both political independence and monetary heritage.
highlights.design: Front: Genghis Khan portrait with Soyombo symbol and mountainous landscape; reverse: seven revolutionary leaders (Sukhbaatar, Bodoo, Danzan, and others) with Mongolia map and warrior on horseback.
highlights.security: Features multiple registration devices including warrior-on-horseback motifs, Soyombo symbol, and map elements as security design components; red overprint stamp dates (2021 and 2025-12-09) indicate authenticity.

highlights.design: Polymer banknote featuring Bank of Papua New Guinea design standards across denominations
highlights.security: Polymer substrate provides inherent security advantages with enhanced durability

The predominantly red note displays her portrait on the obverse alongside a jaguar and Puya Raimondi plant on the reverse. Rostworowski was a renowned expert on Andean civilization and founder of the Institute of Peruvian Studies.

highlights.design: Obverse features Rostworowski; reverse displays jaguar and Puya Raimondi alpine plant symbolizing Andean heritage

The brick-red and multicolor note features King Salman's portrait and the Green Dome of Medina on the obverse; the reverse depicts the Prophet's Mosque in Medina and the national emblem with crossed swords and palm tree.

highlights.design: Obverse displays King Salman and Green Dome of Prophet's Mosque; reverse features full view of Prophet's Mosque and national emblem
highlights.security: Part of sixth issue currency series with standard Saudi Central Bank security specifications

title: 200 Riyals
description: King Abdulaziz Ibn Saud. 5th anniversary of launching Vision 2030 (2016-2021).
body:
The Saudi Central Bank (SAMA) issued the 200 Riyals commemorative banknote on 25 April 2021 to mark the fifth anniversary of the Kingdom's Vision 2030 initiative, launched in 2016. The front features a portrait of King Abdulaziz ibn Abdulrahman Al Saud, the founder of the modern Saudi state, alongside the distinctive 3D logo of Saudi Vision 2030 in a prominent position. The banknote is printed predominantly in gray tones with accents in other colors, presenting a modern aesthetic while maintaining the traditional elements of Saudi currency design.

The reverse side displays additional security and design elements, including a watermark and foil strip bearing the emblem of Saudi Arabia and the banknote value. The 200 Riyals denomination represents a major note within the sixth issue of Saudi Arabian banknotes, and its design incorporates the latest standards in banknote printing technology. The note carries the name and logo of the Saudi Central Bank (SAMA), the denomination in both Arabic letters and numbers, and all design elements are executed to the highest security standards.

This commemorative issue reflects Saudi Arabia's commitment to modernization and long-term development goals through Vision 2030, while celebrating the foundational leadership of King Abdulaziz and the nation's historical continuity.

highlights.reason: Commemorates the fifth anniversary of Saudi Arabia's Vision 2030 initiative (2016-2021), representing the nation's modernization and development program.
highlights.design: Front: King Abdulaziz ibn Abdulrahman Al Saud portrait with prominent 3D Vision 2030 logo in gray with color accents; reverse: Saudi emblem watermark and foil strip with banknote value.
highlights.security: Includes watermark and foil strip bearing Saudi emblem, latest banknote printing standards with multiple security layers, demetalalized elements, and denomination markings in Arabic and numbers.

title: 100 Syrian Pounds
description: Basalt stone gate and amphitheatre of Bosra.
body: The 100 Syrian Pounds note, first issued July 27, 2010 (dated 2009), features Bab el-Hawa, the Western Gate of Bosra built from black basalt stone, along with the historic Bosra Amphitheatre on the obverse. The reverse depicts the dome of the Treasury of Damascus within the Umayyad Mosque and the Central Bank of Syria headquarters. Predominantly vermilion-colored, printed on cotton paper measuring 140 x 65 mm with Arabian horse watermark. This denomination commemorates Syria's ancient architectural and cultural heritage.

issuer: Bonki Millii Tochikiston / National Bank of Tajikistan
title: 100 Somoni
description: Ismoili Somoni.
body:
The National Bank of Tajikistan issued this regular circulation 100 Somoni banknote in 2021 as part of its standard currency series, continuing to circulate through 2025. This note differs from the commemorative 2025 issue (e44) and represents the standard design used for general economic circulation.

The obverse depicts Abu Ibrohim Ismoili ibni Ahmad Somonī, the founder of the Samanid state and historical emir of Transoxiana and Khorasan during the 9th century. The design includes the iconic Samanid Mausoleum and the tomb of Ismoili Somoni located in Bukhara, Uzbekistan.

The reverse features the Navruz Palace (Kohi Navruz), a significant architectural landmark in Dushanbe, alongside the national flag of Tajikistan. This represented a design change from earlier issues that featured the Presidential Palace.

Manufactured by Giesecke & Devrient, this 156 × 65 mm paper banknote is signed by national officials and incorporates a holographic stripe featuring Ismoil Somoni, the denomination, and the national coat of arms alongside a color-changing windowed security thread with demetalized elements.

highlights.reason: Regular circulation banknote distinguishable from the 2025 commemorative issue by its standard design and 2021 date.
highlights.design: Features Ismoili Somoni and his mausoleum on the obverse; Navruz Palace (replacing Presidential Palace from earlier versions) on the reverse.
highlights.security: Holographic stripe with Ismoil Somoni, denomination, and coat of arms; color-changing windowed security thread with demetalized elements.

title: 50 Pa'anga

body: The 50 Pa'anga banknote from the 2020-2021 series represents a signature update by the National Reserve Bank of Tonga. The note features His Majesty King Tupou VI on the obverse with traditional Tongan ornamental motifs and the national coat of arms. This polymer or paper issue reflects Tonga's continued modernization of its currency system, with updated signatures representing the central bank's current leadership during the early 2020s period.

highlights.design: Portrait of King Tupou VI with traditional Tongan cultural ornamental motifs.
highlights.security: Updated banknote series incorporating modern security enhancements from 2020 onwards.

The reverse depicts the Gate Tower of Lutsk Castle. The banknote underwent signature updates, with the 2021 commemorative version signed by NBU Governor Kyrylo Shevchenko. Measuring 75 x 148 mm, this note combines cultural heritage with modern security features and represents Ukraine's currency modernization program.

highlights.design: Lesia Ukrainka portrait paired with Lutsk Castle Gate Tower on reverse.
highlights.security: Updated signature line and contemporary banknote security features.

The note features Ivan Franko, the renowned Ukrainian writer, poet, and national symbol, on the obverse. The reverse depicts the Lviv Opera House. Measuring 130 x 69 mm in green, blue, and pink colors, this issue honors Franko's contributions to Ukrainian national identity and culture.

body: The 500 Hriven commemorative banknote, issued August 20, 2021 by the National Bank of Ukraine, commemorates Ukraine's 30th independence anniversary. It features Grigoriy Skovoroda (1722-1794), the Ukrainian philosopher, poet, and enlightener known as 'The Ukrainian Socrates,' on the obverse. The reverse depicts the Kievo-Mogilyansk Academy. Measuring 75 x 154 mm and signed by NBU Governor Kyrylo Shevchenko, this note honors Skovoroda's spiritual and intellectual legacy.

The 2021 commemorative version, signed by NBU Governor Kyrylo Shevchenko and issued December 22, 2021, celebrates Ukraine's 30th independence anniversary.
